question.en:
A company hosts a website on multiple Amazon EC2 instances that run in an Auto Scaling group. Users are reporting slow responses during peak times between
6 PM and 11 PM every weekend. A SysOps administrator must implement a solution to improve performance during these peak times.
What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は、Auto Scaling グループで実行される複数の Amazon EC2 インスタンス上でウェブサイトをホストしています。ユーザーから、毎週末の午後6時から午後11時までのピーク時間帯にレスポンスが遅いという報告が上がっています。SysOps administrator は、このピーク時間帯のパフォーマンスを改善するソリューションを実装する必要があります。
これらの要件を満たす、運用上最も効率的なソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create a scheduled Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule to invoke an AWS Lambda function to increase the desired capacity before peak times.
text.ja: ピーク時間の前に希望容量を増やす AWS Lambda 関数を呼び出すよう、スケジュールされた Amazon EventBridge（Amazon CloudWatch Events）ルールを作成する。

text.en: Configure a scheduled scaling action with a recurrence option to change the desired capacity before and after peak times.
text.ja: ピーク時間の前後で希望容量を変更するよう、繰り返しオプション付きのスケジュールされたスケーリングアクションを設定する。

text.en: Create a target tracking scaling policy to add more instances when memory utilization is above 70%.
text.ja: メモリ使用率が70%を超えたときにインスタンスを追加する、ターゲット追跡スケーリングポリシーを作成する。

text.en: Configure the cooldown period for the Auto Scaling group to modify desired capacity before and after peak times.
text.ja: ピーク時間の前後で希望容量を変更するよう、Auto Scaling グループのクールダウン期間を設定する。

comment.en: Answer is B - "Scheduled scaling helps you to set up your own scaling schedule according to predictable load changes. For example, let's say that every week the traffic to your web application starts to increase on Wednesday, remains high on Thursday, and starts to decrease on Friday. You can configure a schedule for Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling to increase capacity on Wednesday and decrease capacity on Friday." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/schedule_time.html
Target tracking is only for unpredictable workloads.Answer is B.
Vote B
it have specific time. We absolutly use schedule scaling policy to adjust capacity before and after peak time
B. is the correct answer. Schedule time is the tool option that should be used in this situation.
Answer is B
Scheduling with the peak time, and defining ASG
B. Configure a scheduled scaling action with a recurrence option to change the desired capacity before and after peak times.
Answer is B
Answer is B
B. Configure a scheduled scaling action with a recurrence option to change the desired capacity before and after peak times.
Thanks

question.en:
A SysOps administrator is deploying an application on 10 Amazon EC2 instances. The application must be highly available. The instances must be placed on distinct underlying hardware.
What should the SysOps administrator do to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある SysOps administrator は、10台の Amazon EC2 インスタンスにアプリケーションをデプロイしています。このアプリケーションは高可用性が必要です。インスタンスは異なる物理ハードウェア上に配置する必要があります。
これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps administrator は何を行うべきですか。

text.en: Launch the instances into a cluster placement group in a single AWS Region.
text.ja: 単一の AWS リージョン内のクラスタープレイスメントグループにインスタンスを起動する。

text.en: Launch the instances into a partition placement group in multiple AWS Regions.
text.ja: 複数の AWS リージョンのパーティションプレイスメントグループにインスタンスを起動する。

text.en: Launch the instances into a spread placement group in multiple AWS Regions.
text.ja: 複数の AWS リージョンのスプレッドプレイスメントグループにインスタンスを起動する。

text.en: Launch the instances into a spread placement group in a single AWS Region.
text.ja: 単一の AWS リージョン内のスプレッドプレイスメントグループにインスタンスを起動する。

comment.en: D - I think. Keep in mind placement groups can't cross regions.

answer D: Spread works only in 1 region. Max instace per avalibility zone 7. if 3 availibility zone = 21 Istance. ( Question=10 istance) "A spread placement group is a group of instances that are each placed on distinct racks, with each rack having its own network and power source." All istance are placed on different racks for a maximun of 7 istance for Availability Zone. "A spread placement group can span multiple Availability Zones in the same Region. You can have a maximum of seven running instances per Availability Zone per group." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/placement-groups.html#placement-groups-spread

Placement groups are within AZs, hence one AZ correlates to only one Region https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/placement-groups.html Spread placement for unique hardware across the same AZ for ultimate redundancy Cluster placement uses hardware within the same AZ for faster networking Partition placement deploys equal sets of hardware across unique racks for redundancy

D. Launch the instances into a spread placement group in a single AWS Region. A spread placement group is a logical grouping of instances that are placed on distinct underlying hardware to reduce the risk of simultaneous failures due to hardware issues. This ensures high availability. In this scenario, the requirement is to have the instances on distinct underlying hardware, and since the application needs to be highly available, you should launch them in a single AWS Region to keep them close for low latency. Options A and B are incorrect because they involve multiple AWS Regions, which is not necessary for the given requirements. Option C is incorrect because it suggests using a spread placement group in multiple AWS Regions, which is not supported.
D definitely, because we need ec2s in separated racks and because placement groups do not cross regions
support multi-AZ, not multi-Region
D is the answer

What is the difference between spread placement group and partition group? The spread placement group setup may be similar to partition placement groups, but the main difference is that partition placement groups are made of several instances on each partition, while spread groups are just single individual instances spread through different racks or AZs.
We know its spread placement because this is associated with high availability. But its D because they cannot travel across regions
B incorrect - you cant launch them in multiple AWS regions.. Only D
Answer is D
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/placement-groups.html A spread placement group is a group of instances that are each placed on distinct hardware.

question.en:
A company is running distributed computing software to manage a fleet of 20 Amazon EC2 instances for calculations. The fleet includes 2 control nodes and 18 task nodes to run the calculations. Control nodes can automatically start the task nodes.
Currently, all the nodes run on demand. The control nodes must be available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. The task nodes run for 4 hours each day. A SysOps administrator needs to optimize the cost of this solution.
Which combination of actions will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある企業は、計算処理を管理するために20台の Amazon EC2 インスタンスからなるフリートで分散コンピューティングソフトウェアを実行しています。フリートには2台のコントロールノードと、計算を実行する18台のタスクノードが含まれています。コントロールノードはタスクノードを自動的に起動できます。
現在、すべてのノードはオンデマンドで実行されています。コントロールノードは24時間365日利用可能である必要があります。タスクノードは毎日4時間実行されます。SysOps administrator は、このソリューションのコストを最適化する必要があります。
これらの要件を満たす組み合わせはどれですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Purchase EC2 Instance Savings Plans for the control nodes.
text.ja: コントロールノード用に EC2 Instance Savings Plans を購入する。

text.en: Use Dedicated Hosts for the control nodes.
text.ja: コントロールノードに Dedicated Hosts を使用する。

text.en: Use Reserved Instances for the task nodes.
text.ja: タスクノードに Reserved Instances を使用する。

text.en: Use Spot Instances for the control nodes. Use On-Demand Instances if there is no Spot availability.
text.ja: コントロールノードに Spot Instances を使用する。Spot の空きがない場合は On-Demand Instances を使用する。

text.en: Use Spot Instances for the task nodes. Use On-Demand Instances if there is no Spot availability.
text.ja: タスクノードに Spot Instances を使用する。Spot の空きがない場合は On-Demand Instances を使用する。

comment.en: A and E, but i would choose RI for control nodes as a better option.

Can't be "C" and "E" because they are contradictory. Both of these answers suggest two different solutions for "task nodes". People who are choosing "C" are thinking its directed to "control nodes", but it is not. Answer has to be A and E. I'd agree that control nodes should be reserved instances, (or "A") but that is not what "C" is proposing. It's a trick question.
C is not correct C. Use Reserved Instances for the TASK nodes. not CONTROL nodes

As we know from the question - The control nodes must be available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. CE appears to be tight choice. Reserved Instances can provide higher savings than Savings Plan for predictable workloads with consistent usage patterns. Savings Plan can provide higher savings than Reserved Instances for workloads with varying usage patterns or instance types. https://medium.com/@ekantmate/savings-plan-vs-reserved-instances-which-one-to-select-cdb8dba9facd
These are not 'good' options in my opinion. Reserved instances for control nodes and spot block for task nodes would be more ideal.
A and E A. Purchase EC2 Instance Savings Plans for the control nodes.
A and E
Key word is Optimize
Why would you purchase reserved for the task nodes that only need to run for 4 hours? I hope you are not on the AWS team in my company.
Answer is AE

A and E are perfect for this Savings Plans for Control Nodes (Option A): Cost-Effectiveness: Savings Plans provide substantial cost savings for workloads that need to run continuously. Since control nodes need to be available 24/7, Savings Plans are an effective way to reduce costs while maintaining consistent availability. Flexibility: Savings Plans are more flexible than Reserved Instances as they apply to any instance usage within a specific region. Spot Instances for Task Nodes (Option E): Cost Reduction: Spot Instances can offer up to 90% savings over On-Demand prices, making them an excellent choice for non-critical, short-term workloads like the task nodes. Fallback Mechanism: By falling back to On-Demand Instances when Spot Instances are unavailable, you can ensure that the task nodes still execute as required without significant cost implications.
how C&E, A&E, what about control nodes.

question.en:
A company is supposed to receive a data file every hour in an Amazon S3 bucket. An S3 event notification invokes an AWS Lambda function each time a file arrives. The function processes the data for use by an application.
The application team notices that sometimes the file does not arrive. The application team wants to receive a notification whenever the file does not arrive.
What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は、1時間ごとに Amazon S3 バケットにデータファイルを受信することになっています。ファイルが到着すると毎回 S3 イベント通知によって AWS Lambda 関数が呼び出されます。この関数は、アプリケーションが使用するためにデータを処理します。
アプリケーションチームは、ファイルが到着しないことがあると気付きました。アプリケーションチームは、ファイルが到着しなかった場合に通知を受け取りたいと考えています。
これらの要件を満たす、運用上最も効率的なソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Add an S3 Lifecycle rule on the S3 bucket with a scope that is limited to objects that were created in the last hour. Configure another S3 event notification to be invoked by the lifecycle transition when the number of objects transitioned is zero. Publish a message to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to notify the application team.
text.ja: S3 バケットに、過去1時間以内に作成されたオブジェクトに限定したスコープの S3 ライフサイクルルールを追加する。遷移したオブジェクトの数がゼロの場合にライフサイクル遷移によって呼び出される別の S3 イベント通知を設定する。Amazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）トピックにメッセージを発行してアプリケーションチームに通知する。

text.en: Configure another S3 event notification to invoke a Lambda function that posts a message to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to publish a message to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to notify the application team when the ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage metric of the queue is greater than 1 hour.
text.ja: Amazon Simple Queue Service（Amazon SQS）キューにメッセージを投稿する Lambda 関数を呼び出す別の S3 イベント通知を設定する。キューの ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage メトリクスが1時間を超えたときに Amazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）トピックにメッセージを発行してアプリケーションチームに通知する Amazon CloudWatch アラームを作成する。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to publish a message to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to alert the application team when the Invocations metric of the Lambda function is zero for an hour. Configure the alarm to treat missing data as breaching.
text.ja: Lambda 関数の Invocations メトリクスが1時間ゼロの場合にアプリケーションチームへ警告するため、Amazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）トピックにメッセージを発行する Amazon CloudWatch アラームを作成する。データ欠落を違反として扱うようにアラームを設定する。

text.en: Create a new Lambda function to get the timestamp of the newest file in the S3 bucket. If the timestamp is more than 1 hour ago, publish a message to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to notify the application team. Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule to invoke the new function hourly.
text.ja: S3 バケット内の最新ファイルのタイムスタンプを取得する新しい Lambda 関数を作成する。タイムスタンプが1時間以上前であれば、Amazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）トピックにメッセージを発行してアプリケーションチームに通知する。この新しい関数を1時間ごとに呼び出す Amazon EventBridge（Amazon CloudWatch Events）ルールを作成する。

comment.en: C sounds like the best option. Tested it as well.
You chose the D answer, we're talking about "most operationally" solution and the answer D uses 3 services instead of 2 like the answer C. In my opinion, the good answer is the C one.
Answer is C
No point sending to other Lambda functions when it cant even trigger the actual Lambda function! C is the most sane answer.
Answer is C

C. Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to publish a message to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to alert the application team when the Invocations metric of the Lambda function is zero for an hour. Configure the alarm to treat missing data as breaching.

This solution is the most operationally efficient as it uses a simple and straightforward approach to check if the data file is not arriving on time. The new Lambda function can be created with minimal configuration, and the Amazon CloudWatch Events rule can be set up to trigger the function hourly. If the timestamp of the newest file in the S3 bucket is more than an hour ago, the function will publish a message to an Amazon SNS topic to notify the application team. This approach avoids complex configurations and dependencies on other AWS services, making it easier to manage and maintain over time.
C simple and clean as f*
ccccccc
Ans: C
I vote C

question.en:
A SysOps administrator configured AWS Backup to capture snapshots from a single Amazon EC2 instance that has one Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon
EBS) volume attached. On the first snapshot, the EBS volume has 10 GiB of data. On the second snapshot, the EBS volume still contains 10 GiB of data, but 4
GiB have changed. On the third snapshot, 2 GiB of data have been added to the volume, for a total of 12 GiB.
How much total storage is required to store these snapshots?
question.ja:
ある SysOps administrator は、Amazon Elastic Block Store（Amazon EBS）ボリュームが1つアタッチされた単一の Amazon EC2 インスタンスからスナップショットを取得するように AWS Backup を設定しました。1回目のスナップショット時点で、EBS ボリュームには10 GiB のデータがあります。2回目のスナップショット時点で、EBS ボリュームには依然として10 GiB のデータがありますが、そのうち4 GiB が変更されています。3回目のスナップショット時点で、ボリュームに2 GiB のデータが追加され、合計12 GiB になりました。
これらのスナップショットを保存するために必要な合計ストレージはどれですか。

text.en: 12 GiB
text.ja: 12 GiB

text.en: 16 GiB
text.ja: 16 GiB

text.en: 26 GiB
text.ja: 26 GiB

text.en: 32 GiB
text.ja: 32 GiB

comment.en: 100% B. This scenario is explained at https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSSnapshots.html#how_snapshots_work

1. First Snapshot: The entire volume is captured in the first snapshot, so it requires storage equivalent to the size of the volume, which is 10 GiB. 2. Second Snapshot: Only the changed blocks since the first snapshot are stored. In this case, 4 GiB have changed, so the storage required for the second snapshot is 4 GiB. 3. Third Snapshot: Only the changed blocks since the second snapshot are stored. In this case, 2 GiB have been added, so the storage required for the third snapshot is 2 GiB. The total storage required is the sum of the storage for each snapshot: 10 GiB + 4 GiB + 2 GiB = 16 GiB So, the total storage required to store these three snapshots is 16 GiB.

The first snapshot that you create from a volume is always a full snapshot. It includes all of the data blocks written to the volume at the time of creating the snapshot. Subsequent snapshots of the same volume are incremental snapshots. They include only changed and new data blocks written to the volume since the last snapshot was created.
B is correct, 10+4+2=16GiB
Answer is B: Original 10 + 4 (changed data) + 2 (new data) = 16

Amazon EBS snapshots are incremental, meaning only the data changed after the first snapshot is saved. The first snapshot captures 10 GiB, the second snapshot only saves the 4 GiB that changed, and the third snapshot only saves the newly added 2 GiB. Therefore, the total storage required is the initial 10 GiB plus the total of 6 GiB for added/changed data (4 GiB + 2 GiB), making it 12 GiB in total, not 16 GiB.
this is a math question, not aws
bbbbbbb
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSSnapshots.html#how_snapshots_work
Due to incremental snapshots?
AWS EBS snapshots are incremental: after the first full snapshot, each new snapshot stores only the data blocks that changed since the previous one, while unchanged blocks are merely referenced.
ANS: B

question.en:
A SysOps administrator is responsible for managing a company's cloud infrastructure with AWS CloudFormation. The SysOps administrator needs to create a single resource that consists of multiple AWS services. The resource must support creation and deletion through the CloudFormation console.
Which CloudFormation resource type should the SysOps administrator create to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある SysOps administrator は、AWS CloudFormation を使用して会社のクラウドインフラストラクチャを管理する責任を負っています。SysOps administrator は、複数の AWS サービスから構成される単一のリソースを作成する必要があります。このリソースは、CloudFormation コンソールを通じた作成と削除をサポートする必要があります。
これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps administrator はどの CloudFormation リソースタイプを作成すべきですか。

text.en: AWS::EC2::Instance with a cfn-init helper script
text.ja: cfn-init ヘルパースクリプトを使用した AWS::EC2::Instance

text.en: AWS::OpsWorks::Instance
text.ja: AWS::OpsWorks::Instance

text.en: AWS::SSM::Document
text.ja: AWS::SSM::Document

text.en: Custom::MyCustomType
text.ja: Custom::MyCustomType

comment.en: Custom resources enable you to write custom provisioning logic in templates that AWS CloudFormation runs anytime you create, update (if you changed the custom resource), or delete stacks. For example, you might want to include resources that aren't available as AWS CloudFormation resource types. You can include those resources by using custom resources. That way you can still manage all your related resources in a single stack. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/template-custom-resources.html

Option A (AWS::EC2::Instance with a cfn-init helper script) is used to create an EC2 instance, but it does not provide the ability to create multiple AWS services as a single resource. cfn-init is useful for bootstrapping EC2 instances with additional configuration and packages, but it's not designed to create other resources. Option B (AWS::OpsWorks::Instance) is used to create an AWS OpsWorks instance, which is not applicable for the requirement of creating multiple AWS services as a single resource. Option C (AWS::SSM::Document) is used to create an SSM (Systems Manager) document, which is used for automating tasks on instances using SSM, but it does not provide the functionality of creating multiple AWS services as a single resource.

The Custom::MyCustomType is a CloudFormation custom resource type that allows you to define your own resource type using AWS Lambda-backed custom logic. This means you can use AWS Lambda functions to create and delete the custom resource. By creating a custom resource backed by an AWS Lambda function, you have the flexibility to define and orchestrate multiple AWS services as a single resource within your CloudFormation stack. The custom Lambda function can handle the logic for creating and deleting the resources across multiple services in the desired order and configuration.
ddddddd
"For example, you might want to include resources that aren't available as AWS CloudFormation resource types."
Option D is correct. CustomType helps us to write the meaningful and logical nested stack template which the multiple AWS services consisted in 1 resource.
d correct
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/template-custom-resources-lambda.html

question.en:
A new website will run on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer. Amazon Route 53 will be used to manage DNS records.
What type of record should be set in Route 53 to point the website's apex domain name (for example, `company.com`) to the Application Load Balancer?
question.ja:
新しいウェブサイトが、Application Load Balancer の後ろにある Amazon EC2 インスタンス上で実行されます。DNS レコードの管理には Amazon Route 53 が使用されます。
ウェブサイトのアペックスドメイン名（例: `company.com`）を Application Load Balancer に向けるために、Route 53 で設定すべきレコードの種類は何ですか。

text.en: CNAME
text.ja: CNAME

text.en: SOA
text.ja: SOA

text.en: TXT
text.ja: TXT

text.en: ALIAS
text.ja: ALIAS

comment.en: The correct answer is D. You should create an ALIAS record in Route 53 to point the website’s apex domain name (e.g., company.com) to the Application Load Balancer. An ALIAS record is a Route 53-specific extension to DNS that allows you to map the apex domain name to another AWS resource, such as an Application Load Balancer.
ALIA -> APEX https://docs.aws.amazon.com/govcloud-us/latest/UserGuide/setting-up-route53-zoneapex-elb.html

Option D is the correct answer. An ALIAS record is a Route 53 extension to DNS that provides a Route 53-specific alias for a resource record set. When Route 53 receives a DNS query for a domain name that is associated with an ALIAS record, Route 53 responds with the IP address of the resource that is specified in the record. An ALIAS record can be used to map an apex domain name to an Application Load Balancer. In this case, the ALIAS record points to the DNS name of the Application Load Balancer. A CNAME record is a type of DNS record that points one domain name to another. However, CNAME records cannot be used to map an apex domain name to an Application Load Balancer. An SOA (Start of Authority) record is a DNS record that specifies authoritative information about a DNS zone, such as the email address of the person responsible for managing the zone. A TXT record is a type of DNS record that is used to associate some arbitrary text with a DNS record. It is not used to map domain names to IP addresses or to other domain names.
Not CNAME, as CNAME can not be applied to root domain level.
D. SOA and TXT are definitely wrong. CNAME allows you to map names, but only for non zone apex addresses (ie m.example.com to mobile.example.com)
I am going with D
Ans: D
D, "Route 53 supports the alias resource record set, which lets you map your zone apex (e.g. example.com) DNS name to your load balancer DNS name."
The answer is D
thanks for explanation. "Alias : apex domain name to another AWS resource, such as an Application Load Balancer."
Route 53 helps to point out the alias of the apex zone mapping to the aws services like ALB, S3 & cloudfront.
