question.en:
A company runs an application that hosts critical data for several clients. The company uses AWS CloudTrail to track user activities on various AWS resources. To meet new security requirements, the company needs to protect the CloudTrail log files from being modified, deleted, or forged.
Which solution will meet these requirement?
question.ja:
ある企業は、複数の顧客の重要なデータをホストするアプリケーションを運用しています。この企業はAWS CloudTrailを使用して、さまざまなAWSリソースに対するユーザーアクティビティを追跡しています。新しいセキュリティ要件を満たすため、この企業はCloudTrailログファイルが変更、削除、または改ざんされることから保護する必要があります。
この要件を満たす解決策はどれですか。

text.en: Enable CloudTrail log file integrity validation.
text.ja: CloudTrailログファイルの整合性検証を有効にする。

text.en: Use Amazon S3 MFA Delete on the S3 bucket where the CloudTrail log files are stored.
text.ja: CloudTrailログファイルが保存されているS3バケットでAmazon S3 MFA Deleteを使用する。

text.en: Use Amazon S3 Versioning to keep all versions of the CloudTrail log files.
text.ja: Amazon S3 Versioningを使用して、CloudTrailログファイルのすべてのバージョンを保持する。

text.en: Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) security keys to secure the CloudTrail log files.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）のセキュリティキーを使用して、CloudTrailログファイルを保護する。

comment.en: "Enable MFA delete on the Amazon S3 bucket where you store log files When you configure multi-factor authentication (MFA), attempts to change the versioning state of bucket, or delete an object version in a bucket, require additional authentication. This way, even if a user acquires the password of an IAM user with permissions to permanently delete Amazon S3 objects, you can still prevent operations that could compromise your log files." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/best-practices-security.html
There's no question at all it's A.

I think B is correct. CloudTrail Log integrity does not prevent the file from being deleted. It will only detect if the file was deleted, to prevent deletion you should use MFA delete, which requires versioning to be enabled. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-log-file-validation-intro.html

**A. Enable CloudTrail log file integrity validation**. Enabling CloudTrail log file integrity validation ensures that the log files are protected using industry-standard algorithms (SHA-256 for hashing and SHA-256 with RSA for digital signing). This makes it computationally infeasible to modify, delete, or forge the log files without detection[1](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-log-file-validation-intro.html)[2](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/best-practices-security.html).
B is the correct answer. The key word here is to prevent the logs from being deleted. Answer would be incorrect as it does not mention about tracking who deleted the file.

real answer is WORM, aka object locks: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/storage/protecting-data-with-amazon-s3-object-lock/ - but its not in the answers so we need to pick from what we have A is incorrect - https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-log-file-validation-intro.html - integrity feature doe not prevent anyone from deleting the file D is nonsence so its either B or C since MFA delete inherently requires versioning, I'd go for B
ChatGTP suggested A in the first place. I replied with a piece of documentation about log file validation it switched answer to B
I'd go with B here as it specifically asks to protect the CT files from being modified, deleted or forged.
no single option among the provided choices completely fulfills all three requirements
A is correct
If you have answered anything but A, remember that CloudTrail log file integrity validation is EXACTLY made for this purpose and nothing else.

To determine whether a log file was modified, deleted, or unchanged after CloudTrail delivered it, you can use CloudTrail log file integrity validation. This feature is built using industry standard algorithms: SHA-256 for hashing and SHA-256 with RSA for digital signing. This makes it computationally infeasible to modify, delete or forge CloudTrail log files without detection. You can use the AWS CLI to validate the files in the location where CloudTrail delivered them. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudtrail-log-file-validation-intro.html#cloudtrail-log-file-validation-intro-use-cases

question.en:
A global company operates out of five AWS Regions. A SysOps administrator wants to identify all the company's tagged and untagged Amazon EC2 instances.
The company requires the output to display the instance ID and tags.
What is the MOST operationally efficient way for the SysOps administrator to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
グローバル企業が5つのAWSリージョンで事業を展開している。SysOps管理者は、会社のタグ付けされたAmazon EC2インスタンスとタグ付けされていないAmazon EC2インスタンスをすべて特定したいと考えている。
会社は、インスタンスIDとタグを表示する出力を要求している。
これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者が取るべき最も運用効率の良い方法は何か。

text.en: Create a tag-based resource group in AWS Resource Groups.
text.ja: AWS Resource Groupsでタグベースのリソースグループを作成する。

text.en: Use AWS Trusted Advisor. Export the EC2 On-Demand Instances check results from Trusted Advisor.
text.ja: AWS Trusted Advisorを使用する。Trusted AdvisorからEC2 On-Demand Instancesのチェック結果をエクスポートする。

text.en: Use Cost Explorer. Choose a service type of EC2-Instances, and group by Resource.
text.ja: Cost Explorerを使用する。サービスタイプにEC2-Instancesを選択し、リソース別にグループ化する。

text.en: Use Tag Editor in AWS Resource Groups. Select all Regions, and choose a resource type of AWS::EC2::Instance.
text.ja: AWS Resource GroupsのTag Editorを使用する。すべてのリージョンを選択し、リソースタイプにAWS::EC2::Instanceを選択する。

comment.en: With Tag Editor, you build a query to find resources in one or more AWS Regions that are available for tagging. You can choose up to 20 individual resource types, or build a query on All resource types. Your query can include resources that already have tags, or resources that have no tags. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ARG/latest/userguide/find-resources-to-tag.html
ddddddd
You can't create a tag-based group if the instances may not be tagged -_- Ans: D

question.en:
A company uploaded its website files to an Amazon S3 bucket that has S3 Versioning enabled. The company uses an Amazon CloudFront distribution with the S3 bucket as the origin. The company recently modified the files, but the object names remained the same. Users report that old content is still appearing on the website.
How should a SysOps administrator remediate this issue?
question.ja:
会社はS3バージョニングが有効なAmazon S3バケットにウェブサイトファイルをアップロードした。会社はそのS3バケットをオリジンとするAmazon CloudFrontディストリビューションを使用している。会社は最近ファイルを変更したが、オブジェクト名は変わっていない。ユーザーからは、古いコンテンツが依然としてウェブサイトに表示されているという報告がある。
SysOps管理者はこの問題をどのように解決すべきか。

text.en: Create a CloudFront invalidation, and add the path of the updated files.
text.ja: CloudFrontの無効化(invalidation)を作成し、更新されたファイルのパスを追加する。

text.en: Create a CloudFront signed URL to update each object immediately.
text.ja: 各オブジェクトを即時に更新するために、CloudFrontの署名付きURLを作成する。

text.en: Configure an S3 origin access identity (OAI) to display only the updated files to users.
text.ja: 更新されたファイルのみをユーザーに表示するように、S3オリジンアクセスアイデンティティ(OAI)を設定する。

text.en: Disable S3 Versioning on the S3 bucket so that the updated files can replace the old files.
text.ja: 更新されたファイルが古いファイルを置き換えられるように、S3バケットのS3バージョニングを無効にする。

comment.en: Answer is A! .Short description By default, CloudFront caches a response from Amazon S3 for 24 hours (Default TTL of 86,400 seconds). If your request lands at an edge location that served the Amazon S3 response within 24 hours, then CloudFront uses the cached response. This happens even if you updated the content in Amazon S3. Use one of the following ways to push the updated Amazon S3 content from CloudFront: Invalidate the Amazon S3 objects. Use object versioning https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/cloudfront-serving-outdated-content-s3/
aaaaaa
Ans: A
A is the answer.

question.en:
A company runs a multi-tier web application with two Amazon EC2 instances in one Availability Zone in the us-east-1 Region. A SysOps administrator must migrate one of the EC2 instances to a new Availability Zone.
Which solution will accomplish this?
question.ja:
ある会社は、us-east-1リージョンの1つのアベイラビリティーゾーンで2台のAmazon EC2インスタンスを使用してマルチティアのウェブアプリケーションを運用している。SysOps管理者は、そのうち1台のEC2インスタンスを新しいアベイラビリティーゾーンに移行しなければならない。
この要件を実現するソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Copy the EC2 instance to a different Availability Zone. Terminate the original instance.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスを別のアベイラビリティーゾーンにコピーする。元のインスタンスを終了する。

text.en: Create an Amazon Machine Image (AMI) from the EC2 instance and launch it in a different Availability Zone. Terminate the original instance.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスからAmazon Machine Image(AMI)を作成し、別のアベイラビリティーゾーンで起動する。元のインスタンスを終了する。

text.en: Move the EC2 instance to a different Availability Zone using the AWS CLI.
text.ja: AWS CLIを使用して、EC2インスタンスを別のアベイラビリティーゾーンに移動する。

text.en: Stop the EC2 instance, modify the Availability Zone, and start the instance.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスを停止し、アベイラビリティーゾーンを変更してから、インスタンスを起動する。

comment.en: Answer is B! It's not possible to move an existing instance to another subnet, Availability Zone, or VPC. Instead, you can manually migrate the instance by creating a new Amazon Machine Image (AMI) from the source instance. Then, launch a new instance using the new AMI in the desired subnet, Availability Zone, or VPC. Finally, you can reassign any Elastic IP addresses from the source instance to the new instance. https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/move-ec2-instance/
B due to you cannot move an EC2's AV or VPC
Ans: B
bbbbbbb

question.en:
A company is expanding its fleet of Amazon EC2 instances before an expected increase of traffic. When a SysOps administrator attempts to add more instances, an InstanceLimitExceeded error is returned.
What should the SysOps administrator do to resolve this error?
question.ja:
ある会社は、トラフィックの増加が見込まれる前にAmazon EC2インスタンスのフリートを拡張している。SysOps管理者がインスタンスを追加しようとすると、InstanceLimitExceededエラーが返される。
このエラーを解決するために、SysOps管理者は何をすべきか。

text.en: Add an additional CIDR block to the VPC.
text.ja: VPCに追加のCIDRブロックを追加する。

text.en: Launch the EC2 instances in a different Availability Zone.
text.ja: 別のアベイラビリティーゾーンでEC2インスタンスを起動する。

text.en: Launch new EC2 instances in another VPC.
text.ja: 別のVPCで新しいEC2インスタンスを起動する。

text.en: Use Service Quotas to request an EC2 quota increase.
text.ja: Service Quotasを使用してEC2のクォータ増加をリクエストする。

comment.en: - Instance Limit Exceeded -> Over 64 vCPU - Insufficient Instance Capacity -> AWS's problem - Instance Terminates Immediately -> EBS volume limit, corrupt or no permission for decryption

Description You get the InstanceLimitExceeded error when you try to launch a new instance or restart a stopped instance. Cause If you get an InstanceLimitExceeded error when you try to launch a new instance or restart a stopped instance, you have reached the limit on the number of instances that you can launch in a Region. When you create your AWS account, we set default limits on the number of instances you can run on a per-Region basis. Solution You can request an instance limit increase on a per-region basis. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/troubleshooting-launch.html#troubleshooting-launch-limit

Instance Limit Exceeded -> You have reached max vCPU limit in a Region C is wrong because a different VPC can still be in the same Region,and solving this problem require you to launch EC2 in another Region
Use Service Quotas to request an EC2 quota increase.

If you get an InstanceLimitExceeded error when you try to launch a new instance or restart a stopped instance, you have reached the limit on the number of instances that you can launch in a Region. When you create your AWS account, we set default limits on the number of instances you can run on a per-Region basis. Solution You can request an instance limit increase on a per-region basis. For more information, see Amazon EC2 service quotas. THe option B gave AZ which is wrong it should be region
ddddddd
Ans: D

question.en:
A company's application is hosted by an internet provider at app.example.com. The company wants to access the application by using www.company.com, which the company owns and manages with Amazon Route 53.
Which Route 53 record should be created to address this?
question.ja:
ある会社のアプリケーションは、インターネットプロバイダーによってapp.example.comでホストされている。会社は、自社が所有し、Amazon Route 53で管理しているwww.company.comを使用してこのアプリケーションにアクセスしたいと考えている。
この要件に対応するために作成すべきRoute 53レコードはどれか。

text.en: A record
text.ja: Aレコード

text.en: Alias record
text.ja: Aliasレコード

text.en: CNAME record
text.ja: CNAMEレコード

text.en: Pointer (PTR) record
text.ja: ポインタ(PTR)レコード

comment.en: A record: Domain → IP address Alias record: Domain → AWS resource CNAME record: Domain → Another domain PTR record: IP address → Domain (reverse lookup)

C is correct. "A CNAME record can redirect DNS queries to any DNS record. For example, you can create a CNAME record that redirects queries from acme.example.com to zenith.example.com or to acme.example.org. " https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/resource-record-sets-choosing-alias-non-alias.html
URL to URL is CNAME. probably the only question in a whole sys ops where answer is CNAME
This website is hosted outside of AWS. It cannot be an alias.
Alias is for Domain to AWS Resource, not for Domain to Domain I believe.
why not B : - CNAME : hostname redirect to DNS - Alias : hostname redirect to ARN
C is the right way to go. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/resource-record-sets-choosing-alias-non-alias.html
I think the correct answer is c. You should never use a CNAME record for your root domain name (e.g. example.com). https://support.dnsimple.com/articles/differences-between-a-cname-alias-url/

If company.com is hosted in Route53 then Alias record can also be used. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/resource-record-sets-choosing-alias-non-alias.html#resource-record-sets-choosing-alias-non-alias-comparison Alias records An alias record can only redirect queries to selected AWS resources, such as the following: Amazon S3 buckets CloudFront distributions Another record in the same Route 53 hosted zone For example, you can create an alias record named acme.example.com that redirects queries to an Amazon S3 bucket that is also named acme.example.com. You can also create an acme.example.com alias record that redirects queries to a record named zenith.example.com in the example.com hosted zone.
B: Alias! It does the same as CNAME, it's cheaper and always preferred by AWS ;)
It does not do the same as CNAME. Alias cannot point a hostname to another hostname.
It is CNAME for www , not just on AWS Route 53, for all DNS domain name servers in the world. See screenshot https://gist.github.com/assets/1328316/b8cc838c-3821-457e-aefc-b3e0f70c8937 .

question.en:
A SysOps administrator wants to upload a file that is 1 TB in size from on-premises to an Amazon S3 bucket using multipart uploads.
What should the SysOps administrator do to meet this requirement?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者が、オンプレミスからAmazon S3バケットへマルチパートアップロードを使用して1TBのファイルをアップロードしたいと考えている。
この要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者は何をすべきか。

text.en: Upload the file using the S3 console.
text.ja: S3コンソールを使用してファイルをアップロードする。

text.en: Use the s3api copy-object command.
text.ja: s3api copy-objectコマンドを使用する。

text.en: Use the s3api put-object command.
text.ja: s3api put-objectコマンドを使用する。

text.en: Use the s3 cp command
text.ja: s3 cpコマンドを使用する。

comment.en: I vote for D! It's a best practice to use aws s3 commands (such as aws s3 cp) for multipart uploads and downloads, because these aws s3 commands automatically perform multipart uploading and downloading based on the file size. By comparison, aws s3api commands, such as aws s3api create-multipart-upload, should be used only when aws s3 commands don't support a specific upload need, such as when the multipart upload involves multiple servers, a multipart upload is manually stopped and resumed later, or when the aws s3 command doesn't support a required request parameter. https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/s3-multipart-upload-cli/
The correct answer is D: Use the s3 cp command. s3 cp file.txt s3://my-bucket/ --multipart-upload
Ill go with D (aws s3 cp) on that one. It's recommended at https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/s3-multipart-upload-cli/ and there is more to be done for s3api to work.
D is the answer
great explanation !!
Important: It's a best practice to use aws s3 commands, such as aws s3 cp,

A Sysops administrator wants to securely share an object from a private Amazon S3 bucket with a group of users who do not have an AWS account. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that will meet this requirement? A. Attach an s3 bucket policy that only allows object downloads from the users' IP addresses. B. Create an IAM role that has access to the object. Instruct the users to assume the role C. Create an IAM user that has access to the object Share the credentials with the users D. Generate a presigned URL for tho object. Share tho URL with the users.
It is infact 'D' the correct ans. for this question
dddddddd
Ans: D

The Correct answer is D Read Article https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/s3-multipart-upload-cli/ To use a high-level aws s3 command for your multipart upload, run this command: $ aws s3 cp large_test_file s3://DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET/ Important: Use this aws s3api procedure only when aws s3 commands don't support a specific upload need
It's a best practice to use aws s3 commands (such as aws s3 cp) for multipart uploads and downloads. here , there is a multipart uploads

question.en:
A company hosts a static website on Amazon S3. An Amazon CloudFront distribution presents this site to global users. The company uses the Managed-
CachingDisabled CloudFront cache policy. The company's developers confirm that they frequently update a file in Amazon S3 with new information.
Users report that the website presents correct information when the website first loads the file. However, the users' browsers do not retrieve the updated file after a refresh.
What should a SysOps administrator recommend to fix this issue?
question.ja:
ある会社はAmazon S3で静的ウェブサイトをホストしている。Amazon CloudFrontディストリビューションがこのサイトをグローバルなユーザーに提供している。会社はManaged-CachingDisabledのCloudFrontキャッシュポリシーを使用している。会社の開発者は、Amazon S3内のファイルを新しい情報で頻繁に更新していることを確認している。
ユーザーからは、ウェブサイトを最初に読み込んだときは正しい情報が表示されるが、更新後にブラウザをリフレッシュしても更新されたファイルが取得されないという報告がある。
この問題を解決するために、SysOps管理者は何を推奨すべきか。

text.en: Add a Cache-Control header field with max-age=0 to the S3 object.
text.ja: S3オブジェクトにmax-age=0のCache-Controlヘッダーフィールドを追加する。

text.en: Change the CloudFront cache policy to Managed-CachingOptimized.
text.ja: CloudFrontキャッシュポリシーをManaged-CachingOptimizedに変更する。

text.en: Disable bucket versioning in the S3 bucket configuration.
text.ja: S3バケット設定でバケットバージョニングを無効にする。

text.en: Enable content compression in the CloudFront configuration.
text.ja: CloudFront設定でコンテンツ圧縮を有効にする。

comment.en: A! You can control how long your files stay in a CloudFront cache before CloudFront forwards another request to your origin. Reducing the duration allows you to serve dynamic content. Increasing the duration means that your users get better performance because your files are more likely to be served directly from the edge cache. A longer duration also reduces the load on your origin. To change the cache duration for an individual file, you can configure your origin to add a Cache-Control header with the max-age or s-maxage directive, or an Expires header to the file. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Expiration.html

link in the comments section states: The Cache-Control max-age directive lets you specify how long (in seconds) that you want an object to remain in the cache before CloudFront gets the object again from the origin server. The minimum expiration time CloudFront supports is 0 seconds. The maximum value is 100 years. And the company continue using the Managed- CachingDisabled CloudFront cache policy.

By adding a Cache-Control header with max-age=0 to the S3 object, you instruct CloudFront to revalidate the content on every request. When a user refreshes the page, CloudFront checks with S3 to ensure it has the latest version of the file. This approach ensures that users receive the most up-to-date content.

I think this question is wrong. By having the cache policy set to Managed-CachingDisabled all files will be fetched every time. This is also true of A if we have Managed-CachingOptimized. The answer is probably A but it shouldn't make a difference in this case.
You should disable Cache in Browser with Cache-Control: max-age=0.
A is the answer.
aaaaaaaa
Would it not be B since Caching is already Disabled?

question.en:
A company has a policy that requires all Amazon EC2 instances to have a specific set of tags. If an EC2 instance does not have the required tags, the noncompliant instance should be terminated.
What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirement?
question.ja:
ある会社には、すべてのAmazon EC2インスタンスに特定のタグセットを付与することを求めるポリシーがある。EC2インスタンスに必要なタグが付いていない場合、非準拠のインスタンスは終了されるべきである。
これらの要件を満たす最も運用効率の良いソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule to send all EC2 instance state changes to an AWS Lambda function to determine if each instance is compliant. Terminate any noncompliant instances.
text.ja: すべてのEC2インスタンスの状態変化をAWS Lambda関数に送信するAmazon EventBridge(Amazon CloudWatch Events)ルールを作成し、各インスタンスが準拠しているかを判定する。非準拠のインスタンスを終了する。

text.en: Create an IAM policy that enforces all EC2 instance tag requirements. If the required tags are not in place for an instance, the policy will terminate noncompliant instance.
text.ja: すべてのEC2インスタンスのタグ要件を強制するIAMポリシーを作成する。インスタンスに必要なタグがない場合、そのポリシーが非準拠のインスタンスを終了する。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function to determine if each EC2 instance is compliant and terminate an instance if it is noncompliant. Schedule the Lambda function to invoke every 5 minutes.
text.ja: 各EC2インスタンスが準拠しているかを判定し、非準拠であればインスタンスを終了するAWS Lambda関数を作成する。そのLambda関数を5分ごとに呼び出すようスケジュールする。

text.en: Create an AWS Config rule to check if the required tags are present. If an EC2 instance is noncompliant, invoke an AWS Systems Manager Automation document to terminate the instance.
text.ja: 必要なタグが存在するかを確認するAWS Configルールを作成する。EC2インスタンスが非準拠の場合、AWS Systems Manager Automationドキュメントを呼び出してインスタンスを終了する。

comment.en: its D. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/evaluate-config_use-managed-rules.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-automation.html
DDDDDDD
ddddddd
DDDDDD
Ans: D
D. Use Config.
Config Rule terminate or restrict many rules.

question.en:
A SysOps administrator needs to control access to groups of Amazon EC2 instances using AWS Systems Manager Session Manager. Specific tags on the EC2 instances have already been added.
Which additional actions should the administrator take to control access? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、AWS Systems Manager Session Managerを使用してAmazon EC2インスタンスのグループへのアクセスを制御する必要がある。EC2インスタンスには特定のタグがすでに追加されている。
アクセスを制御するために、管理者が取るべき追加のアクションはどれか。(2つ選択)

text.en: Attach an IAM policy to the users or groups that require access to the EC2 instances.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスへのアクセスが必要なユーザーまたはグループにIAMポリシーを添付する。

text.en: Attach an IAM role to control access to the EC2 instances.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスへのアクセスを制御するためにIAMロールを添付する。

text.en: Create a placement group for the EC2 instances and add a specific tag.
text.ja: EC2インスタンス用のプレイスメントグループを作成し、特定のタグを追加する。

text.en: Create a service account and attach it to the EC2 instances that need to be controlled.
text.ja: サービスアカウントを作成し、制御が必要なEC2インスタンスに添付する。

text.en: Create an IAM policy that grants access to any EC2 instances with a tag specified in the Condition element.
text.ja: Condition要素で指定されたタグを持つEC2インスタンスへのアクセスを許可するIAMポリシーを作成する。

comment.en: A. Attach an IAM policy to the users or groups that require access to the EC2 instances: IAM policies can be used to control access to resources in AWS. The policy can specify which actions are allowed or denied and which resources the user or group can access. In this case, the policy should include permissions to use the Session Manager service. E. Create an IAM policy that grants access to any EC2 instances with a tag specified in the Condition element: This policy can specify that access is granted only to instances with specific tags. For example, a policy could specify that users or groups can only access instances that have a specific tag, such as "Environment=Prod". This helps to ensure that only the appropriate instances are accessed.
A and E
AE is the correct answer !
I go for A and E, given that create a policy and attach a role does not solve the problem. You need to attach the policy you've created somewhere.
Could have been A, except you wouldn't attach a policy to a "users or a groups". That's too broad. Best practice is to use a group or a role, and not apply policies directly to a user.

Q97: A team of on-call engineers frequently needs to connect to Amazon EC2 instances in a private subnet to troubleshoot and run commands. The instances use either the latest AWS-provided Windows Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) or Amazon Linux AMIs. The team has an existing 1AM role for authorization. A SysOps administrator must provide the team with access to the instances by granting IAM permissions to this role. Which solution will meet this requirement?" Correct answer: A. Add a statement to the 1AM role policy to allow the ssm:StartSession action on the instances. Instruct the team to use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to connect to the instances by using the assumed IAM role." None of the other answers have antything to do with a policy for group or users. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/getting-started-restrict-access-examples.html#restrict-access-example-instance-tags I vote for "BE" because of policy example and I know role could work. I think wording in "A." is purposefully vague.
Simply remember: The IAM role attached to ec2 instances (B) allows the instances to interact with AWS services (Systems Manager) but doesn't control user access

Control access to smaller deployments of Amazon EC2 instances as follows: 1. Add a specific tag to the instances that you want to grant the users or groups access to. 2. Create an IAM policy that grants access to any instances with the specific tag. 3. Attach the IAM policy to the users or groups that you want to access the instances. Please refer to this link for more information - https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/iam-ec2-resource-tags
B and E
The question does not mention granting users access to anything. Here we only discuss the access from SSM to the instances, which is not enabled by default.

You would create an IAM policy that grants access to any EC2 instance with a tag specified I the condition element (E) and then you would attach that policy to the users or groups which require the access (A). I think B is wrong as you assume and IAM role, you don't attach it...
it cant be be since they are logically unraleted, where one creates a role and second a policy its AE

question.en:
A company has an AWS Lambda function in Account A. The Lambda function needs to read the objects in an Amazon S3 bucket in Account B. A SysOps administrator must create corresponding IAM roles in both accounts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある会社は、アカウントAにAWS Lambda関数を持っている。このLambda関数は、アカウントBにあるAmazon S3バケット内のオブジェクトを読み取る必要がある。SysOps管理者は、両方のアカウントに対応するIAMロールを作成しなければならない。
これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれか。

text.en: In Account A, create a Lambda execution role to assume the role in Account B. In Account B. create a role that the function can assume to gain access to the S3 bucket.
text.ja: アカウントAで、アカウントBのロールを引き受けるLambda実行ロールを作成する。アカウントBで、関数がS3バケットへのアクセスを得るために引き受けられるロールを作成する。

text.en: In Account A, create a Lambda execution role that provides access to the S3 bucket. In Account B, create a role that the function can assume.
text.ja: アカウントAで、S3バケットへのアクセスを提供するLambda実行ロールを作成する。アカウントBで、関数が引き受けられるロールを作成する。

text.en: In Account A, create a role that the function can assume. In Account B, create a Lambda execution role that provides access to the S3 bucket.
text.ja: アカウントAで、関数が引き受けられるロールを作成する。アカウントBで、S3バケットへのアクセスを提供するLambda実行ロールを作成する。

text.en: In Account A. create a role that the function can assume to gain access to the S3 bucket. In Account B, create a Lambda execution role to assume the role in Account A.
text.ja: アカウントAで、S3バケットへのアクセスを得るために関数が引き受けられるロールを作成する。アカウントBで、アカウントAのロールを引き受けるLambda実行ロールを作成する。

comment.en: A. https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/lambda-function-assume-iam-role/
aaaaaaaa
Account A: Lambda function needs a role to "ask permission" (assume a role) in Account B. Account B: S3 bucket has a role that says, "If Account A asks properly, let them access my bucket."
Ans: A
A is the answer.

question.en:
A company is using Amazon CloudFront to serve static content for its web application to its users. The CloudFront distribution uses an existing on-premises website as a custom origin.
The company requires the use of TLS between CloudFront and the origin server. This configuration has worked as expected for several months. However, users are now experiencing HTTP 502 (Bad Gateway) errors when they view webpages that include content from the CloudFront distribution.
What should a SysOps administrator do to resolve this problem?
question.ja:
ある会社は、Amazon CloudFrontを使用してウェブアプリケーションの静的コンテンツをユーザーに配信している。CloudFrontディストリビューションは、既存のオンプレミスのウェブサイトをカスタムオリジンとして使用している。
会社は、CloudFrontとオリジンサーバー間でTLSを使用することを要求している。この構成は数か月間問題なく動作していた。しかし、現在ユーザーは、CloudFrontディストリビューションのコンテンツを含むウェブページを表示する際にHTTP 502(Bad Gateway)エラーを経験している。
この問題を解決するために、SysOps管理者は何をすべきか。

text.en: Examine the expiration date on the certificate on the origin site. Validate that the certificate has not expired. Replace the certificate if necessary.
text.ja: オリジンサイトの証明書の有効期限を確認する。証明書が期限切れになっていないことを検証する。必要に応じて証明書を交換する。

text.en: Examine the hostname on the certificate on the origin site. Validate that the hostname matches one of the hostnames on the CloudFront distribution. Replace the certificate if necessary.
text.ja: オリジンサイトの証明書のホスト名を確認する。ホスト名がCloudFrontディストリビューションのホスト名のいずれかと一致していることを検証する。必要に応じて証明書を交換する。

text.en: Examine the firewall rules that are associated with the origin server. Validate that port 443 is open for inbound traffic from the internet. Create an inbound rule if necessary.
text.ja: オリジンサーバーに関連付けられたファイアウォールルールを確認する。インターネットからのインバウンドトラフィックに対してポート443が開いていることを検証する。必要に応じてインバウンドルールを作成する。

text.en: Examine the network ACL rules that are associated with the CloudFront distribution. Validate that port 443 is open for outbound traffic to the origin server. Create an outbound rule if necessary.
text.ja: CloudFrontディストリビューションに関連付けられたネットワークACLルールを確認する。オリジンサーバーへのアウトバウンドトラフィックに対してポート443が開いていることを検証する。必要に応じてアウトバウンドルールを作成する。

comment.en: A! is correct HTTP 502 errors from CloudFront can occur because of the following reasons: There's an SSL negotiation failure because the origin is using SSL/TLS protocols and ciphers that aren't supported by CloudFront. There's an SSL negotiation failure because the SSL certificate on the origin is expired or invalid, or because the certificate chain is invalid. There's a host header mismatch in the SSL negotiation between your CloudFront distribution and the custom origin. The custom origin isn't responding on the ports specified in the origin settings of the CloudFront distribution. The custom origin is ending the connection to CloudFront too quickly. https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/resolve-cloudfront-connection-error/

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/http-502-bad-gateway.html#origin-not-responding-on-specified-ports could be C, however the keyword is that it used to work and stopped hence A

An HTTP 502 status code (Bad Gateway) indicates that CloudFront wasn't able to serve the requested object because it couldn't connect to the origin server. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/http-502-bad-gateway.html
great !

I think this could be A or B. Both are equally as likely. The certificate could either be expired or doesn't match. Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/http-502-bad-gateway.html
A is the way here
aaaaaaaaa

question.en:
An Amazon CloudFront distribution has a single Amazon S3 bucket as its origin. A SysOps administrator must ensure that users can access the S3 bucket only through requests from the CloudFront endpoint.
Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
あるAmazon CloudFrontディストリビューションは、単一のAmazon S3バケットをオリジンとしている。SysOps管理者は、ユーザーがCloudFrontエンドポイントからのリクエストを通じてのみS3バケットにアクセスできるようにする必要がある。
これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Configure S3 Block Public Access on the S3 bucket. Update the S3 bucket policy to allow the GetObject action from only the CloudFront distribution.
text.ja: S3バケットでS3 Block Public Accessを設定する。S3バケットポリシーを更新して、CloudFrontディストリビューションからのみGetObjectアクションを許可する。

text.en: Configure Origin Shield in the CloudFront distribution. Update the CloudFront origin to include a custom Origin_Shield header.
text.ja: CloudFrontディストリビューションでOrigin Shieldを設定する。CloudFrontオリジンを更新して、カスタムのOrigin_Shieldヘッダーを含めるようにする。

text.en: Create an origin access identity (OAI). Assign the OAI to the CloudFront distribution. Update the S3 bucket policy to restrict access to the OAI.
text.ja: オリジンアクセスアイデンティティ(OAI)を作成する。OAIをCloudFrontディストリビューションに割り当てる。S3バケットポリシーを更新して、OAIへのアクセスのみに制限する。

text.en: Create an origin access identity (OAI). Assign the OAI to the S3 bucket. Update the CloudFront origin to include a custom Origin header with the OAI value.
text.ja: オリジンアクセスアイデンティティ(OAI)を作成する。OAIをS3バケットに割り当てる。CloudFrontオリジンを更新して、OAIの値を持つカスタムのOriginヘッダーを含めるようにする。

comment.en: C is the correct answer https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/private-content-restricting-access-to-s3.html
C is the answer

They probably typed wrongly. If I recall correctly, the button reads somewhere along the lines of 'bucket can restrict access to only OAI', and what it achieves is allowing S3 objects to be accessed only through CloudFront.
Answer C says, "..restrict access to OAI" I obviously didn't understand it. Why do we restrict access to OAI? Actually, I think we need to allow read-only access to OAI.
Origin Access Identity (OAI): An OAI is a special CloudFront user that you can associate with one or more CloudFront distributions
