question.en:
A SysOps administrator needs to automate the invocation of an AWS Lambda function. The Lambda function must run at the end of each day to generate a report on data that is stored in an Amazon S3 bucket.

What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirements?
question.ja:
SysOps administrator は、AWS Lambda 関数の呼び出しを自動化する必要があります。この Lambda 関数は、Amazon S3 バケットに保存されているデータに関するレポートを生成するために、毎日の終わりに実行される必要があります。

これらの要件を満たす最も運用効率の良いソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule that has an event pattern for Amazon S3 and the Lambda function as a target.
text.ja: Amazon S3 に対するイベントパターンを持ち、Lambda 関数をターゲットとする Amazon EventBridge（Amazon CloudWatch Events）ルールを作成する。

text.en: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule that has a schedule and the Lambda function as a target.
text.ja: スケジュールを持ち、Lambda 関数をターゲットとする Amazon EventBridge（Amazon CloudWatch Events）ルールを作成する。

text.en: Create an S3 event notification to invoke the Lambda function whenever objects change in the S3 bucket.
text.ja: S3 バケット内のオブジェクトが変更されたときに Lambda 関数を呼び出す S3 イベント通知を作成する。

text.en: Deploy an Amazon EC2 instance with a cron job to invoke the Lambda function.
text.ja: cron ジョブを持つ Amazon EC2 インスタンスをデプロイして Lambda 関数を呼び出す。

comment.en: Ans B S3 Event notification has nothing to do here since the lambda will be triggered based on a daily schedule not every time an object is uploaded
bbbbbbbb
B: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/compute/introducing-amazon-eventbridge-scheduler/
b is correct

question.en:
A company is releasing a new static website hosted on Amazon S3. The static website hosting feature was enabled on the bucket and content was uploaded; however, upon navigating to the site, the following error message is received:

403 Forbidden - Access Denied

What change should be made to fix this error?
question.ja:
ある企業は、Amazon S3 でホストされる新しい静的ウェブサイトをリリースしています。バケットで静的ウェブサイトホスティング機能を有効化し、コンテンツをアップロードしましたが、サイトにアクセスすると次のエラーメッセージが表示されます。

403 Forbidden - Access Denied

このエラーを解消するために、どのような変更を行うべきですか。

text.en: Add a bucket policy that grants everyone read access to the bucket.
text.ja: バケットへの読み取りアクセスを全員に許可するバケットポリシーを追加する。

text.en: Add a bucket policy that grants everyone read access to the bucket objects.
text.ja: バケットオブジェクトへの読み取りアクセスを全員に許可するバケットポリシーを追加する。

text.en: Remove the default bucket policy that denies read access to the bucket.
text.ja: バケットへの読み取りアクセスを拒否するデフォルトのバケットポリシーを削除する。

text.en: Configure cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) on the bucket.
text.ja: バケットに対して cross-origin resource sharing（CORS）を設定する。

comment.en: B. Per AWS: https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/s3-static-website-endpoint-error "If an Access Denied error is returned by the web browser or cURL command, then the object isn't publicly accessible. To allow public read access to your S3 object, create a bucket policy that allows public read access for all objects in the bucket." I'm not sure why everyone here is trying to provide answers without actual references. How you think in your head is irrelevant if it's not correct with AWS documentation.
Ans B Ref: https://aws.amazon.com/es/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/s3-static-website-endpoint-error/

The answer is B, here is a sample bucket policy { "Version": "2012-10-12", "Statement": { "Sid": "PublicReadGetObject", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "s3:GetObject" ], "Principal": "*", "Resouce": [ "arn:aws:s3:::example-s3-website.com/*" ] } }

A. Add a bucket policy that grants everyone read access to the bucket. To fix the error message "403 Forbidden - Access Denied" when trying to access a static website hosted on Amazon S3, you should add a bucket policy that grants everyone read access to the bucket. By default, Amazon S3 bucket policies deny all public access to the bucket and its contents. To allow users to access the static website hosted on the bucket, you need to add a bucket policy that grants read access to the bucket. You can do this by specifying a bucket policy that includes a "Principal" element with the value "*" (wildcard), which grants access to everyone, and an "Action" element with the value "s3:GetObject", which allows users to retrieve objects from the bucket. Option B, adding a bucket policy that grants everyone read access to the bucket objects, would not be a valid solution, as this would not grant read access to the bucket itself.
Reading objects, not just reading from the bucket, is the key to static website hosting. Nobody browses to the bucket, but instead the files themselves.

It can't be A because Amazon doesn't have "Block Public Access Policy" feature, it has "Block Public Access Settings" - The key difference is Policy vs. Settings. Therefore A is confusing to many. Once you clear the "Block Public Access" SETTINGS you then ADD A BUCKET POLICY that makes your bucket content publicly available.
Answer: B

This will allow anyone to access the objects in the bucket and view the static website. Option A, "Add a bucket policy that grants everyone read access to the bucket," grants read access to the bucket itself, but not necessarily to its objects.
Answer is B.

COMPLETE WRONG IF YOU'RE GOING WITH OPTION A!....You're adding a bucket policy which grants Read access only to the bucket level?? Do you think everyone can read the bucket objects 'Magically' ??! Pls. read the question again and then you'll realize that the correct option is only B!
bbbbbb

question.en:
A company uses AWS Organizations. A SysOps administrator wants to use AWS Compute Optimizer and AWS tag policies in the management account to govern all member accounts in the billing family. The SysOps administrator navigates to the AWS Organizations console but cannot activate tag policies through the management account.

What could be the reason for this issue?
question.ja:
ある企業は AWS Organizations を使用しています。SysOps administrator は、管理アカウントで AWS Compute Optimizer と AWS のタグポリシーを使用して、billing family 内のすべてのメンバーアカウントを統制したいと考えています。SysOps administrator は AWS Organizations コンソールに移動しますが、管理アカウントからタグポリシーを有効化できません。

この問題の原因として考えられるものは何ですか。

text.en: All features have not been enabled in the organization.
text.ja: 組織内ですべての機能が有効化されていない。

text.en: Consolidated billing has not been enabled.
text.ja: 一括請求（consolidated billing）が有効化されていない。

text.en: The member accounts do not have tags enabled for cost allocation.
text.ja: メンバーアカウントでコスト配分用のタグが有効化されていない。

text.en: The member accounts have not manually enabled trusted access for Compute Optimizer.
text.ja: メンバーアカウントで Compute Optimizer の信頼されたアクセス（trusted access）が手動で有効化されていない。

comment.en: Ans: A Ref: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_tag-policies-prereqs.html
sorry A is the correct
ag policies and AWS Compute Optimizer are such features that need to be enabled explicitly within the organization.
aaaaaa
aaaaaaa

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_org_support-all-features.html

question.en:
A company is storing media content in an Amazon S3 bucket and uses Amazon CloudFront to distribute the content to its users. Due to licensing terms, the company is not authorized to distribute the content in some countries. A SysOps administrator must restrict access to certain countries.

What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は Amazon S3 バケットにメディアコンテンツを保存し、Amazon CloudFront を使用してユーザーにコンテンツを配信しています。ライセンス条件により、この企業は一部の国でコンテンツを配信することを許可されていません。SysOps administrator は、特定の国からのアクセスを制限する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たす最も運用効率の良いソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Configure the S3 bucket policy to deny the GetObject operation based on the S3:LocationConstraint condition.
text.ja: S3:LocationConstraint 条件に基づいて GetObject 操作を拒否するように S3 バケットポリシーを設定する。

text.en: Create a secondary origin access identity (OAI). Configure the S3 bucket policy to prevent access from unauthorized countries.
text.ja: セカンダリの origin access identity（OAI）を作成する。許可されていない国からのアクセスを防止するように S3 バケットポリシーを設定する。

text.en: Enable the geo restriction feature in the CloudFront distribution to prevent access from unauthorized countries.
text.ja: CloudFront ディストリビューションの geo restriction 機能を有効化し、許可されていない国からのアクセスを防止する。

text.en: Update the application to generate signed CloudFront URLs only for IP addresses in authorized counties.
text.ja: アプリケーションを更新し、許可された国の IP アドレスに対してのみ署名付き CloudFront URL を生成するようにする。

comment.en: You can use geographic restrictions, sometimes known as geo blocking, to prevent users in specific geographic locations from accessing content that you're distributing through a CloudFront distribution. To use geographic restrictions, you have two options: Use the CloudFront geographic restrictions feature. Use this option to restrict access to all of the files that are associated with a distribution and to restrict access at the country level. Use a third-party geolocation service. Use this option to restrict access to a subset of the files that are associated with a distribution or to restrict access at a finer granularity than the country level.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/georestrictions.html
cccccc
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/georestrictions.html
