spot instance advisor is an advisor: https://www.cloudzero.com/blog/spot-instance-advisor capacity optimized is a strategy, need to use a strategy for launch instance in ASG https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/spot-fleet-allocation-strategy.html
C is correct https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2019/08/new-capacity-optimized-allocation-strategy-for-provisioning-amazon-ec2-spot-instances/

question.en:
A SysOps administrator creates a custom Amazon Machine Image (AMI) in the eu-west-2 Region and uses the AMI to launch Amazon EC2 instances. The SysOps administrator needs to use the same AMI to launch EC2 instances in two other Regions: us-east-1 and us-east-2.

What must the SysOps administrator do to use the custom AMI in the additional Regions?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者がeu-west-2リージョンでカスタムAmazon Machine Image（AMI）を作成し、そのAMIを使用してAmazon EC2インスタンスを起動しています。このSysOps管理者は、同じAMIを使用して、他の2つのリージョン（us-east-1とus-east-2）でEC2インスタンスを起動する必要があります。

SysOps管理者は、追加のリージョンでこのカスタムAMIを使用するために何を行う必要がありますか。

text.en: Copy the AMI to the additional Regions.
text.ja: AMIを追加のリージョンにコピーする。

text.en: Make the AMI public in the Community AMIs section of the AWS Management Console.
text.ja: AWS Management ConsoleのCommunity AMIsセクションでAMIをパブリックにする。

text.en: Share the AMI to the additional Regions. Assign the required access permissions.
text.ja: AMIを追加のリージョンに共有する。必要なアクセス許可を割り当てる。

text.en: Copy the AMI to a new Amazon S3 bucket. Assign access permissions to the AMI for the additional Regions.
text.ja: AMIを新しいAmazon S3バケットにコピーする。追加のリージョンに対してAMIへのアクセス許可を割り当てる。

comment.en: AMIs are region specfic so you must copy to use them in different regions...

Region – AMIs are a Regional resource. When you share an AMI, it is only available in that Region. To make an AMI available in a different Region, copy the AMI to the Region and then share it. For more information, see Copy an AMI. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/sharingamis-explicit.html
https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/copy-ami-region https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/CopyingAMIs.html#ami-copy-steps
Ref link: https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/copy-ami-region
D also works, but not normally used.

question.en:
A company is building an interactive application for personal finance. The application stores financial data in Amazon S3, and the data must be encrypted. The company does not want to provide its own encryption keys. However, the company wants to maintain an audit trail that shows when an encryption key was used and who used the key.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業が個人向け財務管理のためのインタラクティブなアプリケーションを構築しています。このアプリケーションはAmazon S3に財務データを保存し、そのデータは暗号化されている必要があります。この企業は自社で暗号化キーを用意したくありません。しかし、暗号化キーがいつ、誰によって使用されたかを示す監査証跡を維持したいと考えています。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Use client-side encryption with client-provided keys. Upload the encrypted user data to Amazon S3.
text.ja: クライアント提供のキーを使用してクライアント側暗号化を行う。暗号化されたユーザーデータをAmazon S3にアップロードする。

text.en: Use server-side encryption with S3 managed encryption keys (SSE-S3) to encrypt the user data on Amazon S3.
text.ja: S3マネージド暗号化キー（SSE-S3）によるサーバー側暗号化を使用して、Amazon S3上のユーザーデータを暗号化する。

text.en: Use server-side encryption with customer-provided encryption keys (SSE-C) to encrypt the user data on Amazon S3.
text.ja: カスタマー提供の暗号化キー（SSE-C）によるサーバー側暗号化を使用して、Amazon S3上のユーザーデータを暗号化する。

text.en: Use server-side encryption with AWS KMS managed encryption keys (SSE-KMS) to encrypt the user data on Amazon S3.
text.ja: AWS KMSマネージド暗号化キー（SSE-KMS）によるサーバー側暗号化を使用して、Amazon S3上のユーザーデータを暗号化する。

comment.en: While SSE-S3 is convenient, it doesn't allow the company to have control over key management or maintain a detailed audit trail for key usage. AWS KMS provides detailed audit logs through AWS CloudTrail, which allows you to monitor and log all API calls related to key usage
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/concepts.html#auditing_key_use
why not B : SSE-S3 do not provide detailed audit.
Answer D: with SSE-KMS, you have more control over your keys than you do with SSE-S3. For example, you can follow the keys in AWS Cloud Trail.

question.en:
A company has an AWS CloudFormation template that creates an Amazon S3 bucket. A user authenticates to the corporate AWS account with their Active Directory credentials and attempts to deploy the CloudFormation template. However, the stack creation fails.

Which factors could cause this failure? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある企業に、Amazon S3バケットを作成するAWS CloudFormationテンプレートがあります。あるユーザーがActive Directoryの認証情報で企業のAWSアカウントに認証し、そのCloudFormationテンプレートをデプロイしようとしています。しかし、スタックの作成が失敗します。

この失敗を引き起こす可能性のある要因はどれですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: The user’s IAM policy does not allow the cloudformation:CreateStack action.
text.ja: ユーザーのIAMポリシーがcloudformation:CreateStackアクションを許可していない。

text.en: The user’s IAM policy does not allow the cloudformation:CreateStackSet action.
text.ja: ユーザーのIAMポリシーがcloudformation:CreateStackSetアクションを許可していない。

text.en: The user’s IAM policy does not allow the s3:CreateBucket action.
text.ja: ユーザーのIAMポリシーがs3:CreateBucketアクションを許可していない。

text.en: The user’s IAM policy explicitly denies the s3:ListBucket action.
text.ja: ユーザーのIAMポリシーがs3:ListBucketアクションを明示的に拒否している。

text.en: The user’s IAM policy explicitly denies the s3:PutObject action.
text.ja: ユーザーのIAMポリシーがs3:PutObjectアクションを明示的に拒否している。

comment.en: Options B, D, and E are not relevant to the CloudFormation stack creation failure for an S3 bucket. Option B is related to CloudFormation StackSets, not individual stacks. Option D and E are related to specific S3 bucket actions (list and put) and are not directly related to the stack creation process. Therefore, the correct answers are A and C.

The failure of the CloudFormation stack creation can be due to several factors related to IAM policies and S3 bucket permissions. Let's go through the options: A. The user’s IAM policy does not allow the cloudformation:CreateStack action. This could definitely cause the stack creation to fail, as the user needs permission to create CloudFormation stacks. B. The user’s IAM policy does not allow the cloudformation:CreateStackSet action. This action is related to stack sets, not individual stacks. Since the question is about stack creation, this is less likely to be the cause of the failure. C. The user’s IAM policy does not allow the s3:CreateBucket action. This is a crucial permission because the CloudFormation template is creating an S3 bucket. If the user doesn't have permission to create S3 buckets, the stack creation will fail.

Options B, D, and E are less likely to be directly related to the failure of stack creation: B. The user’s IAM policy does not allow the cloudformation:CreateStackSet action. Stack sets are generally used for deploying stacks across multiple accounts and regions, and it might not be directly related to a stack creation failure in a single account. D. The user’s IAM policy explicitly denies the s3:ListBucket action. While s3:ListBucket is needed for some S3 operations, it's not necessarily required for creating an S3 bucket. E. The user’s IAM policy explicitly denies the s3:PutObject action. Denying s3:PutObject would prevent the user from uploading objects to an existing S3 bucket. It's not a direct factor for creating a new S3 bucket.

D. The user’s IAM policy explicitly denies the s3:ListBucket action. While this could cause issues with certain operations on the bucket, it's not directly related to the creation of the bucket itself during the stack creation process. E. The user’s IAM policy explicitly denies the s3:PutObject action. This is related to adding objects to the bucket, not creating the bucket itself during stack creation. So, the two factors that could cause the failure of the stack creation are: A. The user’s IAM policy does not allow the cloudformation:CreateStack action. C. The user’s IAM policy does not allow the s3:CreateBucket action.

question.en:
An Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL DB cluster has automated backups turned on with a 7-day retention period. A SysOps administrator needs to create a new RDS DB cluster by using data that is no more than 24 hours old from the original DB cluster.

Which solutions will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
あるAmazon RDS for PostgreSQL DBクラスターでは、自動バックアップが有効になっており、保持期間は7日間に設定されています。SysOps管理者は、元のDBクラスターから24時間以内のデータを使用して、新しいRDS DBクラスターを作成する必要があります。

これらの要件を最も運用オーバーヘッドが少ない方法で満たすソリューションはどれですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Identify the most recent automated snapshot. Restore the snapshot to a new RDS DB cluster.
text.ja: 最新の自動スナップショットを特定する。そのスナップショットを新しいRDS DBクラスターに復元する。

text.en: Back up the database to Amazon S3 by using native database backup tools. Create a new RDS DB cluster and restore the data to the new RDS DB cluster.
text.ja: ネイティブのデータベースバックアップツールを使用して、データベースをAmazon S3にバックアップする。新しいRDS DBクラスターを作成し、そのデータを新しいRDS DBクラスターに復元する。

text.en: Create a read replica instance in the original RDS DB cluster. Promote the read replica to a standalone DB cluster.
text.ja: 元のRDS DBクラスターにリードレプリカインスタンスを作成する。そのリードレプリカをスタンドアロンのDBクラスターにプロモートする。

text.en: Create a new RDS DB cluster. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) to migrate data from the current RDS DB cluster to the newly created RDS DB cluster.
text.ja: 新しいRDS DBクラスターを作成する。AWS Database Migration Service（AWS DMS）を使用して、現在のRDS DBクラスターから新しく作成したRDS DBクラスターへデータを移行する。

text.en: Use the pg_dump utility to export data from the original RDS DB cluster to an Amazon EC2 instance. Create a new RDS DB cluster. Use the pg_restore utility to import the data from the EC2 instance to the new RDS DB cluster.
text.ja: pg_dumpユーティリティを使用して、元のRDS DBクラスターからAmazon EC2インスタンスへデータをエクスポートする。新しいRDS DBクラスターを作成する。pg_restoreユーティリティを使用して、EC2インスタンスから新しいRDS DBクラスターへデータをインポートする。

comment.en: Option A - Automated Snapshot: Since automated backups are enabled with a 7-day retention period, you can easily identify the most recent automated snapshot taken within the last 24 hours. You can use this snapshot to restore a new RDS DB cluster. This process is straightforward and does not require much operational overhead. Option C - Read Replica and Promotion: By creating a read replica in the original RDS DB cluster, you ensure that the replica stays up-to-date with the source database in near real-time. To meet the requirement of having data no more than 24 hours old, you can promote the read replica to a standalone DB cluster within the desired time frame. This option leverages the replication mechanism and is also relatively simple to implement.

There are actually CLI commands defined to do both "A" and "C". I believe the DB Migration Service is used to migrate and reformat external databases to AWS. I see no references to it being used to copy/migrate data within AWS. Anyway, here are the CLI examples I mentioned which I found online: # Promotes specified read replica to become a standalone DB cluster. aws rds promote-read-replica-db-cluster --db-cluster-identifier mydbcluster-1 # Creates a new DB cluster by resoring a DB cluster snapshot named test-instance-snapshot. aws rds restore-db-cluster-from-snapshot --db-cluster-identifier newdbcluster --snapshot-identifier test-instance-snapshot --engine aurora-postgresql --engine-version 10.7

A - https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_WorkingWithAutomatedBackups.html C - You can promote a read replica into a standalone DB instance. When you promote a read replica, the DB instance is rebooted before it becomes available. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_ReadRepl.html
A C looks good

Options B, D, and E involve additional steps and may have more operational overhead: B. Back up the database to Amazon S3 by using native database backup tools. Create a new RDS DB cluster and restore the data to the new RDS DB cluster. This involves manual steps and might have more operational overhead. D. Create a new RDS DB cluster. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) to migrate data from the current RDS DB cluster to the newly created RDS DB cluster. AWS DMS is a powerful tool but may introduce more complexity, especially if the goal is a relatively straightforward copy of data within a short time frame. E. Use the pg_dump utility to export data from the original RDS DB cluster to an Amazon EC2 instance. Create a new RDS DB cluster. Use the pg_restore utility to import the data from the EC2 instance to the new RDS DB cluster. This involves manual export/import steps and may not be as efficient as using automated snapshot restoration or read replica promotion.
backup, migration require "Read" Transaction - cause "Operational Overhead".
Create new from backup or read replica

question.en:
A SysOps administrator needs to configure the Amazon Route 53 hosted zone for example.com and www.example.com to point to an Application Load Balancer (ALB).

Which combination of actions should the SysOps administrator take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、example.comとwww.example.comのAmazon Route 53ホストゾーンを、Application Load Balancer（ALB）を指すように設定する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者はどの組み合わせのアクションを取るべきですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Configure an A record for example.com to point to the IP address of the ALB.
text.ja: example.comのAレコードを、ALBのIPアドレスを指すように設定する。

text.en: Configure an A record for www.example.com to point to the IP address of the ALB.
text.ja: www.example.comのAレコードを、ALBのIPアドレスを指すように設定する。

text.en: Configure an alias record for example.com to point to the CNAME of the ALB.
text.ja: example.comのエイリアスレコードを、ALBのCNAMEを指すように設定する。

text.en: Configure an alias record for www.example.com to point to the Route 53 example.com record.
text.ja: www.example.comのエイリアスレコードを、Route 53のexample.comレコードを指すように設定する。

text.en: Configure a CNAME record for example.com to point to the CNAME of the ALB.
text.ja: example.comのCNAMEレコードを、ALBのCNAMEを指すように設定する。

comment.en: C. Configure an alias record for example.com to point to the CNAME of the ALB. Explanation: Amazon Route 53 supports alias records, which allow you to map your root domain (example.com) to the ALB directly without using an IP address. To do this, you would create an alias record for example.com and select the ALB's CNAME as the alias target. B. Configure an A record for www.example.com to point to the IP address of the ALB. Explanation: For the www subdomain (www.example.com), you typically use an A record to map it to an IP address. In this case, you would create an A record for www.example.com and set its value to the IP address of the ALB.
Alias to the ALB in both cases, not A record to ALB IP address.

:) No correct answer here, but the clothes you can lean - C, D. C, D - wrong, - there is no such a thing as IP address for ALB, as it's hidden for user and you only have an ALB DNS name, therefore it's not A&B. E - wrong, as you can't create CNAME for apex (example.com). C, D - well, you are creating record type A, not alias, but once you create record type A, you use ALB alias to route traffic to ALB endpoint (DNS).
alias for root/sub domain point to ALB cname. (name for root domain not possible)
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/routing-to-elb-load-balancer.html
Typo A, B - - wrong, - there is no such a thing as IP address for ALB, as it's hidden for user and you only have an ALB DNS name, therefore it's not A&B.

I vote A and D because I believe you have to have an "A" record pointing to an IP for the domain name, and that the you just need an "www" alias to point to the "A" record so they both return same results to browser.

I think it is B, not A. Recently I needed to point a domain apex to an Application Load Balancer created in an AWS environment. The only problem here was that the domain apex or root domain cannot point to an Application Load Balancer’s endpoint. If we talk about in terms of DNS records, we cannot add a CNAME record against the apex. We can map an IP address to the apex as an A record. So, all we needed was a static IP address for our load balancer which we could map to our root domain and point it to the Load Balancer. The small issue was that Application Load Balancer does not have static IP addresses. So, I looked for methods to assign a static IP (elastic IP) address to the load balancer and found an AWS service named Global Accelerator which made it super-easy to assign static IP to the load balancer. Ref link: https://kavishbaghel.com/how-to-point-domain-apex-to-an-application-load-balancer-88a7b82d19e9
I meant C.
Look at these questions which help to understand the answer B & B: (Questions #5 and #23)

question.en:
A company has a hybrid environment. The company has set up an AWS Direct Connect connection between the company's on-premises data center and a workload that runs in a VPC. The company uses Amazon Route 53 for DNS on AWS. The company uses a private hosted zone to manage DNS names for a set of services that are hosted on AWS.

The company wants the on-premises servers to use Route 53 for DNS resolution of the private hosted zone.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業がハイブリッド環境を運用しています。この企業は、オンプレミスのデータセンターとVPC内で稼働するワークロードとの間にAWS Direct Connect接続を設定しています。この企業はAWS上のDNSにAmazon Route 53を使用しています。この企業は、AWS上でホストされている一連のサービスのDNS名を管理するために、プライベートホストゾーンを使用しています。

この企業は、オンプレミスのサーバーがプライベートホストゾーンのDNS解決にRoute 53を使用できるようにしたいと考えています。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create a Route 53 inbound endpoint. Ensure that security groups and routing allow the traffic from the on-premises data center. Configure the DNS server on the on-premises network to conditionally forward DNS queries for the private hosted zone's domain name to the IP addresses of the inbound endpoint.
text.ja: Route 53インバウンドエンドポイントを作成する。オンプレミスのデータセンターからのトラフィックがセキュリティグループとルーティングによって許可されていることを確認する。オンプレミスネットワーク上のDNSサーバーを設定して、プライベートホストゾーンのドメイン名に対するDNSクエリを、インバウンドエンドポイントのIPアドレスへ条件付きで転送する。

text.en: Create a Route 53 outbound endpoint. Ensure that security groups and routing allow the traffic from the VPC. Configure the DNS server on the on-premises network to conditionally forward DNS queries for the private hosted zone’s domain name to the IP addresses of the outbound endpoint.
text.ja: Route 53アウトバウンドエンドポイントを作成する。VPCからのトラフィックがセキュリティグループとルーティングによって許可されていることを確認する。オンプレミスネットワーク上のDNSサーバーを設定して、プライベートホストゾーンのドメイン名に対するDNSクエリを、アウトバウンドエンドポイントのIPアドレスへ条件付きで転送する。

text.en: Edit the private hosted zone in Route 53 with a TXT record that references the on-premises DNS servers. Configure the DNS server on the on-premises network to conditionally forward DNS queries for the private hosted zone’s domain name to the base of the VPC CIDR IPv4 network range, plus two.
text.ja: Route 53のプライベートホストゾーンを、オンプレミスのDNSサーバーを参照するTXTレコードで編集する。オンプレミスネットワーク上のDNSサーバーを設定して、プライベートホストゾーンのドメイン名に対するDNSクエリを、VPC CIDRのIPv4ネットワーク範囲の先頭アドレス+2へ条件付きで転送する。

text.en: Edit the private hosted zone in Route 53 with a PTR record that references the on-premises DNS servers. Configure the DNS server on the on-premises network to conditionally forward DNS queries for the private hosted zone’s domain name to the base of the VPC CIDR IPv4 network range, plus two.
text.ja: Route 53のプライベートホストゾーンを、オンプレミスのDNSサーバーを参照するPTRレコードで編集する。オンプレミスネットワーク上のDNSサーバーを設定して、プライベートホストゾーンのドメイン名に対するDNSクエリを、VPC CIDRのIPv4ネットワーク範囲の先頭アドレス+2へ条件付きで転送する。

comment.en: Inbound Resolver endpoints allow DNS queries to your VPC from your on-premises network or another VPC. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/resolver.html

Explanation: By creating a Route 53 outbound endpoint, the on-premises servers can forward DNS queries for the private hosted zone's domain name to Route 53. This allows the on-premises servers to resolve DNS queries for the private hosted zone using Route 53's DNS resolution. The outbound endpoint provides a secure and efficient way for on-premises DNS resolution. A is incorrect because the inbound endpoint is used for receiving DNS queries from your on-premises network to resolve public DNS names. It is not suitable for resolving private hosted zone names. C and D are incorrect because editing the private hosted zone with TXT or PTR records does not enable the on-premises servers to use Route 53 for DNS resolution. The solution lies in using outbound endpoints for DNS resolution from on-premises servers.

question.en:
A SysOps administrator is evaluating Amazon Route 53 DNS options to address concerns about high availability for an on-premises website. The website consists of two servers: a primary active server and a secondary passive server. Route 53 should route traffic to the primary server if the associated health check returns 2xx or 3xx HTTP codes. All other traffic should be directed to the secondary passive server. The failover record type, set ID, and routing policy have been set appropriately for both primary and secondary servers.

Which next step should be taken to configure Route 53?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者が、オンプレミスのウェブサイトに関する高可用性の懸念に対応するために、Amazon Route 53のDNSオプションを評価しています。このウェブサイトは、プライマリのアクティブサーバーとセカンダリのパッシブサーバーという2台のサーバーで構成されています。関連するヘルスチェックが2xxまたは3xxのHTTPコードを返す場合、Route 53はトラフィックをプライマリサーバーへルーティングする必要があります。それ以外のすべてのトラフィックはセカンダリのパッシブサーバーへ振り向けられる必要があります。フェイルオーバーレコードタイプ、セットID、およびルーティングポリシーは、プライマリサーバーとセカンダリサーバーの両方に対して適切に設定済みです。

Route 53を設定するために取るべき次のステップはどれですか。

text.en: Create an A record for each server. Associate the records with the Route 53 HTTP health check.
text.ja: 各サーバーのAレコードを作成する。それらのレコードをRoute 53のHTTPヘルスチェックに関連付ける。

text.en: Create an A record for each server. Associate the records with the Route 53 TCP health check.
text.ja: 各サーバーのAレコードを作成する。それらのレコードをRoute 53のTCPヘルスチェックに関連付ける。

text.en: Create an alias record for each server with evaluate target health set to yes. Associate the records with the Route 53 HTTP health check.
text.ja: 各サーバーのエイリアスレコードを、evaluate target healthを「yes」に設定して作成する。それらのレコードをRoute 53のHTTPヘルスチェックに関連付ける。

text.en: Create an alias record for each server with evaluate target health set to yes. Associate the records with the Route 53 TCP health check.
text.ja: 各サーバーのエイリアスレコードを、evaluate target healthを「yes」に設定して作成する。それらのレコードをRoute 53のTCPヘルスチェックに関連付ける。

comment.en: We are talking about two on-premise web servers, meaning we have IP addresses for them. What we should do is to create proper records in a public hosted zone, e.g. A-record in this case. We are not working with aliases for on-premise resources. Aliases are for resources in Cloud, e.g. ELB. Last thing is that we are talking about HTTP protocol.
wow almost all of these posts are wrong. it is 100% A and not C. You CANT use alias for on-prem
By Jove, I think you may be right!

"Evaluate health of an AWS resource (alias records)" "If you're using alias records to route traffic to selected AWS resources, such as ELB load balancers, you can configure Route 53 to evaluate the health of the resource and to route traffic only to resources that are healthy." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/dns-failover-simple-configs.html

Explanation: In this scenario, you want Route 53 to route traffic to the primary server if the associated health check returns 2xx or 3xx HTTP codes. All other traffic should be directed to the secondary passive server. To achieve this, you need to create alias records for each server with evaluate target health set to yes, which means Route 53 will perform health checks on the targets associated with these records. Choosing an alias record is appropriate because alias records can be used to map the domain name to an Elastic Load Balancer, an Amazon S3 bucket, an Amazon CloudFront distribution, or another Route 53 resource record set. Alias records also support health checks. Using an HTTP health check is suitable because you need to check the health of the servers based on HTTP responses. Route 53 will evaluate the HTTP status codes and determine which server is healthy to direct traffic to.
a record can't failover
A record also works, but alias is more recommended.
you cant use alias outside AWS read the docs before posting

from https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/ResourceRecordTypes.html "Amazon Route 53 also supports alias records, which allow you to route queries to selected AWS resources, such as CloudFront distributions and Amazon S3 buckets."

question.en:
An Amazon EC2 instance is running an application that uses Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queues. A SysOps administrator must ensure that the application can read, write, and delete messages from the SQS queues.

Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST secure manner?
question.ja:
あるAmazon EC2インスタンスが、Amazon Simple Queue Service（Amazon SQS）キューを使用するアプリケーションを実行しています。SysOps管理者は、そのアプリケーションがSQSキューからメッセージの読み取り、書き込み、削除を行えることを保証する必要があります。

これらの要件を最も安全な方法で満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an IAM user with an IAM policy that allows the sqs:SendMessage permission, the sqs:ReceiveMessage permission, and the sqs:DeleteMessage permission to the appropriate queues. Embed the IAM user's credentials in the application's configuration
text.ja: 該当するキューに対してsqs:SendMessage許可、sqs:ReceiveMessage許可、sqs:DeleteMessage許可を許可するIAMポリシーを持つIAMユーザーを作成する。そのIAMユーザーの認証情報をアプリケーションの設定に埋め込む。

text.en: Create an IAM user with an IAM policy that allows the sqs:SendMessage permission, the sqs:RecelveMessage permission, and the sqs:DeleteMessage permission to the appropriate queues. Export the IAM user's access key and secret access key as environment variables on the EC2 instance.
text.ja: 該当するキューに対してsqs:SendMessage許可、sqs:ReceiveMessage許可、sqs:DeleteMessage許可を許可するIAMポリシーを持つIAMユーザーを作成する。そのIAMユーザーのアクセスキーとシークレットアクセスキーをEC2インスタンス上の環境変数としてエクスポートする。

text.en: Create and associate an IAM role that allows EC2 instances to call AWS services. Attach an IAM policy to the role that allows sqs:* permissions to the appropriate queues.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスがAWSサービスを呼び出せるようにするIAMロールを作成し、関連付ける。該当するキューに対してsqs:*許可を許可するIAMポリシーをそのロールにアタッチする。

text.en: Create and associate an IAM role that allows EC2 instances to call AWS services. Attach an IAM policy to the role that allows the sqs:SendMessage permission, the sqs:ReceiveMessage permission, and the sqs:DeleteMessage permission to the appropriate queues.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスがAWSサービスを呼び出せるようにするIAMロールを作成し、関連付ける。該当するキューに対してsqs:SendMessage許可、sqs:ReceiveMessage許可、sqs:DeleteMessage許可を許可するIAMポリシーをそのロールにアタッチする。

comment.en: 1.IAM users - aren't recommended for use. Out. 2.Considering "least privilege" approach, option D is a better fit.
FYI, 99.9% times you are not supposed to use the wildcard on any policy (service:*). you are always supposed to use the least privilege access, meaning you need to only declare the actions you need.
correct !!
wrong. it's C.

question.en:
A SysOps administrator needs to configure an Amazon S3 bucket to host a web application. The SysOps administrator has created the S3 bucket and has copied the static files for the web application to the S3 bucket.

The company has a policy that all $3 buckets must not be public.

What should the SysOps administrator do to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、ウェブアプリケーションをホストするためにAmazon S3バケットを設定する必要があります。SysOps管理者はすでにS3バケットを作成し、ウェブアプリケーションの静的ファイルをそのS3バケットにコピーしています。

この企業には、すべてのS3バケットを非公開にしなければならないというポリシーがあります。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者は何を行うべきですか。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudFront distribution. Configure the S3 bucket as an origin with an origin access identity (OAI). Give the OAI the s3:GetObject permission in the S3 bucket policy.
text.ja: Amazon CloudFrontディストリビューションを作成する。オリジンアクセスアイデンティティ（OAI）を使用してS3バケットをオリジンとして設定する。S3バケットポリシーでOAIにs3:GetObject許可を与える。

text.en: Configure static website hosting in the S3 bucket. Use Amazon Route 53 to create a DNS CNAME to point to the S3 website endpoint.
text.ja: S3バケットで静的ウェブサイトホスティングを設定する。Amazon Route 53を使用して、S3のウェブサイトエンドポイントを指すDNS CNAMEを作成する。

text.en: Create an Application Load Balancer (ALB). Change the protocol to HTTPS in the ALB listener configuration. Forward the traffic to the S3 bucket.
text.ja: Application Load Balancer（ALB）を作成する。ALBのリスナー設定でプロトコルをHTTPSに変更する。トラフィックをS3バケットへ転送する。

text.en: Create an accelerator in AWS Global Accelerator. Set up a listener configuration for port 443. Set the endpoint type to forward the traffic to the S3 bucket.
text.ja: AWS Global Acceleratorでアクセラレータを作成する。ポート443のリスナー設定を構築する。エンドポイントタイプを、トラフィックをS3バケットへ転送するように設定する。

comment.en: A - Option A is the best fit.

B makes no sense since Route 53 will point to a bucket that does not have public permissions. . . Answer is A because you can create OAI and set s3 bucket as origin for CloudFront, and have the Bucket completely blocked publicly
A. If it was an Alias record instead of CNAME, would be an option as well. Others just doesnt make sense at all.

"If you don't want to disable block public access settings for your bucket but you still want your website to be public, you can create a Amazon CloudFront distribution to serve your static website." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/WebsiteAccessPermissionsReqd.html

Options A, C, and D involve additional services (CloudFront, Application Load Balancer, and AWS Global Accelerator), but they may introduce unnecessary complexity for a static web application. Option B is a simpler and more direct solution for hosting static websites in S3.

question.en:
A company manages its multi-account environment by using AWS Organizations. The company needs to automate the creation of daily incremental backups of any Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume that is marked with a Lifecycle: Production tag in one of its primary AWS accounts.

The company wants to prevent users from using Amazon EC2 * permissions to delete any of these production snapshots.

What should a SysOps administrator do to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業が、AWS Organizationsを使用してマルチアカウント環境を管理しています。この企業は、主要なAWSアカウントの1つでLifecycle: Productionタグが付けられたAmazon Elastic Block Store（Amazon EBS）ボリュームについて、日次の増分バックアップの作成を自動化する必要があります。

この企業は、ユーザーがAmazon EC2の「*」許可を使用して、これらの本番用スナップショットを削除できないようにしたいと考えています。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者は何を行うべきですか。

text.en: Create a daily snapshot of all EBS volumes by using Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager. Specify Lifecycle as the tag key. Specify Production as the tag value.
text.ja: Amazon Data Lifecycle Managerを使用して、すべてのEBSボリュームの日次スナップショットを作成する。タグキーとしてLifecycleを指定し、タグ値としてProductionを指定する。

text.en: Associate a service control policy (SCP) with the account to deny users the ability to delete EBS snapshots. Create an Amazon EventBridge rule with a 24-hour cron schedule. Configure EBS Create Snapshot as the target. Target all EBS volumes with the specified tags.
text.ja: アカウントにサービスコントロールポリシー（SCP）を関連付けて、ユーザーがEBSスナップショットを削除できないようにする。24時間のcronスケジュールでAmazon EventBridgeルールを作成する。ターゲットとしてEBSのCreate Snapshotを設定する。指定されたタグを持つすべてのEBSボリュームをターゲットにする。

text.en: Create a daily snapshot of all EBS volumes by using AWS Backup. Specify Lifecycle as the tag key. Specify Production as the tag value.
text.ja: AWS Backupを使用して、すべてのEBSボリュームの日次スナップショットを作成する。タグキーとしてLifecycleを指定し、タグ値としてProductionを指定する。

text.en: Create a daily Amazon Machine Image (AMI) of every production EC2 instance within the AWS account by using Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager.
text.ja: Amazon Data Lifecycle Managerを使用して、AWSアカウント内のすべての本番EC2インスタンスの日次Amazon Machine Image（AMI）を作成する。

comment.en: I'm gonna go against the grain here and go with answer B. While initially it looks like an elephant because of its complexity compared to the other options...it does satisfy every requirement and its the ONLY option that satisfies the stipulation that denys the deletion of a resource.

I think answer is "C" because using AWS backup is the established method/tool for this, and EC2 instance role/profile would not be allow to control or delete backups unless explicitly allowed. The word "cron" in en answer "B" is red flag that is is the wrong answer. I know all about cron, and it's invaluable and bullet proof on a system. However, it's anathema to AWS Cloud way of doing things. Wherever you see the word "cron" in AWS response, you know its the wrong answer (IMHO).

The question mentioned the solution requirements are: 1. Automated EBS backups 2. Incremental EBS backups 3. Lifecycle policy 4. Prevent deletion by users (with Permission to delete snapshots) AWS Backup is smarter than DLM, they both automatically create AMIs and Incremental Snapshots. However, AWS BACKUP comes with features to manage access to the created AMIs and snapshots, and protect them from deletion with a vault lock. https://repost.aws/questions/QU8o5m88yhQk6UVAgQA3Mnng/aws-backup-vs-aws-data-lifecycle-management.
Exactly that, letter A does not resolve the stipulation of denying the deletion of snapshots, just resolve the problem of creating automated daily basis snapshots.
Agreed I will go with B. It covers all the requirements needed.
Doesn't prevent users from using Amazon EC2 * permissions to delete snapshots still.
Aws backup fulfills all needs. So C

For me the answer has to be C. Cron is usually a red herring that it's the wrong answer. Whilst B makes sense in the fact that it's using SCP controls alongside organizations, Cron just feels like bad practice in the context of AWS exams. Consider in backup controls that you are not able to delete unless specifically allowed and to really prevent deletion at an organizational level you can apply compliance mode which after a cooling off period, will not allow any users to delete the resource: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-backup/latest/devguide/vault-lock.html#:~:text=AWS%20Backup%20ensures%20that%20your%20backups%20are%20available,locked%20vault%2C%20AWS%20Backup%20will%20deny%20the%20operation.
Altough not elegant, its the only option that fulfils `prevent deletion` requirement
bbbbbbbbb
It is also the one option that mentions the SCP. Remember that the accounts are in AWS Organizations.
Answer:C

question.en:
A company hosts a Windows-based file server on a fleet of Amazon EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones. The current setup does not allow application servers to access files simultaneously from the EC2 fleet.

Which solution will allow this access in the MOST operationally efficient way?
question.ja:
ある企業が、複数のアベイラビリティーゾーンにわたるAmazon EC2インスタンスのフリート上で、Windowsベースのファイルサーバーをホストしています。現在の構成では、アプリケーションサーバーがEC2フリートから同時にファイルへアクセスすることができません。

このアクセスを最も運用効率の良い方法で実現するソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) Multi-AZ file system. Copy the files to the EFS file system. Connect the EFS file system to mount points on the application servers.
text.ja: Amazon Elastic File System（Amazon EFS）のマルチAZファイルシステムを作成する。ファイルをEFSファイルシステムにコピーする。EFSファイルシステムをアプリケーションサーバーのマウントポイントに接続する。

text.en: Create an Amazon FSx for Windows File Server Multi-AZ file system. Copy the files to the Amazon FSx file system. Adjust the connections from the application servers to use the share that the Amazon FSx file system exposes.
text.ja: Amazon FSx for Windows File Serverのマルチazファイルシステムを作成する。ファイルをAmazon FSxファイルシステムにコピーする。アプリケーションサーバーからの接続を、Amazon FSxファイルシステムが公開する共有を使用するように調整する。

text.en: Create an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume that has EBS Multi-Attach enabled. Create an Auto Scaling group for the Windows file server. Use a script in the file server's user data to attach the SharedFileAccess tag to the EBS volume during launch.
text.ja: EBS Multi-Attachを有効にしたAmazon Elastic Block Store（Amazon EBS）ボリュームを作成する。Windowsファイルサーバー用のAuto Scalingグループを作成する。ファイルサーバーのユーザーデータ内のスクリプトを使用して、起動時にSharedFileAccessタグをEBSボリュームにアタッチする。

text.en: Create two Amazon FSx for Windows File Server file systems. Configure Distributed File System (DFS) replication between the file systems. Copy the files to the Amazon FSx file systems. Adjust the connections from the application servers to use the shares that the Amazon FSx file systems expose.
text.ja: 2つのAmazon FSx for Windows File Serverファイルシステムを作成する。ファイルシステム間でDistributed File System（DFS）レプリケーションを設定する。ファイルをAmazon FSxファイルシステムにコピーする。アプリケーションサーバーからの接続を、Amazon FSxファイルシステムが公開する共有を使用するように調整する。

comment.en: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/fsx/latest/WindowsGuide/what-is.html
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/fsx/latest/WindowsGuide/high-availability-multiAZ.html

A is incorrect because talks about EFS in general. C is incorrect because EBS Multi-Attach only works for instances in the same AZ. D is incorrect because Distributed File System (DFS) replication applies only for regions. B is the correct answer. https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/storage/how-to-replicate-amazon-fsx-file-server-data-across-aws-regions/ https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/storage/delivering-instant-data-sharing-with-multi-attach-enabled-amazon-ebs/

Options A and B involve using Amazon EFS and Amazon FSx for Windows File Server, respectively, but the DFS replication for synchronization between file systems is a key feature for simultaneous access in multiple Availability Zones, making option D more suitable for the specified requirements.

question.en:
A company has deployed an application on Amazon EC2 instances in a single VPC. The company has placed the EC2 instances in a private subnet in the VPC.

The EC2 instances need access to Amazon S3 buckets that are in the same AWS Region as the EC2 instances. A SysOps administrator must provide the EC2 instances with access to the S3 buckets without requiring any changes to the EC2 instances or the application. The EC2 instances must not have access to the internet.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業が、単一のVPC内のAmazon EC2インスタンス上にアプリケーションをデプロイしています。この企業はEC2インスタンスをVPC内のプライベートサブネットに配置しています。

このEC2インスタンスは、そのEC2インスタンスと同じAWSリージョンにあるAmazon S3バケットへのアクセスが必要です。SysOps管理者は、EC2インスタンスやアプリケーションに変更を加えずに、EC2インスタンスがS3バケットへアクセスできるようにする必要があります。EC2インスタンスはインターネットへアクセスできてはいけません。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an S3 gateway endpoint that uses the default gateway endpoint policy. Associate the private subnet with the gateway endpoint.
text.ja: デフォルトのゲートウェイエンドポイントポリシーを使用するS3ゲートウェイエンドポイントを作成する。プライベートサブネットをそのゲートウェイエンドポイントに関連付ける。

text.en: Create an S3 interface endpoint. Associate the EC2 instances with the interface endpoint.
text.ja: S3インターフェイスエンドポイントを作成する。EC2インスタンスをそのインターフェイスエンドポイントに関連付ける。

text.en: Configure a NAT gateway. Associate the private subnet with the NAT gateway.
text.ja: NATゲートウェイを設定する。プライベートサブネットをそのNATゲートウェイに関連付ける。

text.en: Configure a proxy EC2 instance. Update the private subnet route tables to route traffic through the proxy EC2 instance. Configure the proxy to route all S3 requests to the target S3 bucket.
text.ja: プロキシ用のEC2インスタンスを設定する。プライベートサブネットのルートテーブルを更新して、トラフィックがそのプロキシEC2インスタンスを経由するようにする。すべてのS3リクエストが対象のS3バケットへルーティングされるようにプロキシを設定する。

comment.en: Create an S3 gateway endpoint that uses the default gateway endpoint policy. Associate the private subnet with the gateway endpoint. Notes: Amazon S3 supports both gateway endpoints and interface endpoints. With a gateway endpoint, you can access Amazon S3 from your VPC, without requiring an internet gateway or NAT device for your VPC, and with no additional cost. However, gateway endpoints do not allow access from on-premises networks, from peered VPCs in other AWS Regions, or through a transit gateway. For those scenarios, you must use an interface endpoint, which is available for an additional cost. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/vpc-endpoints-s3.html Interface endpoints - These endpoints are directly accessible from applications that are on premises over VPN and AWS Direct Connect, or in a different AWS Region over VPC peering. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/privatelink-interface-endpoints.html
same s3 region - use gateway endpoint another s3 region - use interface endpoint https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/choosing-your-vpc-endpoint-strategy-for-amazon-s3/

S3 Interface Endpoint (Gateway VPC Endpoint): This allows communication between resources in your VPC and Amazon S3, without relying on internet access. Interface endpoints are powered by AWS PrivateLink and provide a secure connection over the AWS global network. It does not require a NAT gateway or a proxy EC2 instance. Private Subnet Association: By associating the EC2 instances with the interface endpoint, you enable them to communicate with S3 securely without internet access.

I think both A and B could provide EC2 access to S3. However, I found gateway endpoint is only associated with a VPC, not a subnet. The Interface Endpoint is associated with a subnet. Secondly, I don't think the default endpoint policy is sufficient. "ensure that your endpoint policy allows the AWS service or resource to access these buckets using the s3:GetObject action" To me, the answer has to be "B". Its a tough question (at least for me) https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/vpc-endpoints-s3.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/privatelink-interface-endpoints.html#types-of-vpc-endpoints-for-s3
A The EC2 instances must not have access to the internet, so gateway and point

Gateway Endpoint: Free to use Interface Endpoint: $0.01 per hour per Availability Zone (AZ) with additional charges of $0.01 per GB of data transferred (depending on region) Connectivity: Gateway Endpoint: Limited to access within the same region and VPC. Does not allow access from on-premises networks, peered VPCs in other regions, or through a transit gateway. Interface Endpoint: More flexible, allowing access from your VPC, peered VPCs in other regions, on-premises networks, and through a transit gateway. Use Cases: Gateway Endpoint: Ideal for simple setups within a single region where cost is a major concern. Interface Endpoint: Better suited for complex network architectures, cross-region access, or when you need to connect to other AWS services besides S3.
Gateway Endpoint is the right answer. Interface Endpoints offer more connectivity options at much higher cost!

A This option, utilizing an S3 gateway endpoint, is designed for secure and private communication between a VPC and S3 over Direct Connect or VPN connections without requiring changes to the instances or applications. It allows EC2 instances in the private subnet to access S3 securely without using the public internet and does not require modifications to the instances or applications. Option B (S3 interface endpoint) is also a valid choice, but it's typically used for private VPC-to-S3 communication within the AWS network. However, it still requires creating an interface endpoint and associating EC2 instances with it.
A makes more sense

The solution that meets these requirements is option B. In option B, you would create an S3 interface endpoint and associate the EC2 instance with that interface endpoint. The S3 interface endpoint provides a private connection for accessing the S3 bucket directly from within the VPC. This solution allows the EC2 instance to access the S3 bucket without requiring internet access. Option A suggests using an S3 gateway endpoint with a default gateway endpoint policy. While the gateway endpoint provides a private connection for S3 object operations, it does not provide direct file system-level access from the EC2 instance.

Answer is A. Gateway endpoint purpose is to provide access to S3 without giving access to the internet. https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=&cad=rja&uact=8&ved=2ahUKEwjlmd3xgNP_AhXSSDABHeCrDTwQFnoECBgQAQ&url=https%3A%2F%2Fdocs.aws.amazon.com%2Fvpc%2Flatest%2Fprivatelink%2Fvpc-endpoints-s3.html&usg=AOvVaw0FB63FuHzsLuHYlvNgB-rW&opi=89978449

While I agree that you are right about the use of the default endpoint policy not being sufficient and kinda is throwing me off as well. I am still going to go with A because it states they want no change to the EC2 instances. A gateway endpoint is done with a route entry

question.en:
A company has a public web application that experiences rapid traffic increases after advertisements appear on local television. The application runs on Amazon EC2 instances that are in an Auto Scaling group. The Auto Scaling group is not keeping up with the traffic surges after an advertisement runs. The company often needs to scale out to 100 EC2 instances during the traffic surges.

The instance startup times are lengthy because of a boot process that creates machine-specific data caches that are unique to each instance. The exact timing of when the advertisements will appear on television is not known. A SysOps administrator must implement a solution so that the application can function properly during the traffic surges.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業が、地元テレビの広告が流れた後にトラフィックが急増するパブリックなウェブアプリケーションを運用しています。このアプリケーションは、Auto Scalingグループに属するAmazon EC2インスタンス上で稼働しています。このAuto Scalingグループは、広告放映後のトラフィック急増に追従できていません。この企業は、トラフィック急増時にしばしば100台のEC2インスタンスまでスケールアウトする必要があります。

各インスタンスに固有のマシン固有データキャッシュを作成する起動プロセスがあるため、インスタンスの起動時間が長くかかります。広告がいつテレビに放映されるかの正確なタイミングはわかっていません。SysOps管理者は、トラフィック急増時にアプリケーションが正常に機能できるようにするソリューションを実装する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create e warm pool. Keep enough instances in the Stopped state to meet the increased demand.
text.ja: ウォームプールを作成する。増大した需要に対応できるだけの数のインスタンスを停止（Stopped）状態で保持しておく。

text.en: Start 100 instances. Allow the boot process to finish running. Store this data on the instance store volume before stopping the instances.
text.ja: 100台のインスタンスを起動する。起動プロセスが完了するまで待つ。インスタンスを停止する前に、そのデータをインスタンスストアボリュームに保存する。

text.en: Increase the value of the instance warmup time in the scaling policy
text.ja: スケーリングポリシーのインスタンスウォームアップ時間の値を増やす。

text.en: Use predictive scaling for the Auto Scaling group.
text.ja: Auto Scalingグループに予測スケーリング（predictive scaling）を使用する。

comment.en: "A warm pool gives you the ability to decrease latency for your applications that have exceptionally long boot times, for example, because instances need to write massive amounts of data to disk" https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/ec2-auto-scaling-warm-pools.html I'm confident the answer is not "D" because they the requirements state "The exact timing of when the advertisements will appear on television is not known." That was put there to eliminate Predictive Scaling as an option.
Warm pool are perfect for boot long time. Predictive you need specific time to escalate, you need PATTERNS, in this question, specific time is UNKNOWN
I think here we need a Warm pool

Good catch, however I found this: Predictive scaling is well suited for situations where you have: - Cyclical traffic, such as high use of resources during regular business hours and low use of resources during evenings and weekends - Recurring on-and-off workload patterns, such as batch processing, testing, or periodic data analysis - Applications that take a long time to initialize, causing a noticeable latency impact on application performance during scale-out events In general, if you have regular patterns of traffic increases and applications that take a long time to initialize, you should consider using predictive scaling.
I think because "The exact timing of when the advertisements will appear on television is not known" so it's difficult to use predictive scaling.
definitely A

I feel like it is D. It's predictive, not scheduled. The part that is throwing me off for the warm pool is "The instance startup times are lengthy because of a boot process that creates machine-specific data caches that are unique to each instance". Each instance is unique. Wouldn't having an established warm pool of instances prevent that uniqueness?

question.en:
A company hosts an internal application on Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). The instances are in an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group. Employees use the application to provide product prices to potential customers. The Auto Scaling group is configured with a dynamic scaling policy and tracks average CPU utilization of the instances.

Employees have noticed that sometimes the application becomes slow or unresponsive. A SysOps administrator finds that some instances are experiencing a high CPU load. The Auto Scaling group cannot scale out because the company is reaching the EC2 instance service quota.

The SysOps administrator needs to implement a solution that provides a notification when the company reaches 70% or more of the EC2 instance service quota.

Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST operationally efficient manner?
question.ja:
ある企業が、Application Load Balancer（ALB）の背後にあるAmazon EC2オンデマンドインスタンス上で社内アプリケーションをホストしています。これらのインスタンスはAmazon EC2 Auto Scalingグループに属しています。従業員はこのアプリケーションを使用して、潜在的な顧客へ製品価格を提供しています。このAuto Scalingグループは、インスタンスの平均CPU使用率を追跡する動的スケーリングポリシーで設定されています。

従業員は、アプリケーションが時々遅くなったり応答しなくなったりすることに気づいています。SysOps管理者は、一部のインスタンスで高いCPU負荷が発生していることを発見しました。この企業がEC2インスタンスのサービスクォータに達しているため、Auto Scalingグループはスケールアウトできません。

SysOps管理者は、企業がEC2インスタンスのサービスクォータの70%以上に達したときに通知を提供するソリューションを実装する必要があります。

これらの要件を最も運用効率の良い方法で満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function that lists the EC2 instances, counts the EC2 instances, and compares the total number against the applied quota value by using the Service Quotas API. Configure the Lambda function to publish an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) notification if the quota utilization is equal to or greater than 70%. Create an Amazon EventBridge rule to invoke the Lambda function.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスをリストし、その数をカウントして、Service Quotas APIを使用して適用されているクォータ値と比較するAWS Lambda関数を作成する。クォータ使用率が70%以上の場合にAmazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）通知を発行するようにLambda関数を設定する。そのLambda関数を呼び出すAmazon EventBridgeルールを作成する。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function that lists the EC2 instances, counts the EC2 instances, and compares the total number against the applied quota value by using the Amazon CloudWatch Metrics API. Configure the Lambda function to publish an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) notification if the quota utilization is equal to or greater than 70%. Create an Amazon EventBridge rule to invoke the Lambda function.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスをリストし、その数をカウントして、Amazon CloudWatch Metrics APIを使用して適用されているクォータ値と比較するAWS Lambda関数を作成する。クォータ使用率が70%以上の場合にAmazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）通知を発行するようにLambda関数を設定する。そのLambda関数を呼び出すAmazon EventBridgeルールを作成する。

text.en: Use the Service Quotas console to create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm for the EC2 instances. Configure the alarm with quota utilization equal to or greater than 70%. Configure the alarm to publish an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) notification when the alarm enters ALARM state.
text.ja: Service Quotasコンソールを使用して、EC2インスタンス用のAmazon CloudWatchアラームを作成する。クォータ使用率が70%以上のときにアラームが発生するように設定する。アラームがALARM状態になったときにAmazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）通知を発行するように設定する。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm. Configure the alarm with a threshold of 70% for the CPUUtilization metric for the EC2 instances. Configure the alarm to publish an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) notification when the alarm enters ALARM state.
text.ja: Amazon CloudWatchアラームを作成する。EC2インスタンスのCPUUtilizationメトリクスに対して70%のしきい値を設定する。アラームがALARM状態になったときにAmazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）通知を発行するように設定する。

comment.en: Just a comment on this: a question made to verify if you are paying attention, not your knowledge on aws.
C - sounds more like it. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicequotas/latest/userguide/configure-cloudwatch.html

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicequotas/latest/userguide/configure-cloudwatch.html You can create Amazon CloudWatch alarms to notify you when you're close to a quota value threshold. Setting an alarm can help alert you if you need to request a quota increase. To create a CloudWatch alarm for a quota Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the Service Quotas console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/servicequotas/home. In the navigation pane, choose AWS services and then select a service. Select a quota that supports CloudWatch alarms. If you actively use the quota, utilization appears beneath the quota description. If CloudWatch alarms are supported, the CloudWatch alarms section appears at the bottom of the page. In Amazon CloudWatch alarms, choose Create. For Alarm threshold, choose a threshold. For Alarm name, enter a name for the alarm. This name must be unique within the AWS account. Choose Create.

D sounds terrible tbh

question.en:
A SysOps administrator needs to update an AWS account name.

What should the SysOps administrator do to accomplish this goal?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者がAWSアカウント名を更新する必要があります。

この目標を達成するために、SysOps管理者は何を行うべきですか。

text.en: Add the AdministratorAccess policy to the SysOps administrator’s IAM user.
text.ja: SysOps管理者のIAMユーザーにAdministratorAccessポリシーを追加する。

text.en: Add the AWS_ConfigureRole policy to the SysOps administrator’s IAM user.
text.ja: SysOps管理者のIAMユーザーにAWS_ConfigureRoleポリシーを追加する。

text.en: Change the AWS account name through the AWS Trusted Advisor interface.
text.ja: AWS Trusted Advisorのインターフェースを通じてAWSアカウント名を変更する。

text.en: Sign in as the AWS account root user to make the change.
text.ja: AWSアカウントのルートユーザーとしてサインインし、変更を行う。

comment.en: "To edit your AWS account name, root user password, or root user email address" "Minimum permissions" "To perform the following steps, you must have at least the following IAM permissions:" "You must sign in as the AWS account root user, which requires no additional IAM permissions. You can't perform these steps as an IAM user or role." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/accounts/latest/reference/manage-acct-update-root-user.html
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/accounts/latest/reference/manage-acct-update-root-user.html
The SysOps admin would have to sign is as root for this task.

question.en:
A company has a policy that all Amazon EC2 instance logs must be published to Amazon CloudWatch Logs. A SysOps administrator is troubleshooting an EC2 instance that is running Amazon Linux 2. The EC2 instance is not publishing logs to CloudWatch Logs. The Amazon CloudWatch agent is running on the EC2 instance, and the agent configuration file is correct.

What should the SysOps administrator do to resolve the issue?
question.ja:
ある企業には、すべてのAmazon EC2インスタンスのログをAmazon CloudWatch Logsに発行しなければならないというポリシーがあります。SysOps管理者は、Amazon Linux 2を実行しているEC2インスタンスのトラブルシューティングを行っています。そのEC2インスタンスはCloudWatch Logsへログを発行していません。Amazon CloudWatchエージェントはそのEC2インスタンス上で稼働しており、エージェントの設定ファイルは正しい状態です。

この問題を解決するために、SysOps管理者は何を行うべきですか。

text.en: Configure the AWS CLI on the EC2 instance. Create a cron job that calls the PutLogEvents API operation to push the log files to CloudWatch every 5 minutes.
text.ja: EC2インスタンス上でAWS CLIを設定する。PutLogEvents API操作を呼び出して、5分ごとにログファイルをCloudWatchへプッシュするcronジョブを作成する。

text.en: Inspect the retention period of the CloudWatch Logs log group. Ensure that the retention period is set to a value that is greater than 1 day.
text.ja: CloudWatch Logsのロググループの保持期間を確認する。保持期間が1日より長い値に設定されていることを確認する。

text.en: Set up an Amazon Kinesis data stream that is running in the same AWS Region as the EC2 instance. Configure the CloudWatch agent on the EC2 instance to send CloudWatch events to the data stream.
text.ja: そのEC2インスタンスと同じAWSリージョンで稼働しているAmazon Kinesisデータストリームを設定する。EC2インスタンス上のCloudWatchエージェントを、CloudWatchイベントをそのデータストリームへ送信するように設定する。

text.en: Ensure that the IAM role that is attached to the EC2 instance has permissions in CloudWatch Logs for the CreateLogGroup, CreateLogStream, PutLogEvents, and DescribeLogStreams actions.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスにアタッチされているIAMロールが、CloudWatch LogsのCreateLogGroup、CreateLogStream、PutLogEvents、DescribeLogStreamsアクションに対する許可を持っていることを確認する。

comment.en: D is ok

D Amazon EC2 instances need the appropriate permissions to interact with Amazon CloudWatch Logs. These permissions are granted through an IAM (Identity and Access Management) role attached to the EC2 instance.
It's a permissions issue: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/iam-identity-based-access-control-cwl.html

question.en:
A company runs a workload on an Amazon EC2 instance. The workload needs a temporary cache that contains data that changes frequently. The workload does not need to retain the cache across instance restarts.

Which storage option will provide the HIGHEST performance for the cache?
question.ja:
ある企業がAmazon EC2インスタンス上でワークロードを実行しています。このワークロードには、頻繁に変化するデータを含む一時的なキャッシュが必要です。このワークロードでは、インスタンスの再起動をまたいでキャッシュを保持する必要はありません。

このキャッシュに最も高いパフォーマンスを提供するストレージオプションはどれですか。

text.en: General Purpose SSD (gp3) Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume
text.ja: General Purpose SSD (gp3) Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) ボリューム

text.en: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io2) Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume
text.ja: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io2) Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) ボリューム

text.en: Throughput Optimized HDD (st1) Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume
text.ja: Throughput Optimized HDD (st1) Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) ボリューム

text.en: EC2 instance store
text.ja: EC2インスタンスストア

comment.en: I'm sure the "Instance Store" volume is actually stored in local memory, which is why it is non-persistent but will also always have higher performance than any attached EBS volume.

The storage option that will provide the highest performance for the temporary cache is option D: EC2 instance store. EC2 instance store, also known as ephemeral storage, provides temporary block-level storage that is directly attached to the EC2 instance. It offers very high performance and low latency since it is physically attached to the instance's hardware. The performance of instance store volumes is often better than any other storage option. However, it's important to note that EC2 instance store is temporary storage and is not persistent. The data stored on instance store volumes is lost if the instance is stopped or terminated. If data retention is not required across instance restarts, using instance store as a cache can provide the highest performance.
D. instance store provides highest performance
I'm sure the "Instance Store" volume is actually stored in local memory, which is why it is non-persistent but will also always have higher performance than any attached EBS volume.
Should be D, since the workload doesn't need to retain the cache https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/InstanceStorage.html

question.en:
A SysOps administrator manages an AWS account where developers run CPU-intensive tasks on Amazon EC2 instances. The tasks can take several days to finish running and sometimes need to be repeated several times. The developers often forget to terminate the instances when the tasks are complete.

The SysOps administrator needs to implement a solution to monitor EC2 CPU utilization and automatically terminate underutilized instances.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
あるSysOps管理者は、開発者がAmazon EC2インスタンス上でCPU負荷の高いタスクを実行するAWSアカウントを管理しています。これらのタスクは完了までに数日かかることがあり、また何度も繰り返す必要がある場合があります。開発者はタスク完了後にインスタンスを終了させることをよく忘れてしまいます。

SysOps管理者は、EC2のCPU使用率を監視し、使用率の低いインスタンスを自動的に終了させるソリューションを実装する必要があります。

どのソリューションがこれらの要件を満たしますか。

text.en: Configure an Amazon GuardDuty finding that is based on EC2 CPU utilization. Associate an AWS Lambda function with the GuardDuty finding to terminate any instances that are identified as idle.
text.ja: EC2のCPU使用率に基づくAmazon GuardDutyの検出結果を設定する。GuardDutyの検出結果にAWS Lambda関数を関連付け、アイドル状態と識別されたインスタンスを終了させる。

text.en: Configure an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to receive EC2 utilization messages from the AWS Health Dashboard. Create an AWS Lambda function. Subscribe the Lambda function to the SNS topic. Use the ec2.stop_instances operation to terminate idle instances.
text.ja: AWS Health DashboardからEC2使用率メッセージを受信するAmazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) トピックを設定する。AWS Lambda関数を作成する。そのLambda関数をSNSトピックにサブスクライブする。ec2.stop_instances操作を使用してアイドル状態のインスタンスを終了させる。

text.en: Configure a Low Utilization Amazon EC2 Instances check in AWS Trusted Advisor to publish status changes to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic. Create an AWS Lambda function. Subscribe the Lambda function to the SNS topic. Use the ec2.stop_instances operation to terminate idle instances.
text.ja: AWS Trusted Advisorの「Low Utilization Amazon EC2 Instances」チェックを設定し、ステータス変更をAmazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) トピックにパブリッシュさせる。AWS Lambda関数を作成する。そのLambda関数をSNSトピックにサブスクライブする。ec2.stop_instances操作を使用してアイドル状態のインスタンスを終了させる。

text.en: Configure an Amazon EventBridge rule for the Low Utilization Amazon EC2 Instances check in AWS Trusted Advisor. Select the EC2 Terminatelnstances API call as the target.
text.ja: AWS Trusted Advisorの「Low Utilization Amazon EC2 Instances」チェックに対するAmazon EventBridgeルールを設定する。ターゲットとしてEC2のTerminateInstances API呼び出しを選択する。

comment.en: I concur with D based on the link. I know there other/better ways to do this without trusted advisor, but they are not listed in the choices. The other choices are essentially convoluted gibberish. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/cloudwatch-events-ta.html
Voting for D, same result simpler solution

A. No, Guarduty is more focused on security monitoring and thread detection B. No, Healthcheck doesn't have CPU utilization data C. Trusted adviso desn't provide automated check on CPU D. it is correct. Another solution coul be to trigger with an Event Bridge Rule from a CW Alarm to trigger an SSM Document that run a script that can chec if CPU is still under the threshold and in case delete the EC2 (could be even 2 script)

Options A, B, and D do not utilize AWS Trusted Advisor and introduce additional complexities that might not be necessary for this specific requirement. Trusted Advisor provides specific checks and recommendations for cost optimization, making it a suitable tool for identifying underutilized instances.

question.en:
A company decides to stop non-production Amazon EC2 instances during the EC2 instances. The company's IT manager must receive notification in near real time whenever an EC2 instance that has an environment type tag value of non-production is started during the night.

Which solution will meet this requirement with the MOST operational efficiency?
question.ja:
ある企業は、EC2インスタンスの夜間に非本番のAmazon EC2インスタンスを停止することを決定しました。同社のITマネージャーは、環境タイプタグの値が「non-production」であるEC2インスタンスが夜間に起動された場合、ほぼリアルタイムで通知を受け取る必要があります。

最も高い運用効率でこの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Configure an AWS Lambda function with an SMTP client library. Subscribe the Lambda function to the AWS Health Dashboard to receive notification whenever an EC2 instance is in the running state. Configure the Lambda function to use Amazon Pinpoint to send email notifications to the IT manager. Deploy a second Lambda function to throttle calls from the first Lambda function during the daytime.
text.ja: SMTPクライアントライブラリを備えたAWS Lambda関数を設定する。そのLambda関数をAWS Health Dashboardにサブスクライブし、EC2インスタンスが実行状態になったときに通知を受け取る。Amazon Pinpointを使用してITマネージャーにメール通知を送信するようLambda関数を設定する。日中に最初のLambda関数からの呼び出しをスロットリングする2つ目のLambda関数をデプロイする。

text.en: Deploy an AWS Lambda function that queries the Amazon EC2 API to determine the state of each EC2 instance. Use the EC2 instance scheduler to configure the Lambda function to run every minute during the night and to send an email notification to the IT manager for each non-production EC2 instance that is in the running state.
text.ja: Amazon EC2 APIをクエリして各EC2インスタンスの状態を判定するAWS Lambda関数をデプロイする。EC2インスタンススケジューラを使用して、夜間は毎分そのLambda関数を実行し、実行状態にある非本番のEC2インスタンスごとにITマネージャーへメール通知を送信するように設定する。

text.en: Create an Amazon EventBridge rule that includes the EC2 Instance State-change Notification event type. Filter the event to capture only the running state. Create an AWS Lambda function as a target of the rule. Configure the Lambda function to check the current time and the EC2 instances’ tags to determine the environment type. Create an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic as a target of the Lambda function for notifications. Subscribe the IT manager's email address to the SNS topic.
text.ja: EC2 Instance State-change Notificationイベントタイプを含むAmazon EventBridgeルールを作成する。runningステートのみを対象としてイベントをフィルタする。ルールのターゲットとしてAWS Lambda関数を作成する。現在時刻とEC2インスタンスのタグを確認して環境タイプを判定するようにそのLambda関数を設定する。通知用にLambda関数のターゲットとしてAmazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) トピックを作成する。ITマネージャーのメールアドレスをそのSNSトピックにサブスクライブする。

text.en: Store the EC2 instance metadata, including the environment type, in an Amazon DynamoDB table. Deploy a custom application to an EC2 instance. Configure the custom application to poll the DynamoDB data every minute during the night and to query the Amazon EC2 API to determine the state of each instance. Additionally, configure the custom application to send an email notification to the IT manager for each non-production EC2 instance that is in the running state.
text.ja: 環境タイプを含むEC2インスタンスのメタデータをAmazon DynamoDBテーブルに保存する。カスタムアプリケーションをEC2インスタンスにデプロイする。夜間は毎分DynamoDBのデータをポーリングし、Amazon EC2 APIをクエリして各インスタンスの状態を判定するようにカスタムアプリケーションを設定する。さらに、実行状態にある非本番のEC2インスタンスごとにITマネージャーへメール通知を送信するようにカスタムアプリケーションを設定する。

comment.en: C seems less convoluted than the other options.
The question has a typo.

C Options A and B involve continuously running Lambda functions or custom applications that poll the EC2 instances, which is less efficient and cost-effective than event-driven solutions. Option D involves storing EC2 instance metadata in DynamoDB and continuously polling, which is also less efficient and more complex than using event-driven services like EventBridge and Lambda.

Amazon EventBridge provides near real-time event processing. It enables you to build event-driven architectures and applications by ingesting and processing events from various sources in near real-time.

question.en:
A company’s SysOps administrator manages a fleet of Windows Amazon EC2 instances that run in a single AWS account. The instances have a tag that includes a key of “OS" and a value of "Windows." The company uses AWS Systems Manager to patch the instances.

The company has installed the Amazon CloudWatch agent on the instances, but the configuration is inconsistent. The SysOps administrator needs to reconfigure every instance to use the same predefined CloudWatch configuration.

Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある企業のSysOps管理者は、単一のAWSアカウントで実行されているWindowsのAmazon EC2インスタンス群を管理しています。これらのインスタンスには、キーが「OS」で値が「Windows」のタグが付いています。同社はAWS Systems Managerを使用してインスタンスにパッチを適用しています。

同社はインスタンスにAmazon CloudWatchエージェントをインストールしましたが、設定に一貫性がありません。SysOps管理者は、すべてのインスタンスを同じ事前定義済みのCloudWatch設定を使用するように再設定する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たす手順の組み合わせはどれですか。（2つ選択してください）

text.en: Store the CloudWatch agent configuration file in an Amazon S3 bucket.
text.ja: CloudWatchエージェントの設定ファイルをAmazon S3バケットに保存する。

text.en: Store the contents of the CloudWatch agent configuration file in Systems Manager OpsCenter.
text.ja: CloudWatchエージェントの設定ファイルの内容をSystems Manager OpsCenterに保存する。

text.en: Store the contents of the CloudWatch agent configuration file in Systems Manager Parameter Store.
text.ja: CloudWatchエージェントの設定ファイルの内容をSystems Manager Parameter Storeに保存する。

text.en: Create a Systems Manager State Manager association to run the AmazonCloudWatch-ManageAgent Systems Manager Run Command document. Select Systems Manager as an optional configuration source. Target the instances based on tag values.
text.ja: AmazonCloudWatch-ManageAgentのSystems Manager Run Commandドキュメントを実行するSystems Manager State Manager関連付けを作成する。オプションの設定ソースとしてSystems Managerを選択する。タグ値に基づいてインスタンスをターゲットにする。

text.en: Create a Systems Manager State Manager association to run the AmazonCloudWatch-ManageAgent Systems Manager Run Command document. Configure the document to use the S3 bucket location as the configuration source. Target the instances based on tag value.
text.ja: AmazonCloudWatch-ManageAgentのSystems Manager Run Commandドキュメントを実行するSystems Manager State Manager関連付けを作成する。設定ソースとしてS3バケットの場所を使用するようにドキュメントを設定する。タグ値に基づいてインスタンスをターゲットにする。

comment.en: I don't know if S3 option works, but I do know AWS documents using Parameter Store to store a CloudWatch agent configuration file, and to start the agent using the config stored in Parameter Store, therefore I know C & D are valid. Here are references: "Uploading the CloudWatch agent configuration file to Systems Manager Parameter Store" https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/CloudWatch-Agent-Configuration-File-Details.html Command to start CloudWatch agent by loading configuration file previously saved in Systems Manager Parameter store: sudo /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl -a fetch-config -m ec2 -s -c ssm:configuration-parameter-store-name https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/install-CloudWatch-Agent-on-EC2-Instance-fleet.html
CD, used in prod already

Step A involves storing the CloudWatch agent configuration file in an Amazon S3 bucket. This allows for centralized storage of the configuration file, making it easily accessible. Step D involves creating a Systems Manager State Manager association to run the AmazonCloudWatch-ManageAgent Systems Manager Run Command document. By selecting Systems Manager as an optional configuration source and targeting the instances based on tag values, you can ensure that every instance with the specified tag (in this case, "OS" tag with a value of "Windows") will be reconfigured to use the predefined CloudWatch configuration. These steps ensure that the CloudWatch agent configuration is consistent across all Windows EC2 instances by leveraging Systems Manager and the predefined configuration stored in an S3 bucket. Therefore, the correct choices are A and D.

Assuming there the instances are deployed to different regions, then its better to store the configuration file in S3. Because storing it in Parameter store would require additional steps. "If you use multiple Regions, you must synchronize CloudWatch configuration updates in each Region's Parameter Store. Parameter Store is a Regional service and the same parameter must be updated in each Region that uses the CloudWatch agent." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/implementing-logging-monitoring-cloudwatch/create-store-cloudwatch-configurations.html

A&D Option B and Option C do not directly address the requirement to configure the CloudWatch agent with a consistent predefined configuration across all instances. Option E does not use the S3 bucket as the configuration source, which is typically the recommended way to manage CloudWatch agent configurations for consistency.
We deploy CloudWatch in our company almost everyday using options C and D. if you go with A then D doesn't make sense cuz the source says systems manager no S3...

Why Option A is Correct: Storing the CloudWatch agent configuration file in Amazon S3 makes it easily accessible to the EC2 instances. The S3 bucket acts as a centralized storage location for the predefined configuration file, ensuring all instances use the same version when updating their agent settings. Why Option E is Correct: Using AWS Systems Manager State Manager associations allows the administrator to ensure that every tagged instance consistently applies the same CloudWatch configuration. The AmazonCloudWatch-ManageAgent Run Command document can pull the agent configuration file from the S3 bucket and apply it across all targeted instances. Targeting based on tags (e.g., OS: Windows) ensures that only the Windows EC2 instances are updated.

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/implementing-logging-monitoring-cloudwatch/create-store-cloudwatch-configurations.html Single account in the scenario. Seems like central storage by way of S3 is not required.

question.en:
A SysOps administrator launches an Amazon EC2 instance in a private subnet of a VPC. When the SysOps administrator attempts a curl command from the command line of the EC2 instance, the SysOps administrator cannot connect to https:www.example.com.

What should the SysOps administrator do to resolve this issue?
question.ja:
あるSysOps管理者が、VPCのプライベートサブネットにAmazon EC2インスタンスを起動しました。そのSysOps管理者がEC2インスタンスのコマンドラインからcurlコマンドを実行したところ、https://www.example.comに接続できません。

この問題を解決するために、SysOps管理者は何をすべきですか。

text.en: Ensure that there is an outbound security group for port 443 to 0.0.0.0/0.
text.ja: ポート443の0.0.0.0/0へのアウトバウンドセキュリティグループが存在することを確認する。

text.en: Ensure that there is an inbound security group for port 443 from 0.0.0.0/0.
text.ja: ポート443の0.0.0.0/0からのインバウンドセキュリティグループが存在することを確認する。

text.en: Ensure that there is an outbound network ACL for ephemeral ports 1024-66535 to 0.0.0.0/0.
text.ja: エフェメラルポート1024〜66535の0.0.0.0/0へのアウトバウンドネットワークACLが存在することを確認する。

text.en: Ensure that there is an outbound network ACL for port 80 to 0.0.0.0/0.
text.ja: ポート80の0.0.0.0/0へのアウトバウンドネットワークACLが存在することを確認する。

comment.en: this is an utterly terrible question. that's it. that's my input.
Default sg allows for outbound.
A. Can' t be C because ephemeral ports 1024-65535 are not relevant to HTTPS communication.

C. Ensure that there is an outbound network ACL for ephemeral ports 1024-66535 to 0.0.0.0/0. Explanation: When an EC2 instance in a private subnet needs to access resources on the internet, such as https://www.example.com, it uses outbound connections. Outbound connections are controlled by the Network Access Control List (network ACL) associated with the subnet. Option C suggests ensuring that there is an outbound network ACL rule allowing traffic on ephemeral ports (1024-66535) to 0.0.0.0/0. Ephemeral ports are used as source ports for outbound connections, and allowing traffic from these ports is necessary for the EC2 instance to communicate with external resources. Options A and B involve security groups, but security groups are stateful and are applied to inbound and outbound traffic. In this case, the issue is likely related to the network ACL controlling outbound traffic from the subnet. Option D is not relevant to the issue. It mentions an outbound network ACL for port 80, but the issue is related to connecting to https://www.example.com, which uses port 443 for HTTPS.

WRONG, You are EC2 instance inside a private subnet, trying to go out to destination port 443. Option C only works IF someones connecting to your EC2 from outside and your NACL needs to allow access back to that user on ephemeral port. YOU DO NOT CHECK GO OUTBOUND ON EPHEMERAL PORT AS DESTINATION UNLESS you are the server. SG is the ONLY thing in this answer section that could be wrong. Answer is A

It's important to remember that security groups are stateful. So if you have an outbound rule in your security group for HTTPS and you send a HTTPS request from your instance to a remote web server, the instance will be able to receive the response, even though port 443 is not allowed by your inbound rule in the security group. When a connection is made to or from an instance, a "state" is created that allows bidirectional communication - but only for that connection.

I thing you are wrong https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/resolve-connection-sg-acl-inbound Short description Security groups are stateful, so allowing inbound traffic to the necessary ports activates the connection. Network ACLs are stateless, so you must allow both inbound and outbound traffic. Resolution To turn on the connection to a service running on an instance, the associated network ACL must allow the following: Inbound traffic on the port that the service is listening on Outbound traffic to ephemeral ports When a client connects to a server, a random port from the ephemeral port range (1024-65535) becomes the client's source port.
who wrote this question?

Inbound security group rules control incoming traffic to the EC2 instance, and outbound security group rules control outgoing traffic. For the EC2 instance to access external resources on the internet, including https://www.example.com, you need to ensure that there is an outbound security group rule allowing traffic on port 443 to the destination IP or CIDR range (0.0.0.0/0 in this case).
Going for C based on this: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-network-acls.html
A seems right option

it need to ensure that, on network acl level, the ephemeral port are open inbound, but for outbound the port 443 will be use. No one of this answer is enough to stisfy the requirements, but the only check that deserve to be done is A

question.en:
A SysOps administrator needs to implement a backup strategy for Amazon EC2 resources and Amazon RDS resources. The backup strategy must meet the following retention requirements:

• Daily backups: must be kept for 6 days
• Weekly backups: must be kept for 4 weeks:
• Monthly backups: must be kept for 11 months
• Yearly backups: must be kept for 7 years

Which backup strategy will meet these requirements with the LEAST administrative effort?
question.ja:
あるSysOps管理者は、Amazon EC2リソースとAmazon RDSリソースに対するバックアップ戦略を実装する必要があります。このバックアップ戦略は、次の保持要件を満たす必要があります。

・日次バックアップ：6日間保持する
・週次バックアップ：4週間保持する
・月次バックアップ：11か月間保持する
・年次バックアップ：7年間保持する

最も少ない運用負荷でこれらの要件を満たすバックアップ戦略はどれですか。

text.en: Use Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager to create an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) snapshot policy. Create tags on each resource that needs to be backed up. Create multiple schedules according to the requirements within the policy. Set the appropriate frequency and retention period.
text.ja: Amazon Data Lifecycle Managerを使用してAmazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) スナップショットポリシーを作成する。バックアップが必要な各リソースにタグを作成する。要件に従ってポリシー内に複数のスケジュールを作成する。適切な頻度と保持期間を設定する。

text.en: Use AWS Backup to create a new backup plan for each retention requirement with a backup frequency of daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly. Set the retention period to match the requirement. Create tags on each resource that needs to be backed up. Set up resource assignment by using the tags.
text.ja: AWS Backupを使用して、日次・週次・月次・年次のバックアップ頻度で保持要件ごとに新しいバックアッププランを作成する。要件に合わせて保持期間を設定する。バックアップが必要な各リソースにタグを作成する。そのタグを使用してリソース割り当てを設定する。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function. Program the Lambda function to use native tooling to take backups of file systems in Amazon EC2 and to make copies of databases in Amazon RDS. Create an Amazon EventBridge rule to invoke the Lambda function.
text.ja: AWS Lambda関数を作成する。ネイティブツールを使用してAmazon EC2のファイルシステムのバックアップを取得し、Amazon RDSのデータベースのコピーを作成するようにそのLambda関数をプログラムする。そのLambda関数を呼び出すAmazon EventBridgeルールを作成する。

text.en: Use Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager to create an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) snapshot policy. Create tags on each resource that needs to be backed up. Set up resource assignment by using the tags. Create multiple schedules according to the requirements within the policy. Set the appropriate frequency and retention period. In Amazon RDS, activate automated backups on the required DB instances.
text.ja: Amazon Data Lifecycle Managerを使用してAmazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) スナップショットポリシーを作成する。バックアップが必要な各リソースにタグを作成する。そのタグを使用してリソース割り当てを設定する。要件に従ってポリシー内に複数のスケジュールを作成する。適切な頻度と保持期間を設定する。Amazon RDSでは、必要なDBインスタンスで自動バックアップを有効化する。

comment.en: Has to be AWS Backup since "EBS snapshot" isn't mentioned in requirements. "Use Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager when you want to automate the creation, retention, and deletion of EBS snapshots. Use AWS Backup to manage and monitor backups across the AWS services you use, including EBS volumes, from a single plac" https://aws.amazon.com/backup/faqs/
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_WorkingWithAutomatedBackups.html
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/snapshot-lifecycle.html

question.en:
An application team uses an Amazon Aurora MySQL DB cluster with one Aurora Replica. The application team notices that the application read performance degrades when user connections exceed 200. The number of user connections is typically consistent around 180, with occasional sudden increases above 200 connections.
