The application team wants the application to automatically scale as user demand increases or decreases.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
あるアプリケーションチームは、1つのAurora Replicaを持つAmazon Aurora MySQL DBクラスターを使用しています。このアプリケーションチームは、ユーザー接続数が200を超えるとアプリケーションの読み取りパフォーマンスが低下することに気付きました。ユーザー接続数は通常180前後で安定していますが、時折200を超える急増が発生します。このアプリケーションチームは、ユーザー需要の増減に応じてアプリケーションが自動的にスケールするようにしたいと考えています。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Migrate to a new Aurora multi-master DB cluster. Modify the application database connection string.
text.ja: 新しいAuroraマルチマスターDBクラスターに移行する。アプリケーションのデータベース接続文字列を変更する。

text.en: Modify the DB cluster by changing to serverless mode whenever user connections exceed 200.
text.ja: ユーザー接続数が200を超えたときにサーバーレスモードに切り替えるようにDBクラスターを変更する。

text.en: Create an auto scaling policy with a target metric of 195 DatabaseConnections.
text.ja: 195のDatabaseConnectionsをターゲットメトリクスとするAuto Scalingポリシーを作成する。

text.en: Modify the DB cluster by increasing the Aurora Replica instance size.
text.ja: Aurora Replicaのインスタンスサイズを増やすようにDBクラスターを変更する。

comment.en: Aurora Auto Scaling "Policy details:" (*) "Average connections of Aurora Replicas", "Target value:" 195
just for have some safe space before reaching the 200.
why 195? can somebody explain?
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/Aurora.Integrating.AutoScaling.html

question.en:
A company hosts a production database on an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) backed Amazon EC2 instance. As part of an annual disaster recovery exercise, the company needs to restore recent EBS snapshots to a new EC2 instance in a second Availability Zone.

After the snapshots are restored to EBS volumes, the resulting volumes must deliver all of their provisioned performance. The company must perform validation tests on the restored data as quickly as possible.

Which configuration will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は、Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) をバックエンドとするAmazon EC2インスタンス上で本番データベースをホストしています。毎年実施している災害復旧演習の一環として、同社は最近のEBSスナップショットを、2つ目のアベイラビリティーゾーンにある新しいEC2インスタンスに復元する必要があります。

スナップショットがEBSボリュームに復元された後、結果として得られるボリュームはプロビジョニングされたパフォーマンスをすべて発揮できる必要があります。同社は、復元されたデータに対する検証テストをできるだけ早く実施する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たす設定はどれですか。

text.en: Enable EBS fast snapshot restore (FSR) on the snapshots for the second Availability Zone. Create new EBS volumes in the second Availability Zone from the snapshots. Attach the new EBS volumes to a new EC2 instance.
text.ja: 2つ目のアベイラビリティーゾーン用のスナップショットに対してEBS fast snapshot restore (FSR) を有効化する。そのスナップショットから2つ目のアベイラビリティーゾーンに新しいEBSボリュームを作成する。新しいEBSボリュームを新しいEC2インスタンスにアタッチする。

text.en: Enable EBS fast snapshot restore (FSR) on the snapshots for the current Availability Zone. Create new EBS volumes in the second Availability Zone from the snapshots, Attach the new EBS volumes to a new EC2 instance.
text.ja: 現在のアベイラビリティーゾーン用のスナップショットに対してEBS fast snapshot restore (FSR) を有効化する。そのスナップショットから2つ目のアベイラビリティーゾーンに新しいEBSボリュームを作成する。新しいEBSボリュームを新しいEC2インスタンスにアタッチする。

text.en: Specify Provisioned IOPS on the snapshots, Create new EBS volumes in the second Availability Zone from the snapshots. Attach the new EBS volumes to a new EC2 instance.
text.ja: スナップショットに対してProvisioned IOPSを指定する。そのスナップショットから2つ目のアベイラビリティーゾーンに新しいEBSボリュームを作成する。新しいEBSボリュームを新しいEC2インスタンスにアタッチする。

text.en: Specify Provisioned IOPS on the existing EBS volumes. Create the snapshots. After the snapshots are completed, create new EBS volumes in the second Availability Zone from the snapshots. Attach the new EBS volumes to a new EC2 instance.
text.ja: 既存のEBSボリュームに対してProvisioned IOPSを指定する。スナップショットを作成する。スナップショットの完了後、そのスナップショットから2つ目のアベイラビリティーゾーンに新しいEBSボリュームを作成する。新しいEBSボリュームを新しいEC2インスタンスにアタッチする。

comment.en: I think the point is that a fast snapshot restore must be enabled on the snapshot in same AZ where the new volume will be "To get started, enable fast snapshot restore for specific snapshots in specific Availability Zones. Each snapshot and Availability Zone pair refers to one fast snapshot restore." "When you create a volume from one of these snapshots in one of its enabled Availability Zones, the volume is restored using fast snapshot restore." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ebs-fast-snapshot-restore.html https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/new-amazon-ebs-fast-snapshot-restore-fsr/
Thanks Gomer for writing good responses to all these questions.

question.en:
A company’s financial department needs to view the cost details of each project in an AWS account. A SysOps administrator must perform the initial configuration that is required to view cost for each project in Cost Explorer.

Which solution will meet this requirement?
question.ja:
ある企業の財務部門は、AWSアカウント内の各プロジェクトのコスト詳細を確認する必要があります。SysOps管理者は、Cost Explorerで各プロジェクトのコストを表示するために必要な初期設定を行う必要があります。

この要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Activate cost allocation tags. Add a project tag to the appropriate resources.
text.ja: コスト配分タグを有効化する。該当するリソースにプロジェクトタグを追加する。

text.en: Configure consolidated billing. Create AWS Cost and Usage Reports.
text.ja: 統合請求を設定する。AWS Cost and Usage Reportsを作成する。

text.en: Use AWS Budgets. Create AWS Budgets reports.
text.ja: AWS Budgetsを使用する。AWS Budgetsレポートを作成する。

text.en: Use cost categories to define custom groups that are based on AWS cost and usage dimensions.
text.ja: コストカテゴリを使用して、AWSのコストおよび使用量のディメンションに基づいたカスタムグループを定義する。

comment.en: key word --> initial configuration So, Answer is A
A is correct
the answer is A - granularity

D is the correct answer. Cost categories in AWS Cost Explorer allow you to create custom groups based on AWS cost and usage dimensions such as services, linked accounts, tags, and more. By using cost categories, you can categorize your costs at a granular level, such as by project, and view cost details for each project in Cost Explorer. This enables the financial department to analyze and monitor costs specific to each project. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/billing-what-is.html Option A: Activating cost allocation tags and adding project tags to resources allows you to track costs based on tags, but it does not provide the granularity and flexibility of cost categories for viewing cost details per project.
A is the correct answer

AWS Cost Categories is a feature within AWS Cost Management product suite that enables you to group cost and usage information into meaningful categories based on your needs. AWS Cost Categories enables users to define category rules to map and categorize costs using billing dimensions such as accounts and tags. For example, customers can map specific accounts and tags to multiple projects and track costs based on them. https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2019/11/introducing-aws-cost-categories/
D doesn't emphasize the use of tags for associating costs with projects, which is a more straightforward approach.

question.en:
A SysOps administrator is managing a web application that runs on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). The instances run in an EC2 Auto Scaling group. The administrator wants to set an alarm for when all target instances associated with the ALB are unhealthy.

Which condition should be used with the alarm?
question.ja:
あるSysOps管理者は、Application Load Balancer (ALB) の背後にあるAmazon EC2インスタンス上で実行されるWebアプリケーションを管理しています。これらのインスタンスはEC2 Auto Scalingグループで実行されています。この管理者は、ALBに関連付けられているすべてのターゲットインスタンスが異常（unhealthy）になったときのアラームを設定したいと考えています。

このアラームで使用すべき条件はどれですか。

text.en: AWS/ApplicationELB HealthyHostCount <= 0
text.ja: AWS/ApplicationELB HealthyHostCount <= 0

text.en: AWS/ApplicationELB UnhealthyHostCount >= 1
text.ja: AWS/ApplicationELB UnhealthyHostCount >= 1

text.en: AWS/EC2 StatusCheckFailed <= 0
text.ja: AWS/EC2 StatusCheckFailed <= 0

text.en: AWS/EC2 StatusCheckFailed >= 1
text.ja: AWS/EC2 StatusCheckFailed >= 1

comment.en: "all target instances" means zero Healthy, not one Healthy. "<= 0" means less than or equal to zero

The question states: The administrator wants to set an alarm for when all target instances associated with the ALB are unhealthy. A. AWS/ApplicationELB HealthyHostCount <= 0 would trigger the Alarm because all the host are unhealthy. Option B: would trigger if one or more is unhealthy but you would still could have healthy host!

Some of these answers really show how badly people fail at reading comprehension or are waaay to reliant on Chat AI as their justifications for their answers completely contradict what the question asks for...
I concur. "all target instances" means zero Healthy, not one Healthy. "<= 0" means less than or equal to zero
healthy <= 0 means all are unhealthy
Option A will trigger the alarm when there are no healthy target instances. Option B will trigger the alarm when there is at least one unhealthy target instance registered with the ALB.

B. AWS/ApplicationELB UnhealthyHostCount >= 1 By using this condition, the alarm will be triggered when there is at least one unhealthy target instance associated with the Application Load Balancer (ALB).

Option B, "AWS/ApplicationELB UnhealthyHostCount >= 1," is the correct condition for triggering an alarm when at least one target instance associated with the ALB is unhealthy. This condition ensures that the alarm is triggered when the count of unhealthy instances is greater than or equal to 1. -- Options A and C are incorrect because they are checking for healthy instances: Option A, "AWS/ApplicationELB HealthyHostCount <= 0," would trigger the alarm when the count of healthy instances is zero or less, which is the opposite of the requirement.

B is correct The "UnhealthyHostCount" metric of the Application Load Balancer (ALB) represents the number of registered instances that are reported as unhealthy. By setting the alarm condition to "UnhealthyHostCount >= 1," the alarm will be triggered when at least one target instance associated with the ALB is reported as unhealthy.

To set an alarm for when all target instances associated with the ALB are unhealthy, the SysOps administrator should use the HealthyHostCount metric of the AWS/ApplicationELB namespace and set the condition to <= 0. This means that when the number of healthy hosts is less than or equal to 0, the alarm will be triggered 1. So, the correct answer would be A. AWS/ApplicationELB HealthyHostCount <= 0.
Key word: "ALL target instances"
A is not correct: This would trigger an alarm only if none of the target instances are healthy, but it may not capture cases where some instances are unhealthy.

question.en:
A company has a web application with a database tier that consists of an Amazon EC2 instance that runs MySQL. A SysOps administrator needs to minimize potential data loss and the time that is required to recover in the event of a database failure.

What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は、MySQLを実行するAmazon EC2インスタンスからなるデータベース層を持つWebアプリケーションを運用しています。SysOps管理者は、データベース障害発生時のデータ損失を最小限に抑え、復旧に要する時間を短縮する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たす、最も運用効率の高いソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm for the StatusCheckFailed_System metric to invoke an AWS Lambda function that stops and starts the EC2 instance.
text.ja: StatusCheckFailed_Systemメトリクスに対するAmazon CloudWatchアラームを作成し、EC2インスタンスを停止・起動するAWS Lambda関数を呼び出すようにする。

text.en: Create an Amazon RDS for MySQL Multi-AZ DB instance. Use a MySQL native backup that is stored in Amazon S3 to restore the data to the new database. Update the connection string in the web application.
text.ja: Amazon RDS for MySQLのMulti-AZ DBインスタンスを作成する。Amazon S3に保存されたMySQLネイティブバックアップを使用してデータを新しいデータベースに復元する。Webアプリケーションの接続文字列を更新する。

text.en: Create an Amazon RDS for MySQL Single-AZ DB instance with a read replica. Use a MySQL native backup that is stored in Amazon S3 to restore the data to the new database. Update the connection string in the web application
text.ja: リードレプリカ付きのAmazon RDS for MySQLのSingle-AZ DBインスタンスを作成する。Amazon S3に保存されたMySQLネイティブバックアップを使用してデータを新しいデータベースに復元する。Webアプリケーションの接続文字列を更新する。

text.en: Use Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager (Amazon DLM) to take a snapshot of the Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume every hour. In the event of an EC2 instance failure, restore the EBS volume from a snapshot.
text.ja: Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager (Amazon DLM) を使用して、Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) ボリュームのスナップショットを1時間ごとに取得する。EC2インスタンス障害発生時には、スナップショットからEBSボリュームを復元する。

comment.en: To me both B&D minimize potential data loss. However, the question is not specifying availability (Multi-AZ is B) Instead, the question is emphasizing quick recovery from backup (not active/active recovery) Therefore, I think only option D meets both criteria for minimizing potential data loss and quick recovery. - 1 Hour RPO (only D specifies hourly snapshots & hourly RPO) - Snapshots (D) have faster RTO than native backup restore from S3 (B) In the real world, I've used both backups (regularly/scheduled) and snapshots (prior to scheduled changes, etc.)

D is incorrect why: While using Amazon DLM for EBS snapshots provides backups, it's not as comprehensive for database use as RDS. In the event of a failure, recovery time would be longer as you would have to manually restore the EBS volume from a snapshot and then attach it to a new EC2 instance.
3rd point: - We are talkng about MySQL hosted on EBS volume on EC2 and native backups to S3 (not Aurora MySQL and AWS Backup)
Def B as to minimize the Data loss, In case of D which is 1 hour Snapshot there would be a data lost during this 1 hour interval, while in B Multi-AZ there won't be any data loss

My first questions was: "why has this not already been migrated over to RDS Multi-AZ" I think it most operationally efficient to use a service that could be renamed "MySQL designed for failover events" data loss would be minutes. Data loss on EBS snapshots would be up to 60 mins. Being in this situation I would be quizzing my project manager on why in gods name are we storing this on EC2/EBS and aren't these backups slight overkill.
B satisfy the "minimize potential data loss and the time that is required to recover in the event of a database failure and the MOST operationally efficient solution - doing one time and forget it.
B "MOST operationally efficient solution" is to use Rds with multi az

The key is operationally efficient - While minimizing data loss and recovery time. B - Operationally efficient - Multi - AZ failover - ensures no data loss of committed transactions, automated operation. D - Operationally - the Administrator has to restore the snapshot, use native tools to recover the database which is operationally inefficient. Consistent Backups of data plus database logs is simpler using RDS Hence - A
I meant 'B' sorry

This is a strong solution because Amazon RDS for MySQL with a Multi-AZ deployment provides high availability by automatically replicating database instances across multiple Availability Zones (AZs). This means if one AZ has issues, RDS will automatically fail over to the standby in another AZ with minimal disruption and without manual intervention. Additionally, RDS handles backups automatically and stores them for a user-defined period, which can be restored as needed. Updating the application's connection string to the new RDS instance completes the migration process from EC2 to RDS. While snapshotting the EBS volume frequently does help to minimize data loss, this option does NOT provide a QUICK failover solution in case of a database failure. Recovery time would include the process of provisioning a new EC2 instance and attaching the restored EBS volume, which can be time-consuming and also may result in data loss since the last snapshot.

B by chatGpto4 Conclusion: Option B (RDS Multi-AZ): Using Amazon RDS for MySQL Multi-AZ DB instance offers the most operationally efficient and robust solution to minimize data loss and downtime. It ensures high availability, automated backups, and failover capabilities, making it ideal for production environments where database reliability a
Sold by Gomer's answer

question.en:
Accompany wants to monitor the number of Amazon EC2 instances that it is running. The company also wants to automate a service quota increase when the number of instances reaches a specific threshold.

Which solution meets these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は、実行中のAmazon EC2インスタンスの数を監視したいと考えています。同社はまた、インスタンス数が特定のしきい値に達したときに、サービスクォータの増加を自動化したいと考えています。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to monitor Service Quotas. Configure the alarm to invoke an AWS Lambda function to request a quota increase when the alarm reaches the threshold.
text.ja: Service Quotasを監視するAmazon CloudWatchアラームを作成する。アラームがしきい値に達したときにクォータ増加をリクエストするAWS Lambda関数を呼び出すように設定する。

text.en: Create an AWS Config rule to monitor Service Quotas. Call an AWS Lambda function to remediate the action and increase the quota.
text.ja: Service Quotasを監視するAWS Configルールを作成する。アクションを修復してクォータを増加させるAWS Lambda関数を呼び出す。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWateh alarm to monitor the AWS Health Dashboard. Configure the alarm to invoke an AWS Lambda function to request a quota increase when the alarm reaches the threshold.
text.ja: AWS Health Dashboardを監視するAmazon CloudWatchアラームを作成する。アラームがしきい値に達したときにクォータ増加をリクエストするAWS Lambda関数を呼び出すように設定する。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to monitor AWS Trusted Advisor service quotas. Configure the alarm to publish a message to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to increase the quota.
text.ja: AWS Trusted Advisorのサービスクォータを監視するAmazon CloudWatchアラームを作成する。クォータを増加させるためにAmazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) トピックにメッセージをパブリッシュするように設定する。

comment.en: SNS cannot request a service limit increase, however CloudWatch can trigger an AWS Lambda function to automatically request a quota increase. here is an example: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3WUShZ4lZGE

Option A leverages Amazon CloudWatch alarms to monitor the Service Quotas metric related to the number of EC2 instances. When the alarm's threshold is breached, it triggers an action to invoke an AWS Lambda function. This Lambda function can be programmed to automatically request a quota increase for the EC2 instances, based on the predetermined conditions set in the alarm.

Based on my research - I do know that Trusted Advisor forwards service quota issues to CloudWatch - I don't know if Trusted Advisor (on a schedule?) - I do not know if CloudWatch monitors Service Quota's directely (independend of Trusted Advisor) - I think best solution is for SNS to call RequestServiceQuotaIncrease API directly (don't need Lambda) Here's some CLI examples I came up with for EC2 "New Reserved Instances per month" quota aws service-quotas get-aws-default-service-quota --service-code ec2 --quota-code L-D0B7243C aws service-quotas list-service-quotas --service-code ec2 --query "Quotas[?QuotaName=='New Reserved Instances per month']" aws service-quotas request-service-quota-increase --service-code ec2 --quota-code L-D0B7243C --desired-value 21 aws service-quotas get-requested-service-quota-change --request-id 0f06391f1678494bad04e24d91c88fb8OF5J35Qu URLs https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicequotas/latest/userguide/request-quota-increase.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicequotas/2019-06-24/apireference/API_RequestServiceQuotaIncrease.html

For my knowledge SNS cannot do data manipulation, to ask a quota increase we need to specify a desired value, I can easy the desired value to be easily calculate with lambda, while i don't understand how to do it with SNS.

To monitor the number of Amazon EC2 instances and automate a service quota increase when a specific threshold is reached, you can use Amazon CloudWatch alarms and AWS Lambda functions. Monitoring AWS Trusted Advisor service quotas is not the correct method for monitoring the number of EC2 instances or automating quota increases.
I go with A. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicequotas/latest/userguide/configure-cloudwatch.html

For my knowledge SNS cannot do data manipulation. To ask a quota increase we need to specify a desired value, I can easy the desired value to be easily calculate with lambda, while i don't understand how to do it with SNS.So i will go for A, but open to change my mind

CloudWatch is OK to monitor Service Quotas and Trusted Advisor forwards quota issues to CloudWatch. IMHO, the question centers around the best way to initiate a Quota Increase. I think it could be done with Lambda (aws cli). However, I think it's cleaner to just use SNS to publish the quota increase request directly to HTTPS endpoint (AWS API Call). Don't need Lambda (IMHO)
Cloudwatch alarms -> Invoke Lambda -> increase quota
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/servicequotas/latest/userguide/request-quota-increase.html Use the AWS Management Console and/or AWS CLI(Lambda)
https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2022/11/amazon-sns-enhanced-integration-aws-service-quotas/
this is quota increase of SNS not SNS topic to increase quota increase of an instance. SNS topic can not trigger Quota increase

question.en:
A SysOps administrator is responsible for more than 50 Amazon EC2 instances that are deployed in a single production AWS account. The EC2 instances are running several different operating systems. The company's standards require patching to be completed at least once a month.

The SysOps administrator wants to use AWS Systems Manager to reduce the number of hours the company spends on operating system patching each month.

Which combination of steps should the SysOps administrator take to meet these requirements? (Choose three.)
question.ja:
あるSysOps管理者は、単一の本番AWSアカウントにデプロイされている50を超えるAmazon EC2インスタンスを担当しています。これらのEC2インスタンスは、いくつかの異なるオペレーティングシステムで実行されています。同社の基準では、少なくとも月に1回パッチ適用を完了する必要があります。

SysOps管理者は、AWS Systems Managerを使用して、オペレーティングシステムのパッチ適用に毎月費やす時間を削減したいと考えています。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者が取るべき手順の組み合わせはどれですか。（3つ選択してください）

text.en: Group similar EC2 instances together into resource groups by using AWS Resource Groups.
text.ja: AWS Resource Groupsを使用して、類似のEC2インスタンスをリソースグループにまとめる。

text.en: Create a schedule in Systems Manager Patch Manager. Specify the appropriate resource group as the target.
text.ja: Systems Manager Patch Managerでスケジュールを作成する。ターゲットとして適切なリソースグループを指定する。

text.en: Specify Systems Manager Automation runbooks to patch the operating systems. Register the runbooks as tasks in the maintenance window. Specify the appropriate resource group as the target.
text.ja: オペレーティングシステムにパッチを適用するSystems Manager Automationランブックを指定する。そのランブックをメンテナンスウィンドウのタスクとして登録する。ターゲットとして適切なリソースグループを指定する。

text.en: Create a Systems Manager Automation runbook to monitor and control the state of the patches required. Apply the runbook to Systems Manager Patch Manager.
text.ja: 必要なパッチの状態を監視・制御するSystems Manager Automationランブックを作成する。そのランブックをSystems Manager Patch Managerに適用する。

text.en: Create a single Systems Manager maintenance window for each resource group.
text.ja: 各リソースグループに対して1つのSystems Managerメンテナンスウィンドウを作成する。

text.en: Configure Systems Manager Fleet Manager to apply a Systems Manager Automation runbook to the appropriate resource group.
text.ja: Systems Manager Fleet Managerを設定して、適切なリソースグループにSystems Manager Automationランブックを適用する。

comment.en: A: I see a "Resource Group" as the same as a "Patch Group", other than it was created by AWS Resource Groups Service. As I see it, both just apply tags to AWS instances. B: I don't see the term "schedule" EVER being used with Patch Manager. I'm of the opinion that a "Maintenance Window" isn't the same thing in this context. C: See the SSM Runbooks for patching "AWS-RunPatchBaseline" D: I couldn't find an SSM Runbook for monitor/control patch states. E: Maintenance Window is Patch Group setting F: I can't find a reference for using Fleet Manager with Patch Manager or run books.
Reference regarding patch related runbooks: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/patch-manager-ssm-documents.html

A: By grouping similar EC2 instances into resource groups, you can easily manage and apply patching configurations and schedules to specific sets of instances. This helps to organize and target the patching process effectively. B: Using Systems Manager Patch Manager, you can create a patching schedule that defines when the patching operations should occur. By specifying the appropriate resource group as the target, you ensure that the patching schedule is applied to the specific group of instances. C: Systems Manager Automation runbooks provide predefined workflows that can automate common operational tasks, such as patching. You can create and register Automation runbooks to handle the patching process for the operating systems on your EC2 instances. By specifying the appropriate resource group as the target for the runbooks, you ensure that the patching is applied to the specific group of instances.
Sorry, I was so tired last night. Gomer is right. ACE

A - Patch group B - patch manager policy D - Monitoring This is the best outcome I can see. from clickopsing on the console. I am able to create a patch policy which schedules install and scan activities based on custom cron or daily/weekly. I can deploy these by resource group in the UI. This policy seems to be a better practice than older methods such as run command or ssm baseline/maintenance window. The last point in the document mentions monitoring. I hope that AWS has followed their own documentation on best practices when I answer this in my exam. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/patch-manager-console.html#:~:text=Verify%20that%20the,and%20investigate%20failures

E not correct because it would increase the administrative burden rather than reducing it.B, correct answer Patch Manager helps automate the process of patching managed instances. By creating a patching schedule and targeting the resource groups created in step A, the administrator can ensure that patching is performed consistently and in line with company standards.

question.en:
ASysOps administrator configures an application to run on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB) in a simple scaling Auto Scaling group with the default settings. The Auto Scaling group is configured to use the RequestCountPerTarget metric for scaling. The SysOps administrator notices that the RequestCountPerTarget metric exceeded the specified limit twice in 180 seconds.

How will the number of EC2 instances in this Auto Scaling group be affected in this scenario?
question.ja:
あるSysOps管理者は、デフォルト設定のsimple scalingを使用するAuto ScalingグループのApplication Load Balancer (ALB) 配下で、Amazon EC2インスタンス上で実行されるアプリケーションを設定しました。このAuto Scalingグループは、スケーリングにRequestCountPerTargetメトリクスを使用するように設定されています。SysOps管理者は、RequestCountPerTargetメトリクスが180秒間に2回、指定した上限を超えたことに気付きました。

このシナリオでは、このAuto ScalingグループのEC2インスタンス数はどのように影響を受けますか。

text.en: The Auto Scaling group will launch an additional EC2 instance every time the RequestCountPerTarget metric exceeds the predefined limit.
text.ja: RequestCountPerTargetメトリクスが事前定義した上限を超えるたびに、Auto Scalingグループは追加のEC2インスタンスを起動する。

text.en: The Auto Scaling group will launch one EC2 instance and will wait for the default cooldown period before launching another instance.
text.ja: Auto Scalingグループは1台のEC2インスタンスを起動し、次のインスタンスを起動するまでデフォルトのクールダウン期間だけ待機する。

text.en: The Auto Scaling group will send an alert to the ALB to rebalance the traffic and not add new EC2 instances until the load is normalized.
text.ja: Auto Scalingグループはトラフィックを再分散させるアラートをALBに送信し、負荷が正常化するまで新しいEC2インスタンスを追加しない。

text.en: The Auto Scaling group will try to distribute the traffic among all EC2 instances before launching another instance.
text.ja: Auto Scalingグループは、新しいインスタンスを起動する前に、すべてのEC2インスタンス間でトラフィックを分散させようとする。

comment.en: Simple Scaling policy "DefaultCooldown" = 300 seconds https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/consolidated-view-of-warm-up-and-cooldown-settings.html
BBBB....

question.en:
A SysOps administrator launches an Amazon EC2 Linux instance in a public subnet. When the instance is running, the SysOps administrator obtains the public IP address and attempts to remotely connect to the Instance multiple times. However, the SysOps administrator always receives a timeout error.

Which action will allow the SysOps administrator to remotely connect to the instance?
question.ja:
あるSysOps管理者が、パブリックサブネットにAmazon EC2 Linuxインスタンスを起動しました。インスタンスが実行状態になった後、SysOps管理者はパブリックIPアドレスを取得し、そのインスタンスに複数回リモート接続を試みました。しかし、SysOps管理者は常にタイムアウトエラーを受け取ります。

SysOps管理者がそのインスタンスにリモート接続できるようにするために、どの対処を行うべきですか。

text.en: Add a route table entry in the public subnet for the SysOps administrator's IP address.
text.ja: パブリックサブネットに、SysOps管理者のIPアドレス宛のルートテーブルエントリを追加する。

text.en: Add an outbound network ACL rule to allow TCP port 22 for the SysOps administrator's IP address.
text.ja: SysOps管理者のIPアドレスに対してTCPポート22を許可するアウトバウンドネットワークACLルールを追加する。

text.en: Modify the instance security group to allow inbound SSH traffic from the SysOps administrator's IP address.
text.ja: SysOps管理者のIPアドレスからのインバウンドSSHトラフィックを許可するように、インスタンスのセキュリティグループを変更する。

text.en: Modify the instance security group to allow outbound SSH traffic to the SysOps administrator's IP address.
text.ja: SysOps管理者のIPアドレスへのアウトバウンドSSHトラフィックを許可するように、インスタンスのセキュリティグループを変更する。

comment.en: need to allow inbound SSH traffic
It must be C. I have this experience in real world.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/authorizing-access-to-an-instance.html

question.en:
A company hosts a production MySQL database on an Amazon Aurora single-node DB cluster. The database is queried heavily for reporting purposes. The DB cluster is experiencing periods of performance degradation because of high CPU utilization and maximum connections errors. A SysOps administrator needs to improve the stability of the database.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は、Amazon Auroraのシングルノードクラスターで本番のMySQLデータベースをホストしています。このデータベースはレポート作成のために頻繁にクエリされます。CPU使用率の高さと最大接続数エラーにより、DBクラスターのパフォーマンスが低下する時間帯があります。SysOps管理者は、データベースの安定性を改善する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an Aurora Replica node. Create an Auto Scaling policy to scale replicas based on CPU utilization. Ensure that all reporting requests use the read-only connection string
text.ja: Aurora Replicaノードを作成する。CPU使用率に基づいてレプリカをスケーリングするAuto Scalingポリシーを作成する。すべてのレポート要求が読み取り専用の接続文字列を使用するようにする。

text.en: Create a second Aurora MySQL single-node DB cluster in a second Availability Zone. Ensure that all reporting requests use the connection string for this additional node
text.ja: 2つ目のアベイラビリティーゾーンに2つ目のAurora MySQLシングルノードDBクラスターを作成する。すべてのレポート要求がこの追加ノードの接続文字列を使用するようにする。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function that caches reporting requests. Ensure that all reporting requests call the Lambda function
text.ja: レポート要求をキャッシュするAWS Lambda関数を作成する。すべてのレポート要求がそのLambda関数を呼び出すようにする。

text.en: Create a multi-node Amazon ElastiCache cluster. Ensure that all reporting requests use the ElastiCache cluster. Use the database if the data is not in the cache.
text.ja: 複数ノードのAmazon ElastiCacheクラスターを作成する。すべてのレポート要求がそのElastiCacheクラスターを使用するようにする。データがキャッシュに存在しない場合はデータベースを使用する。

comment.en: Creating an Aurora Replica node will offload the reporting workload from the primary DB instance, reducing the CPU utilization and improving stability. By creating an Auto Scaling policy to scale replicas based on CPU utilization, you can automatically add or remove replicas as needed to handle the reporting workload. Ensuring that all reporting requests use the read-only connection string directs the reporting workload to the replica nodes, further reducing the load on the primary DB instance.
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
A, read degradation = read replica + reader endpoint
A is the correct answer
reporting --> replica

question.en:
A company runs a web application that users access using the name www example com. The company manages the domain name example.com using Amazon Route 53. The company created an Amazon CloudFront distribution in front of the application and would like www.example.com to access the application through CloudFront.

What is the MOST cost-effective way to achieve this?
question.ja:
ある企業は、ユーザーがwww.example.comという名前でアクセスするWebアプリケーションを運用しています。同社は、Amazon Route 53を使用してドメイン名example.comを管理しています。同社は、そのアプリケーションの前段にAmazon CloudFrontディストリビューションを作成し、www.example.comがCloudFrontを経由してアプリケーションにアクセスできるようにしたいと考えています。

これを実現する最もコスト効率の良い方法はどれですか。

text.en: Create a CNAME record in Amazon Route 53 that points to the CloudFront distribution URL.
text.ja: CloudFrontディストリビューションのURLを指すCNAMEレコードをAmazon Route 53に作成する。

text.en: Create an ALIAS record in Amazon Route 53 that points to the CioudFront distribution URL.
text.ja: CloudFrontディストリビューションのURLを指すALIASレコードをAmazon Route 53に作成する。

text.en: Create an A record in Amazon Route 53 that points to the public IP address of the web application,
text.ja: WebアプリケーションのパブリックIPアドレスを指すAレコードをAmazon Route 53に作成する。

text.en: Create a PTR record in Amazon Route 53 that points to the public IP address of the web application.
text.ja: Webアプリケーションのパブリックイアドレスを指すPTRレコードをAmazon Route 53に作成する。

comment.en: Option B is the correct answer because ALIAS records in Amazon Route 53 are used to map domain names directly to AWS resources like CloudFront distributions, S3 buckets, Elastic Load Balancers, and more. ALIAS records provide a more efficient and cost-effective way to route traffic to these AWS resources compared to CNAME records.
after more research, answer is B since it is more cost effective to create an alias (which is free) WWW that points to Cloudfront distribution URL.
apex zone= ALias
B is the correct answer
www.example.com -- alias -- example.com
Answer is B - this is a very similar question that other practice test from Udemy offer
Answer is A. CNAME – maps a hostname to another hostname. Example: you can’t create for example.com, but you can create for www.example.com
Correct Answer B

Answer is A, it is TRUE that apex maps only to alias… HOWEVER in this question you are mapping www subdomain to cname of the url of cloudfront… WWW is not APEX… https://repost.aws/questions/QUISgcF7I1Q9K4jzyReQiDMA/cname-for-domain-com-without-www

question.en:
A company is managing multiple AWS accounts in AWS Organizations. The company is reviewing internal security of its AWS environment. The company’s security administrator has their own AWS account and wants to review the VPC configuration of developer AWS accounts.

Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST secure manner?
question.ja:
ある企業はAWS Organizationsで複数のAWSアカウントを管理している。この企業は自社のAWS環境の内部セキュリティを見直している。企業のセキュリティ管理者は自分自身のAWSアカウントを持っており、開発者用AWSアカウントのVPC設定を確認したいと考えている。

これらの要件を最も安全な方法で満たすソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Create an IAM policy in each developer account that has read-only access related to VPC resources. Assign the policy to an IAM user. Share the user credentials with the security administrator.
text.ja: 各開発者アカウントに、VPCリソースに関する読み取り専用アクセス権を持つIAMポリシーを作成する。そのポリシーをIAMユーザーに割り当てる。ユーザーの認証情報をセキュリティ管理者と共有する。

text.en: Create an IAM policy in each developer account that has administrator access to all Amazon EC2 actions, including VPC actions. Assign the policy to an IAM user. Share the user credentials with the security administrator.
text.ja: 各開発者アカウントに、VPCアクションを含むすべてのAmazon EC2アクションに対する管理者アクセス権を持つIAMポリシーを作成する。そのポリシーをIAMユーザーに割り当てる。ユーザーの認証情報をセキュリティ管理者と共有する。

text.en: Create an IAM policy in each developer account that has administrator access related to VPC resources. Assign the policy to a cross-account IAM role. Ask the security administrator to assume the role from their account.
text.ja: 各開発者アカウントに、VPCリソースに関する管理者アクセス権を持つIAMポリシーを作成する。そのポリシーをクロスアカウントIAMロールに割り当てる。セキュリティ管理者に、自分のアカウントからそのロールをAssumeしてもらう。

text.en: Create an IAM policy in each developer account that has read-only access related to VPC resources. Assign the policy to a cross-account IAM role. Ask the security administrator to assume the role from their account.
text.ja: 各開発者アカウントに、VPCリソースに関する読み取り専用アクセス権を持つIAMポリシーを作成する。そのポリシーをクロスアカウントIAMロールに割り当てる。セキュリティ管理者に、自分のアカウントからそのロールをAssumeしてもらう。

comment.en: D because I am D best admin in the world right now.

The most secure way to provide access between AWS accounts is by using IAM roles with cross-account access. And also, the security administrator only needs read-only access to review the VPC configuration.
read-only --> A, D. Assign policy to an IAM user --> remove A. Choose D.
A, D --> read-only access. D: cross-account IAM role --> Choose D.
Answer C; a dev account with read-only access to VPC is ok whereas a dev account with admin access to VPC requires review.
Answer C; a dev account with read-only access to VPC is ok whereas a dev account with admin access to VPC requires review.
D , AWS = AlWays aSume the role
D is the answer
security administrator has their own AWS account so you should use cross-account and read only to follow least privilege principle

question.en:
A company wants to monitor the security groups of its Amazon EC2 instances to ensure that SSH is not open to the public. If the port is opened, the company needs to close the port as soon as possible.

Which combination of actions should a SysOps administrator take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある企業はAmazon EC2インスタンスのセキュリティグループを監視し、SSHがパブリックに開放されていないことを確認したいと考えている。ポートが開いてしまった場合は、できるだけ早くそのポートを閉じる必要がある。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者はどの組み合わせの操作を行うべきか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Add an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to detect the security groups that allow SSH.
text.ja: SSHを許可しているセキュリティグループを検知するAmazon CloudWatchアラームを追加する。

text.en: Add an AWS Config rule to detect the security groups that allow SSH.
text.ja: SSHを許可しているセキュリティグループを検知するAWS Configルールを追加する。

text.en: Add an assessment template to Amazon Inspector to detect the security groups that allow SSH.
text.ja: SSHを許可しているセキュリティグループを検知するために、Amazon Inspectorにアセスメントテンプレートを追加する。

text.en: Call an AWS Systems Manager Automation runbook to close the port.
text.ja: AWS Systems Manager Automationランブックを呼び出してポートを閉じる。

text.en: Call AWS Systems Manager Run Command to close the port.
text.ja: AWS Systems Manager Run Commandを呼び出してポートを閉じる。

comment.en: Add an AWS Config rule to detect the security groups that allow SSH. By creating a custom AWS Config rule, you can define the desired configuration that checks if SSH ports are open in security groups. This rule will evaluate the current state of the security groups and report any violations. Call an AWS Systems Manager Automation runbook to close the port. Set up an AWS Systems Manager Automation runbook that can be triggered when a violation is detected by the AWS Config rule. The runbook should include the necessary steps to close the SSH port in the affected security groups, ensuring that the port is no longer accessible to the public.
It's B and D
B and D
* Detect SSH port open: A, B, or C? --> AWS Config rule --> B. * Close: D or E? --> automation --> D.

(AWS Systems Manager Run Command) is a valid Systems Manager feature, but it is more suitable for one-time commands rather than automated remediation based on continuous compliance checks performed by AWS Config. For continuous compliance checks, AWS Config Rules and Automation runbooks are more appropriate.

B no issues. D and E, choose D as it's using automation to call AWS service. D: Automation, a capability of AWS Systems Manager, simplifies common maintenance, deployment, and remediation tasks for AWS services like Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2), Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS), Amazon Redshift, Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3), and many more. E: Using Run Command, a capability of AWS Systems Manager, you can remotely and securely manage the configuration of your managed nodes. With command documents you are executing commands on your managed instances (i.e. yum update)

I cant see any pre-defined runbooks that are designed to close a port. that being said this is a simple script. why do we think we are better off not using run command? sure I can create my own automation runbook but I still need to provide a script. makes it a similar overhead to using run command. As this is one off maintenance I personally would use run command. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/run-command.html
I stand corrected.... https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/security/how-to-auto-remediate-internet-accessible-ports-with-aws-config-and-aws-system-manager/#:~:text=Create%20a%20remediation%20action

This scenario is basically what SSM run command was designed for: "Using Run Command, a capability of AWS Systems Manager, you can remotely and securely manage the configuration of your managed nodes. A managed node is any Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instance or non-EC2 machine in your hybrid and multicloud environment that has been configured for Systems Manager. Run Command allows you to automate common administrative tasks and perform one-time configuration changes at scale. You can use Run Command from the AWS Management Console, the AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI), AWS Tools for Windows PowerShell, or the AWS SDKs. Run Command is offered at no additional cost."
B and E works for me

question.en:
A company has an existing public web application for www.example.com. The Application Load Balancer (ALB) is configured with a single HTTP 80 listener. A SysOps administrator must ensure that all web requests to www.example.com are encrypted between the client and the ALB.

The SysOps administrator already has requested and validated a public certificate for www.example.com in AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Existing users of the application must not be required to change the endpoint to which they are connecting.

Which additional set of steps should the SysOps administrator take to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業はwww.example.com向けの既存の公開Webアプリケーションを運用している。Application Load Balancer（ALB）は、単一のHTTP 80リスナーで構成されている。SysOps管理者は、www.example.comへのすべてのWebリクエストが、クライアントとALBの間で暗号化されるようにしなければならない。

SysOps管理者は、AWS Certificate Manager（ACM）でwww.example.com用のパブリック証明書を既にリクエストし、検証済みである。既存のアプリケーション利用者は、接続先のエンドポイントを変更する必要があってはならない。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者はどの追加手順を実行すべきか。

text.en: Create an additional ALB listener for HTTPS on port 443. Set the default action to forward all traffic to the target group. Specify the ACM certificate that was created for www.example.com as the default SSL certificate.
text.ja: ポート443でHTTPS用の追加のALBリスナーを作成する。デフォルトアクションを、すべてのトラフィックをターゲットグループに転送するように設定する。www.example.com用に作成されたACM証明書をデフォルトのSSL証明書として指定する。

text.en: Create an additional ALB listener for HTTPS on port 443. Set the default action to forward all traffic to the target group. Specify the ACM certificate that was created for www.example.com as the default SSL certificate. Delete the original HTTP listener on port 80.
text.ja: ポート443でHTTPS用の追加のALBリスナーを作成する。デフォルトアクションを、すべてのトラフィックをターゲットグループに転送するように設定する。www.example.com用に作成されたACM証明書をデフォルトのSSL証明書として指定する。元のポート80のHTTPリスナーを削除する。

text.en: Modify the ALB default rule for the HTTP port 80 listener. Create a rule in the listener to forward all traffic for the host www example.com to the target group. Specify the ACM certificate that was created for www.example.com as the default SSL certificate.
text.ja: ポート80のHTTPリスナーのALBデフォルトルールを変更する。ホストwww.example.com向けのすべてのトラフィックをターゲットグループに転送するルールをリスナーに作成する。www.example.com用に作成されたACM証明書をデフォルトのSSL証明書として指定する。

text.en: Modify the ALB default rule for the HTTP port 80 listener to redirect to HTTPS on port 443. Create an additional HTTPS listener on port 443. Set the default action to forward all traffic to the target group. Specify the ACM certificate that was created for www example.com as the default SSL certificate.
text.ja: ポート80のHTTPリスナーのALBデフォルトルールを変更し、ポート443のHTTPSへリダイレクトするようにする。ポート443に追加のHTTPSリスナーを作成する。デフォルトアクションを、すべてのトラフィックをターゲットグループに転送するように設定する。www.example.com用に作成されたACM証明書をデフォルトのSSL証明書として指定する。

comment.en: It's D. It's the only option that forces HTTPS to all of the web requests.
D is answer
D to force HTTPS on port 80 and 443

question.en:
A company's SysOps administrator manages a fleet of hundreds of Amazon EC2 instances that run Windows-based workloads and Linux-based workloads. Each EC2 instance has a tag that identifies its operating system. All the EC2 instances run AWS Systems Manager Session Manager.

A zero-day vulnerability is reported, and no patches are available. The company's security team provides code for all the relevant operating systems to reduce the risk of the vulnerability. The SysOps administrator needs to implement the code on the EC2 instances and must provide a report that shows that the code has successfully run on all the instances.

What should the SysOps administrator do to meet these requirements as quickly as possible?
question.ja:
ある企業のSysOps管理者は、WindowsベースのワークロードとLinuxベースのワークロードを実行する数百台のAmazon EC2インスタンスの集合を管理している。各EC2インスタンスには、そのOSを識別するタグが付いている。すべてのEC2インスタンスでAWS Systems Manager Session Managerが稼働している。

ゼロデイ脆弱性が報告されたが、パッチはまだ提供されていない。企業のセキュリティチームは、リスクを低減するためのコードを、関連するすべてのOS向けに提供した。SysOps管理者は、そのコードをEC2インスタンスに実装し、すべてのインスタンスでコードが正常に実行されたことを示すレポートを提出しなければならない。

これらの要件をできるだけ迅速に満たすために、SysOps管理者は何をすべきか。

text.en: Use Systems Manager Run Command. Choose either the AWS-RunShellScript document or the AWS-RunPowerShellScript document. Configure Run Command with the code from the security team. Specify the operating system tag in the Targets parameter. Run the command. Provide the command history's evidence to the security team.
text.ja: Systems Manager Run Commandを使用する。AWS-RunShellScriptドキュメントまたはAWS-RunPowerShellScriptドキュメントのいずれかを選択する。Run Commandに、セキュリティチームからのコードを設定する。TargetsパラメータにOSタグを指定する。コマンドを実行する。コマンドの実行履歴の証跡をセキュリティチームに提供する。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function that connects to the EC2 instances through Session Manager. Configure the Lambda function to identify the operating system, run the code from the security team, and return the results to an Amazon RDS DB instance. Query the DB instance for the results. Provide the results as evidence to the security team.
text.ja: Session Manager経由でEC2インスタンスに接続するAWS Lambda関数を作成する。そのLambda関数を、OSを識別してセキュリティチームからのコードを実行し、結果をAmazon RDS DBインスタンスに返すように構成する。DBインスタンスに結果をクエリする。その結果を証跡としてセキュリティチームに提供する。

text.en: Log on to each EC2 instance. Run the code from the security team on each EC2 instance. Copy and paste the results of each run into a single spreadsheet. Provide the spreadsheet as evidence to the security team.
text.ja: 各EC2インスタンスにログオンする。各EC2インスタンスでセキュリティチームからのコードを実行する。各実行結果を1つのスプレッドシートにコピー＆ペーストする。そのスプレッドシートを証跡としてセキュリティチームに提供する。

text.en: Update the launch templates of the EC2 instances to include the code from the security team in the user data. Relaunch the EC2 instances by using the updated launch templates. Retrieve the EC2 instance logs of each instance. Provide the EC2 instance logs as evidence to the security team.
text.ja: EC2インスタンスの起動テンプレートを更新し、セキュリティチームからのコードをユーザーデータに含める。更新済みの起動テンプレートを使用してEC2インスタンスを再起動する。各インスタンスのEC2インスタンスログを取得する。そのEC2インスタンスログを証跡としてセキュリティチームに提供する。

comment.en: Explanation: The AWS Systems Manager Run Command allows the SysOps administrator to execute commands on multiple EC2 instances simultaneously, which is crucial for managing a large fleet of instances efficiently. By using the AWS-RunShellScript or AWS-RunPowerShellScript documents, the SysOps administrator can run the code provided by the security team on both Windows-based and Linux-based instances, as required. The SysOps administrator can specify the operating system tag as a target parameter, ensuring that the code is only executed on the relevant instances, which saves time and prevents errors. AWS Systems Manager keeps a history of Run Command executions, providing an easy way to provide evidence to the security team that the code has successfully run on all instances.
run command
A is for sure
You don´t need to re-image your instances, just apply the code with RunCommand.

A Option A is the most efficient and automated way to quickly implement the code on all EC2 instances and provide evidence to the security team. AWS Systems Manager Run Command allows you to run commands on multiple instances simultaneously, making it ideal for fleet management. By choosing the appropriate AWS-RunShellScript (for Linux) or AWS-RunPowerShellScript (for Windows) document, the SysOps administrator can execute the code provided by the security team on the instances based on their operating system tags. The command history provides a record of the execution, and this evidence can be shared with the security team to demonstrate that the code has successfully run on all instances.

option A using Systems Manager Run Command is the most efficient and effective way to implement the security code provided by the security team across all EC2 instances and to provide a report or evidence of successful execution to the security team

question.en:
A SysOps administrator needs to deploy an application in multiple AWS Regions. The SysOps administrator must implement a solution that routes users to the Region with the lowest latency. In case of failure, the solution must automatically route requests to a Region with a healthy instance of the application. The company needs a solution with the shortest time to failover.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、複数のAWSリージョンにアプリケーションをデプロイする必要がある。SysOps管理者は、ユーザーを最も低いレイテンシーのリージョンにルーティングするソリューションを実装しなければならない。障害発生時には、リクエストをアプリケーションの正常なインスタンスがあるリージョンへ自動的にルーティングする必要がある。企業は、フェイルオーバーまでの時間が最短のソリューションを必要としている。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Create Amazon Route 53 A records that have the same name for each endpoint. Use a latency routing policy. Associate a health check with each record.
text.ja: 各エンドポイントについて、同じ名前を持つAmazon Route 53 Aレコードを作成する。レイテンシールーティングポリシーを使用する。各レコードにヘルスチェックを関連付ける。

text.en: Create Amazon Route 53 A records that have the same name for each endpoint. Use a failover routing policy. Associate a health check with each record.
text.ja: 各エンドポイントについて、同じ名前を持つAmazon Route 53 Aレコードを作成する。フェイルオーバールーティングポリシーを使用する。各レコードにヘルスチェックを関連付ける。

text.en: Create an AWS Global Accelerator standard accelerator. Create an endpoint group for each Region. Add a listener to the accelerator. Associate the endpoint group with the listener.
text.ja: AWS Global Acceleratorのスタンダードアクセラレーターを作成する。各リージョンにエンドポイントグループを作成する。アクセラレーターにリスナーを追加する。エンドポイントグループをリスナーに関連付ける。

text.en: Create Amazon Route 53 A records that have the same name for each endpoint. Use a geolocation routing policy. Associate a health check with each record.
text.ja: 各エンドポイントについて、同じ名前を持つAmazon Route 53 Aレコードを作成する。ジオロケーションルーティングポリシーを使用する。各レコードにヘルスチェックを関連付ける。

comment.en: AWS Global Accelerator is a service that helps to improve the availability and performance of applications by directing traffic through the AWS global network. It automatically routes traffic to the closest AWS Region based on the lowest latency for the end user. By creating an endpoint group for each AWS Region, the Global Accelerator can distribute traffic across multiple Regions. This means that users will be routed to the Region with the lowest latency, ensuring the best performance for their location. Adding a listener to the accelerator allows it to listen for incoming traffic and direct it to the appropriate endpoint group. The AWS Global Accelerator constantly monitors the health of the endpoints (applications) in each endpoint group. If an endpoint becomes unhealthy or experiences a failure, the Global Accelerator automatically reroutes traffic to a healthy instance of the application in another Region, ensuring high availability and automatic failover.

AWS Global Accelerator is designed to route traffic across multiple AWS Regions with low-latency, high-availability, and automatic failover. By creating an AWS Global Accelerator standard accelerator, you can create endpoint groups for each Region where your application is deployed. The accelerator automatically directs traffic to the healthy endpoint within the endpoint group. This solution provides a low-latency routing policy and supports automatic failover, meeting the requirements specified. Option A (using Route 53 with a latency routing policy) could work for latency-based routing but does not provide automatic failover. Option B (using Route 53 with a failover routing policy) could provide failover, but it doesn't explicitly mention the requirement for low-latency routing. Option D (using Route 53 with a geolocation routing policy) is based on geographic location and doesn't explicitly prioritize low-latency routing or provide automatic failover.
https://aws.amazon.com/global-accelerator/faqs/

A is correct. For C. AWS Global Accelerator improves global application availability and performance using the AWS global network. While it routes user traffic to healthy endpoints and can work well for global applications, it doesn't specifically route based on latency.

Global accelerator cannot satisfy the lowest latency requirement. only latency based routing policy can. it can max out performance but in the documentation the wording does not state latency. it covers many different factors but latency isn't one of them. The wording in the question to me doesn't scream out failover. they are asking if traffic will be re routed to another healthy instance in the event of the lowest latency instance failing. still using the lowest latency policy it will hit the other instance in the ALB target group. This is a tricky one but I cant see past the "has to route based on latency"
At the end, option A says "Associate a health check with each record". Doing this with all the records created, provides the redundancy needed for the application.

Option A, using latency-based routing in Amazon Route 53, can direct traffic to the Region with the lowest latency but doesn't offer the automatic failover capability. Option B, using failover routing in Amazon Route 53, can direct traffic to a standby Region in case of failure, but it doesn't consider latency for optimal routing. Option D, using geolocation routing in Amazon Route 53, routes traffic based on the geographic location of users but doesn't consider latency or provide automatic failover.

AWS Global Accelerator: This service is designed to route user traffic to the nearest AWS endpoint with the lowest latency. It uses the AWS global network, improving the user experience by reducing the number of hops and potentially the distance that user traffic must travel. It also provides automatic failover to the next closest endpoint that is healthy, ensuring high availability and resilience.

I've actually read the question now and I guess I made up there was a load balancer and target group in each region. I'm starting to lean more towards global accelerator as it uses edge locations and hence will be inadvertently routing to the lowest latency.
Global Accelerator
It´s latency based routing with failover.
option A is working, but correct answer is D, coz the question say "the shortest failover time". route 53 can take more time than aws global accellerator to understand an endpoint fail

question.en:
A company has a large on-premises tape backup solution. The company has started to use AWS Storage Gateway. The company created a Tape Gateway to replace the existing on-premises hardware. The company's backup engineer noticed that some of the backup jobs that were supposed to write to AWS failed to run because of a "Not Enough Space" error.

The company does not want these failures to happen again. The company also wants to consistently have enough tape available on AWS.

What is the MOST operationally efficient way for a SysOps administrator to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業は大規模なオンプレミスのテープバックアップソリューションを運用している。この企業はAWS Storage Gatewayの利用を開始した。企業は既存のオンプレミスハードウェアを置き換えるためにTape Gatewayを作成した。企業のバックアップエンジニアは、「Not Enough Space」エラーが原因で、AWSへの書き込みを行うはずのバックアップジョブの一部が失敗していることに気づいた。

企業はこうした失敗が再発することを望んでいない。また、AWS上に常に十分なテープを確保しておきたいと考えている。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者が取るべき最も運用効率の良い方法は何か。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function that runs on an hourly basis and checks how many tapes have available space. If the available tapes are below a certain threshold, provision more.
text.ja: 1時間ごとに実行され、空き容量のあるテープの数を確認するAWS Lambda関数を作成する。利用可能なテープが一定のしきい値を下回った場合は、さらにテープをプロビジョニングする。

text.en: Install the Amazon CloudWatch agent on the on-premises system. Push the log files to a CloudWatch log group. Create an AWS Lambda function that creates more tapes when the "Not Enough Space" error appears. Create a metric filter and a metric alarm that launches the Lambda function.
text.ja: オンプレミスのシステムにAmazon CloudWatchエージェントをインストールする。ログファイルをCloudWatchロググループにプッシュする。「Not Enough Space」エラーが現れたときにテープを追加作成するAWS Lambda関数を作成する。そのLambda関数を起動するメトリクスフィルターとメトリクスアラームを作成する。

text.en: Create an additional Tape Gateway with its own set of tapes. Configure Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) to send a notification to the backup engineer if the tapes that are associated with the primary Tape Gateway do not have available space.
text.ja: 独自のテープ群を持つ追加のTape Gatewayを作成する。プライマリのTape Gatewayに関連付けられたテープに空き容量がなくなった場合に、バックアップエンジニアに通知を送るようAmazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）を設定する。

text.en: Configure tape auto-create on the Tape Gateway. In the auto-create settings, configure a minimum number of tapes, an appropriate barcode prefix, and a tape pool.
text.ja: Tape Gatewayでテープの自動作成を設定する。自動作成の設定で、最小テープ数、適切なバーコードプレフィックス、テーププールを設定する。

comment.en: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/storagegateway/latest/tgw/managing-automatic-tape-creation.html The Tape Gateway automatically creates new virtual tapes to maintain the minimum number of available tapes that you configure. It then makes these new tapes available for import by the backup application so that your backup jobs can run without interruption. Automatic tape creation removes the need for custom scripting in addition to the manual process for creating new virtual tapes.
Auto-Create function is more efficient for this.

Virtual tapes are uniquely identified by a barcode, and you can add a prefix to the barcode. The prefix is optional, but you can use it to help identify your virtual tapes. The prefix must be uppercase letters (A–Z) and must be one to four characters long.

The Tape Gateway automatically creates new virtual tapes to maintain the minimum number of available tapes that you configure. It then makes these new tapes available for import by the backup application so that your backup jobs can run without interruption. Automatic tape creation removes the need for custom scripting in addition to the manual process for creating new virtual tapes.
D is the most appropriate. Ref: https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/s3-empty-bucket-lifecycle-rule

Options A, B, and C may address the issue, but they introduce additional complexity and may require more maintenance and monitoring effort. Option D is the simplest and most automated approach to ensure the availability of enough tape storage for backup jobs on AWS Storage Gateway.

question.en:
A SysOps administrator manages a company's Amazon S3 buckets. The SysOps administrator has identified 5 GB of incomplete multipart uploads in an S3 bucket in the company's AWS account. The SysOps administrator needs to reduce the number of incomplete multipart upload objects in the S3 bucket.

Which solution will meet this requirement?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、ある企業のAmazon S3バケットを管理している。SysOps管理者は、企業のAWSアカウント内のS3バケットに5GBの未完了のマルチパートアップロードがあることを確認した。SysOps管理者は、S3バケット内の未完了マルチパートアップロードオブジェクトの数を減らす必要がある。

この要件を満たすソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Create an S3 Lifecycle rule on the S3 bucket to delete expired markers or incomplete multipart uploads.
text.ja: S3バケットに、期限切れの削除マーカーまたは未完了のマルチパートアップロードを削除するS3ライフサイクルルールを作成する。

text.en: Require users that perform uploads of files into Amazon S3 to use the S3 TransferUtility.
text.ja: Amazon S3へのファイルアップロードを行うユーザーに対し、S3 TransferUtilityの使用を求める。

text.en: Enable S3 Versioning on the S3 bucket that contains the incomplete multipart uploads.
text.ja: 未完了のマルチパートアップロードを含むS3バケットでS3バージョニングを有効化する。

text.en: Create an S3 Object Lambda Access Point to delete incomplete multipart uploads.
text.ja: 未完了のマルチパートアップロードを削除するためのS3 Object Lambdaアクセスポイントを作成する。

comment.en: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws-cloud-financial-management/discovering-and-deleting-incomplete-multipart-uploads-to-lower-amazon-s3-costs/ Delete expired delete markers or incomplete multipart uploads under the Lifecycle rule actions section

A. Create an S3 Lifecycle rule on the S3 bucket to delete expired markers or incomplete multipart uploads. Explanation: S3 Lifecycle rules allow you to define actions that Amazon S3 should take on objects in the bucket over time. This includes transitioning objects between storage classes and deleting objects when they meet certain criteria. To reduce the number of incomplete multipart upload objects in the S3 bucket, you can create an S3 Lifecycle rule that targets incomplete multipart uploads and specifies a deletion action for them. This will help in automatically cleaning up the incomplete multipart uploads after a certain period.

Option B, requiring users to use the S3 TransferUtility when performing uploads, does not directly address the existing incomplete multipart uploads. It may help in preventing new incomplete uploads, but it won't clean up the existing ones. Option C, enabling S3 Versioning, won't automatically delete incomplete multipart uploads. Versioning helps you keep multiple versions of objects in S3, but it won't handle the cleanup of incomplete uploads. Option D, creating an S3 Object Lambda Access Point to delete incomplete multipart uploads, is not a valid solution. S3 Object Lambda Access Points provide a way to modify or transform data as it is being retrieved from an S3 bucket, but it is not designed for deleting objects.
Lifecycle rules can delete incomplete multipart upload files.
delete expired markers
A is true

question.en:
A SysOps administrator manages policies for many AWS member accounts in an AWS Organizations structure. Administrators on other teams have access to the account root user credentials of the member accounts. The SysOps administrator must prevent all teams, including their administrators, from using Amazon DynamoDB. The solution must not affect the ability of the teams to access other AWS services.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、AWS Organizations構成内の多数のAWSメンバーアカウントのポリシーを管理している。他のチームの管理者は、メンバーアカウントのルートユーザー認証情報へのアクセス権を持っている。SysOps管理者は、それらの管理者を含むすべてのチームがAmazon DynamoDBを使用できないようにしなければならない。このソリューションは、各チームが他のAWSサービスにアクセスする能力に影響を与えてはならない。

これらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれか。

text.en: In all member accounts, configure IAM policies that deny access to all DynamoDB resources for all users, including the root user.
text.ja: すべてのメンバーアカウントで、ルートユーザーを含むすべてのユーザーに対してDynamoDBリソースへのアクセスを拒否するIAMポリシーを設定する。

text.en: Create a service control policy (SCP) in the management account to deny all DynamoDB actions. Apply the SCP to the root of the organization
text.ja: 管理アカウントで、すべてのDynamoDBアクションを拒否するサービスコントロールポリシー（SCP）を作成する。そのSCPを組織のルートに適用する。

text.en: In all member accounts, configure IAM policies that deny AmazonDynamoDBFullAccess to all users, including the root user.
text.ja: すべてのメンバーアカウントで、ルートユーザーを含むすべてのユーザーに対してAmazonDynamoDBFullAccessを拒否するIAMポリシーを設定する。

text.en: Remove the default service control policy (SCP) in the management account. Create a replacement SCP that includes a single statement that denies all DynamoDB actions.
text.ja: 管理アカウントのデフォルトのサービスコントロールポリシー（SCP）を削除する。すべてのDynamoDBアクションを拒否する単一のステートメントを含む代替のSCPを作成する。

comment.en: The answer is B as you have no idea what other SCP policies could be in place and deleting the entire SCP would be bad practice.

Service Control Policies (SCPs) are a feature of AWS Organizations that allow you to set permissions across all member accounts in the organization. When you apply an SCP at the root of the organization, it affects all member accounts within that organization. In this scenario, by creating an SCP that denies all DynamoDB actions and applying it to the root of the AWS organization, you effectively block access to Amazon DynamoDB for all users, including the root user, in all member accounts within the organization. This solution prevents any team, including their administrators, from using DynamoDB while still allowing access to other AWS services that are not restricted by the SCP.

Answer: B Service Control Policies (SCPs) are used to manage permissions for all members of an AWS Organization. SCPs allow you to set permissions that restrict actions across the entire organization or specific organizational units (OUs). By creating an SCP that denies all DynamoDB actions and applying it at the root level of the AWS Organization, you can prevent all member accounts, including their administrators with root user credentials, from using Amazon DynamoDB. This solution will not affect the ability of the teams to access other AWS services, as SCPs are used to control permissions for specific services or actions.
I think B is true
I´m not sure, but for me it´s something between B and D, but not C.

I agree with the explanation of many folks here why B is the correct answer. One thing we must understand is that AWS Organizations attaches an AWS managed SCP named FullAWSAccess to every root, OU and account when it's created. This policy allows all services and actions. Therefore, attaching a new SCP that denies all DynamoDB actions to the root of the organizations makes a lot more sense. This is because we don't care what access other SCPs have granted the Management accounts and OUs, this new SCP explicitly denies DynamoDB related actions. We are good since explicit "deny" overrides explicit "allow". Please refer to this documentation - https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_policies_scps_evaluation.html for more information

question.en:
A company has implemented a Kubernetes cluster on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon ECS) to host a microservices-based application. The company expects application traffic to increase significantly for the next month and wants to prevent the application from crashing because of the high number of requests.

Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST administrative overhead?
question.ja:
ある企業はAmazon Elastic Kubernetes Service（Amazon ECS）上にKubernetesクラスターを構築し、マイクロサービス型アプリケーションをホストしている。この企業は、今後1か月間でアプリケーショントラフィックが大幅に増加すると想定しており、リクエスト数の増大が原因でアプリケーションがクラッシュすることを防ぎたいと考えている。

管理オーバーヘッドが最も少なくこれらの要件を満たすソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Create a second EKS cluster. Load balance the workload between the two clusters.
text.ja: 2つ目のEKSクラスターを作成する。ワークロードを2つのクラスター間で負荷分散する。

text.en: Implement the Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler. Set a target CPU utilization percentage.
text.ja: Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscalerを実装する。ターゲットのCPU使用率を設定する。

text.en: Migrate the application from Amazon EKS to Amazon EC2 for the next month. Migrate the application back to Amazon EKS when the month ends.
text.ja: 今後1か月間、アプリケーションをAmazon EKSからAmazon EC2に移行する。1か月が終わったら、アプリケーションをAmazon EKSに戻す。

text.en: Implement the Kubernetes Vertical Pod Autoscaler. Set a target CPU utilization percentage.
text.ja: Kubernetes Vertical Pod Autoscalerを実装する。ターゲットのCPU使用率を設定する。

comment.en: The Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) is designed to automatically scale the number of pods in a deployment or replica set based on observed CPU or memory utilization. In this scenario, the company wants to prevent the application from crashing due to high request traffic. The HPA can dynamically adjust the number of pods based on CPU utilization, ensuring that the application can handle increased traffic while avoiding overloading the system.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/horizontal-pod-autoscaler.html
add more pods B

question.en:
A SysOps administrator configured VPC flow logs by using the default format. The SysOps administrator specified Amazon CloudWatch Logs as the destination. This solution has worked successfully for several months. However, because of additional troubleshooting requirements, the SysOps administrator needs to include the tcp-flags field on the flow logs.

What should the SysOps administrator do to meet this requirement?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、デフォルト形式を使用してVPCフローログを構成した。SysOps管理者は、送信先としてAmazon CloudWatch Logsを指定した。このソリューションは数か月間問題なく機能している。しかし、追加のトラブルシューティング要件のため、SysOps管理者はフローログにtcp-flagsフィールドを含める必要がある。

この要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者は何をすべきか。

text.en: Create a new flow log. Include the tcp-flags field in the custom log format. Delete the original flow log.
text.ja: 新しいフローログを作成する。カスタムログ形式にtcp-flagsフィールドを含める。元のフローログを削除する。

text.en: In the CloudWatch Logs log group, modify the filter to include the tcp-flags field and the type field.
text.ja: CloudWatch Logsのロググループで、フィルターを変更してtcp-flagsフィールドとtypeフィールドを含める。

text.en: In CloudWatch Metrics, modify the metric configuration to include the tcp-flags field.
text.ja: CloudWatch Metricsで、メトリクス設定を変更してtcp-flagsフィールドを含める。

text.en: Modify the existing flow log. Include the tcp-flags field and the type field in the custom log format. Save the configuration.
text.ja: 既存のフローログを変更する。カスタムログ形式にtcp-flagsフィールドとtypeフィールドを含める。設定を保存する。

comment.en: Answer is A. tcp-flags are not included into default format. If you want to have tcp-flags, you should use custom log format. You can't modify existing flow log. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/flow-logs.html

A: Create a new flow log. Include the tcp-flags field in the custom log format. Delete the original flow log. "After you create a flow log, you cannot change its configuration or the flow log record format. For example, you can't associate a different IAM role with the flow log, or add or remove fields in the flow log record. Instead, you can delete the flow log and create a new one with the required configuration." https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/flow-logs.html#flow-logs-limitations

A. Create a new flow log. Include the tcp-flags field in the custom log format. Delete the original flow log. This way, you can create a new flow log with the desired log format, including the tcp-flags field, and then switch to using the new flow log configuration.
Answer is A.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/flow-logs.html#flow-logs-limitations

question.en:
A SysOps administrator notices that the cache hit ratio for an Amazon CloudFront distribution is less than 10%. The SysOps administrator needs to increase the cache hit ratio for the distribution, improve network performance, and reduce the load on the origin.

Which combination of actions should the SysOps administrator take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、Amazon CloudFrontディストリビューションのキャッシュヒット率が10%未満であることに気づいた。SysOps管理者は、このディストリビューションのキャッシュヒット率を上げ、ネットワークパフォーマンスを改善し、オリジンへの負荷を減らす必要がある。

これらの要件を満たすために、SysOps管理者はどの組み合わせの操作を行うべきか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Enable CloudFront Origin Shield for the required AWS Regions.
text.ja: 必要なAWSリージョンでCloudFront Origin Shieldを有効化する。

text.en: Change the viewer protocol policy to use HTTPS only.
text.ja: ビューアープロトコルポリシーをHTTPSのみに変更する。

text.en: Add a second origin. Create an origin group that includes both origins. Activate CloudFront origin failover.
text.ja: 2つ目のオリジンを追加する。両方のオリジンを含むオリジングループを作成する。CloudFrontオリジンフェイルオーバーを有効化する。

text.en: Turn on automatic compression of objects in the cache behavior settings.
text.ja: キャッシュビヘイビア設定で、オブジェクトの自動圧縮を有効化する。

text.en: Increase the CloudFront TTL values in the cache behavior settings.
text.ja: キャッシュビヘイビア設定で、CloudFrontのTTL値を増やす。

comment.en: A is correct as document from https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/origin-shield.html E is second one.

A: Enable CloudFront Origin Shield for the required AWS Regions. Provides: better cache hit ratio, reduced origin load, and better network performance. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/origin-shield.html E: Increase the CloudFront TTL values in the cache behavior settings. Increasing the Time To Live (TTL) values in the cache behavior settings allows CloudFront to cache objects for a longer period, reducing the number of requests that need to go to the origin server. This can significantly improve the cache hit ratio and reduce the load on the origin server. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/Expiration.html
A. Enable CloudFront Origin Shield for the required AWS Regions. E. Increase the CloudFront TTL values in the cache behavior settings.

question.en:
A custom application must be installed on all Amazon EC2 instances. The application is small, updated frequently, and can be installed automatically.

How can the application be deployed on new EC2 instances?
question.ja:
カスタムアプリケーションをすべてのAmazon EC2インスタンスにインストールしなければならない。このアプリケーションは小規模で、頻繁に更新され、自動的にインストールできる。

このアプリケーションを新しいEC2インスタンスにデプロイするには、どうすればよいか。

text.en: Launch a script that downloads and installs the application using Amazon EC2 user data.
text.ja: Amazon EC2ユーザーデータを使用して、アプリケーションをダウンロードしてインストールするスクリプトを起動する。

text.en: Create a custom API using Amazon API Gateway to call an installation executable from an AWS CloudFormation template.
text.ja: Amazon API Gatewayを使用してカスタムAPIを作成し、AWS CloudFormationテンプレートからインストール用の実行ファイルを呼び出す。

text.en: Use AWS Systems Manager to inject the application into an AMI.
text.ja: AWS Systems Managerを使用して、アプリケーションをAMIに組み込む。

text.en: Configure AWS CodePipeline to deploy code changes and updates.
text.ja: AWS CodePipelineを設定して、コードの変更と更新をデプロイする。

comment.en: New EC2 instances means it should be on a user data script. Hence A

User data allows you to run scripts or execute commands on an EC2 instance during launch. You can include the script that downloads and installs the application in the user data section when launching an EC2 instance. This allows the application to be automatically deployed when the instance is launched

Option A - key word "NEW EC2 instances" . We are interested only this app to be present in newly created EC2, question is not how to maintain app version in long-term... For me "update frequently" is to guide that this app should not be part of the AMI - (thus not option C).
voted D. is it a good practice to install app using instance user data at all? sounds really weird to me.
deployed on NEW EC2 instances. So it really seems to be about the user data. But for every update, we need to create a new instance. So alternative answer would be D. What do you think guys?
As What I search from examtopic.com. A is correct one.
deployed on NEW ec2 instances
Option D is the answer since it addresses the frequent updates issue as well whereas A does not.

This is the most appropriate solution for your use case. Amazon EC2 user data is a feature that allows you to pass a script or a set of commands to the instance at launch time. This script can be used to download and install the custom application automatically when a new EC2 instance is launched. This is a simple and effective way to deploy the application, especially since it is small, updated frequently, and can be installed automatically. Key words: new instance -> user data scripts run only during the first boot cycle when an EC2 instance is launched.
Selected Answer: D Not A => By default, user data scripts run only during the first boot cycle when an EC2 instance is launched. "A" does not solve "update frequently"
Yea I agree to ur point on this cuz it's never a good idea to spin up a new EC2 everytime the app. needs a frequent update... For that, CodeDeploy sounds much robust and long term solution IMO.
but u're missing the point of 'frequently updated' requirement in the question. How would you fulfil that through option A??..

question.en:
A company is running an ecommerce application on AWS. The application maintains many open but idle connections to an Amazon Aurora DB cluster. During times of peak usage, the database produces the following error message: "Too many connections." The database clients are also experiencing errors.

Which solution will resolve these errors?
question.ja:
ある企業はAWS上でeコマースアプリケーションを運用している。このアプリケーションは、Amazon Aurora DBクラスターに対して多数のオープンかつアイドル状態の接続を保持している。ピーク使用時には、データベースに「Too many connections」というエラーメッセージが出力される。データベースクライアント側でもエラーが発生している。

これらのエラーを解消するソリューションはどれか。

text.en: Increase the read capacity units (RCUs) and the write capacity units (WCUs) on the database.
text.ja: データベースの読み取りキャパシティユニット（RCU）と書き込みキャパシティユニット（WCU）を増やす。

text.en: Configure RDS Proxy. Update the application with the RDS Proxy endpoint.
text.ja: RDS Proxyを設定する。アプリケーションをRDS Proxyのエンドポイントを使用するように更新する。

text.en: Turn on enhanced networking for the DB instances.
text.ja: DBインスタンスで拡張ネットワーキングを有効化する。

text.en: Modify the DB cluster to use a burstable instance type.
text.ja: DBクラスターをバースト可能なインスタンスタイプに変更する。

comment.en: B is correct. RDS proxy is solved the issue https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/rds-proxy.howitworks.html

B-->Using RDS Proxy, you can handle unpredictable surges in database traffic that otherwise might cause issues due to oversubscribing connections or creating new connections at a fast rate. RDS Proxy establishes a database connection pool and reuses connections in this pool without the memory and CPU overhead of opening a new database connection each time. To protect the database against oversubscription, you can control the number of database connections that are created. https://aws.amazon.com/rds/proxy/
Too many connections -> RDS Proxy

question.en:
A SysOps administrator is troubleshooting a VPC with public and private subnets that leverage custom network ACLs. Instances in the private subnet are unable to access the internet. There is an internet gateway attached to the public subnet. The private subnet has a route to a NAT gateway that is also attached to the public subnet. The Amazon EC2 instances are associated with the default security group for the VPC.

What is causing the issue in this scenario?
question.ja:
SysOps管理者は、カスタムのネットワークACLを利用しているパブリックサブネットとプライベートサブネットを持つVPCのトラブルシューティングを行っている。プライベートサブネット内のインスタンスがインターネットにアクセスできない。パブリックサブネットにはインターネットゲートウェイがアタッチされている。プライベートサブネットには、同じくパブリックサブネットにアタッチされたNATゲートウェイへのルートがある。Amazon EC2インスタンスは、VPCのデフォルトセキュリティグループに関連付けられている。

このシナリオで問題を引き起こしている原因は何か。

text.en: There is a network ACL on the private subnet set to deny all outbound traffic.
text.ja: プライベートサブネットに、すべてのアウトバウンドトラフィックを拒否するように設定されたネットワークACLがある。

text.en: There is no NAT gateway deployed in the private subnet of the VPC.
text.ja: VPCのプライベートサブネットにNATゲートウェイがデプロイされていない。

text.en: The default security group for the VPC blocks all inbound traffic to the EC2 instances.
text.ja: VPCのデフォルトセキュリティグループが、EC2インスタンスへのすべてのインバウンドトラフィックをブロックしている。

text.en: The default security group for the VPC blocks all outbound traffic from the EC2 instances.
text.ja: VPCのデフォルトセキュリティグループが、EC2インスタンスからのすべてのアウトバウンドトラフィックをブロックしている。

comment.en: It is A, default security groups don't block anything so it can only be in ACL.

There is a network ACL on the private subnet set to deny all outbound traffic. Network ACLs (Access Control Lists) are stateless and operate at the subnet level. If there is a network ACL on the private subnet that is configured to deny all outbound traffic, it would prevent instances in the private subnet from accessing the internet through the NAT gateway.
A is the best one.

No. It's C. In this sinario, there are 2 cases. 1. When accessing from outside: Blocked by default security group 2. When taking from inside: Default security group did not succeed. There is Ado Since 2 does not correspond to anything, it should be considered as 1 only, so C is appropriate. I understood that using the custom network ACL meant that the firewall was allowed. And the default security group originally had no settings. So I had to allow inbound in the security group. So I chose C.
